These jeans are great! I have never tried the more stylish "wide leg" jeans, but I think these are actually excellent for my build and shape. I will say that I am normally a size 2, and I ordered a 4 based on the size chart. They were a tad big on the waist, and the denim has some stretch to it. The tag says not to put them in the dryer - not happening! So I washed them and put them in the dryer, and now they fit even better more like the size I needed to begin with. Since the waist is really high, none of my belts were small enough to fit, but I do like the high waist style. The pockets are HUGE and actually hold my phone in front or in back which is fantastic for women's jeans. They drape nicely and are not too stiff for the material. I like the contrast stitching and the dark color. The photos are before I washed and dried them. Now they might be slightly shorter but still a great length that for me at 129 lbs and 5'7" with curvy frame fit great. I do not have to wear heels and wore these with both my flats and sandals. I got a lot of compliments on them.
reviewer_name_raw: Great dressy jeans, check your sizing.

The Angel's Forever Young 360 Bootcut Jeans are perfect for my everyday lifestyle. I purchased these jeans as I have gained weight and all my other jeans cut into my waist and had less give than these jeans. The waistband and denim are soft and thick with enough stretch for comfort without losing its shape. After all we all need a little support when you are as curvy as I am. These do not change your shape like butt lifting jeans do. I will be doing more squats for that...lol. I would buy these again in a different color. I bought a size 6. My measurements are waist 37.5", booty 44", hips 43.5" and inseam 28.5". I could hem them, but I like that my ankles stay warm when I squat.

5’9”, 68 yrs old, 180 + lbs, purchased size 16 Omg this waist band is to die for for those of us that don’t have much of a waistline. The length is casually perfect. I’m buying a couple pairs for sure! I might be able to go down to a 14, but prob won’t gamble on that. My picture isn’t perfect but you can rely on the website to give you a true vision of these lovely stretchy jeans. They are perfectly good quality. Edited to add: I just received my second pair of these jeans in white, and I must say that I think the zippers are problematic, don’t run smoothly, catch frequently, etc. I will try some sort of method to smooth it out (crayons rubbed against the zipper teeth used to work). Second edit: I just received these jeans in Black, and the zipper pull does not hold at the top. The zipper just automatically unzips because the tab doesn’t catch. Frustrating!!

I'm so glad I sized down. I'm 5'6, 162 lbs. Im heavy in the hips and rear, but slim waist. I typically wear a 9/10 jeans and the waist is always to lose and the hips and but tight, but i expected these to have alot of stretch accoding to the reviews, so i sized down and picked a medium based on the waist measurement, and I did the right thing. These pants are super super stretchy and long, bit not ridiculously long. The material isn't as cheap feeling as some reviews stated, it's actually thick enough for Florida. I inspected it for loose stitching and cheap zippers and buttons, but surprisingly I see no defects visibly. I would suggest a gentle cycle when washed since they do have different materials in the design. I think that if I would have gotten a large there would have been too loose in the waist and possibly baggy in the leg area. And that's not the look I'm going for. The only reason I'm giving it four stars is because I do believe it could have been slightly more affordable so that I would find it worthwhile getting other colors.

Definitely size down! I wear a Medium, kind of moving into a large as I've been gaining weight, but after the reviews I ordered a small and it was perfect. I'm 5ft3 and weigh about 155lb. There is a lot of stretch as the waist band is like sweatpant material, and they are baggy design but in photos cling to the hips a little. I wanted more of that look than just the baggy look so the size-down was perfect! Other than that the only other thing I will say is that the color was a bit darker than pictured, but not a big deal, and they are a little long. I prefer this because I live in heels but if you don't and you're not on the taller side they may drag. I wore about 3 in heel with these and they didn't touch the ground.
Size up if you want a more baggy fit. I am 5'3 200 ordered a XL. Its a snuggle fit but these are really nice and O look GUDT!!!

user_comment: I have the hardest time trying to find jeans that fit me in all the right places. I couldn't believe it when I slipped these on and they fit perfectly immediately. I am 5'8 tall so I have fairly long legs which can sometimes be difficult to find the right length in pants. These are perfect without being too terribly long. They're also quite stretchy but not so stretchy that they end up sagging at the end of the day. They have just a ride amount of stretch to make them look good and feel comfortable. They are excellent excellent quality. These jeans were made to last, which doesn't seem to happen often anymore. They are a nice solid denim without being so thick that they're hot. I also love that they have the perfect amount of bell at the bell bottom. They go over part of my shoe but don't cover the entire thing. I would say it's definitely more of a flair than a boot cut for sure. They also seem to fit pretty true to size. I typically wear a size 4 to 6 and the six fits me well.

I'm a millennial and thought I'd NEVER touch bell bottom jeans again; middle school fashion was cringy enough! But I decided to try these because I need a new pair of jeans. I'm a bit wowed by how good they look and their high quality! They are very comfortable and look really good with high heels or boots. The "Bell" on them isn't as exaggerated as it is in the demo pictures. These seem to be more like a slightly roomier boot cut jeans than true bell bottoms. They are true to size, which is fantastic. I'm usually a size 4-6 and ordered a medium. My only complaint is how high the jeans go (my belly button) and that they still drag a bit on the ground even when I'm wearing kitten heels (I'm 5'5") -- but I know high waisted jeans with long long pants legs is currently the trend. Also the crotch bunches up a bit strangely at times, but I don't think that would be noticable to anyone but myself. In all the fabric quality is really good and these are COMFY. I think they would pair really well with just about any kind of boot!

I'm 5'4". 145-150 lbs. I have a little gut. Ordered a Medium. Perfect. I'd say 90% Comfortable...material is a little rough. Loose fitting jeans...truly looks/feels like boyfriend style jeans. They were a bit too long for my style, so I rolled the cuff up one more time. Issue: the 2nd button on the inside of waistline. The button Is a regular button made for something with thinner material like a shirt, which makes it really hard to push through the button hole....can break a nail. The outside button is a regular button used for jeans, and that one is fine. I showed pictures of both buttons.

Belle Poque Women Straight Leg Jeans Stretchy Mid Rise Jeans with Pockets These jeans are similar to two pairs I bought last year at Loft, but I would argue that they are both more comfortable and a better value as I paid half as much for these. I have gained some Winter weight, so based off that and the size chart I went with a size 8 instead of my usual 6. The size 8 fit well. I am barely 5'3" about 150 lbs currently and half a wider backside, hips and thighs. I actually usually prefer my jeans with slightly less stretch to provide a little shaping, but these aren't so stretchy that they have no form at all. They are okay since they are extremely comfortable. The material is more on the thin side, so I likely won't wear them until it warms up in the Spring. They are ankle jeans for an average height person, but as usual for myself, they are too long for ankle jeans and too short for full-length. I feel like they look best on me with sandals or a shoe that shows a little more skin to lengthen my legs some. I will have to hem them if I want to wear them with sneakers, but I probably won't. I haven't worn them consistently or washed them yet, so I can't say how they will hold up, but I feel like they are decent quality for the price. I love that the waist is high and more narrow than the hips and butt so they don't slide down and I only have to wear a belt if I choose to add accessories. I would recommend for those like myself who usually prefer a curvy fit jean. They also seem like they would be forgiving if I were to gain or lose weight. I am losing at the moment so I'll update the review if I'm wrong. If I didn't have to hem them to get the ankle length I would definitely buy another pair in a different color. Overall I believe these jeans are a decent value for the price. Some of my pictures won't load, so I apologize. Hopefully what is there is enough to give some idea of how they appear on me.

I love that the waist is high and more narrow than the hips and butt so they don't slide down and I only have to wear a belt if I choose to add accessories. Belle Poque Women Straight Leg Jeans Stretchy Mid Rise Jeans with Pockets As a person that can't wear tight things around my waist and being plus size it's hard to find pants that fit comfortably. When I got these and saw them I was unsure they would be a good fit for me, after putting them on I was so happy. they fit me perfectly. I ordered a size up so they do look a little big on me, but not so much that anyone would notice. Because I am only 5'3" they came down as far as normal jeans, but I can also roll them up to make a capri look. The material is thick enough to keep you warm in winter, but thin enough to be comfortable in summer. They have a slight stretch to them which is nice, and the quality is very good from the material to the buttons and zipper. The pockets are deep when you have something in the front they are tight enough so they don't look awkward. I am really pleased with the overall look.

Belle Poque Women Straight Leg Jeans Stretchy Mid Rise Jeans with Pockets These jeans are really comfortable and made from a nice, soft fabric with a bit of stretch. I ordered the dark blue, and it looks a little dressier than a typical casual denim, which I like. For reference, I’m 5'3" and about 120 lbs, with more weight through the middle (mid-life “fluff”). I ordered a size 8 and the fit is good overall. They do feel a bit long in the rise/stride for my petite frame, but that’s pretty typical for me and nothing unexpected. They’re listed as wide-leg/straight-leg jeans, but I’d personally call them more straight leg than wide leg. I included measurements in my photos for reference. The bigger issue for me is the length. They’re described as “ankle jeans,” and I assumed that at my height they would hit a bit longer and work well with flat tennis shoes or slip-ons. Instead, they hit at the ankle on me too. That makes me wonder how they’d fit someone with longer legs—possibly more like capris. Overall, they’re comfortable and flattering, but the length description is something to keep in mind when ordering.

I LOVE Luvamia and own at least 20 different items from them. i was so excited to get these retro daisy jeans in my usual Luvamia size (large on my 5’5” 175# body) but so disappointed that they really don’t fit. This is so surprising because I find Luvamia to be very consistent. I was able to at least get them in for photos and to test the comfort and style. I really do love them and can’t wait til I lose another 10 - 15 pounds so I can wear them. Luvamia generally has stretch in their items so even if they are a bit tight first outing then on, they do get better fitting after about an hour. But these are still too small. Outside of that, they are well made and adorable. Especially for the price, I recommend them, just be sure to size up at least once and maybe twice if you’re between sizes.

I ordered the Luvamia Straight Leg Jeans for Women in Floral Print 3-Medium Wash, size Small. I am 5’8, 140 lbs., for reference. These jeans are so cute!! . I have about 15 items of clothing from Luvamia, and I am very impressed with the quality—they are my favorite denim brand sold by Amazon. The denim has a moderate amount of stretch—enough to allow easy movement, but not enough to make them get baggy as the day goes on. They fit at my natural waist. I do think these run a bit smaller in the waist than other Luvamia jeans/shorts I have. They have a true straight leg fit, so the jeans are not tight anywhere on the leg. They hit about ankle length. I do wish they were a little longer, but that is just a personal preference. The pattern is so cute, and it looks just like the listing pictures. I have washed and dried these jeans without any fading or shrinking. I style them with just a simple white shirt and will add a denim jacket once the weather gets cooler. I know I will get a lot of wear from these jeans. With the current price of $29.99, these jeans are a great deal! I highly recommend these!

Other than taking these two things into account (if you're built like me), I would recommend them.

Ever wish your jeans were as comfortable as your yoga pants??? Well, I have and my wish has come true! These jeans feel just like yoga pants. I ordered baby blue in medium and 31”. I am 5’6”, 154lbs, 41” hips, with smaller waist (about 29”). I usually wear medium in most brands, including Old Navy. These fit just right, right now. I am usually about 140lbs, and I think they might be loose at that weight. The waist fits just enough to keep them up, but I do sometimes have to tug them up. This is a typical problem for me because waists are often cut bigger. Still, this is what I call a good fit as compared to other pants I have tried over a lifetime. The length is good. The hem breaks right at the top of my foot, which I prefer over shorter lengths. See pictures. ———I washed and dried these immediately (I washed warm and dried on medium) so that I could wear them today to the rink. They came out great. If they shrank slightly, I can’t tell. They fit great. I have included some pictures of me in these that show the details. I want to order other colors. I love these yoga jeans! I recommend!

Levi's Women's 94 Baggy Wide Leg Jean (Also Available in Plus) These are close to perfect. The only thing is they are a bit short so you can only wear a kitten heel or flats with them. Stilettos would not look good. But I’m 5’6, so if you’re shorter, you’re all good! The fit is amazing. I got a 25 and they are a little baggy but I love that while they still hug your hips. I have jeans like this that cost $250 and aren’t as good. Levi’s for life. These do not disappoint. I’m typically a size 24 or 25 depending on stretch. These have NO stretch. There are a ton of reviews about sizing down but the I’m glad I didn’t. And I weigh 113 pounds. Maybe I got a color where the fit is perfect but I wouldn’t recommend going down two sizes like people are saying. Again- zero stretch. These are soft and comfy light denim. UPDATE - I ordered another pair in a dark wash and it’s like I’m wearing weird super wide leg uncomfy jeans with zero movement and the sizing is wildly different. The first pair I got was a light wash. Super soft and comfy. I got another pair in a much darker wash. They are twice as thick. Twice as uncomfortable and are tighter. Just be careful as this is true denim- no stretch whatsoever. Can’t wear this darker color. Feels like I’m wearing cardboard. Truly. Maybe they will get “worn” if I wear them more. Not sure yet. Update three- literally doing this for all my ladies. And men that would like this cut. I ordered a third pair in the light wash I first got. I am really small but do have hips. I got a 26 because I wanted a the super soft light wash and supper low hip hugging baggy. And sadly they are the exact same size (I measured) as the 25. Exact. In every dimension. One pair says 25. One says 26. If I put them on there’s zero difference. I actually think the 25 is bigger than the 26. The 26 is not longer. Not looser. And I’m wondering at this point if they are real Levi’s. I have about 10 pairs of Levi’s all different cuts. I love these ones so much but was confused that there was zero difference in the 25 vs 26. I will wear them both. But just be mindful of washes and thickness and length. This review had originally four stars. I can’t give it that anymore. The price is Incredible as they look like wide leg baggies I’ve spent over $200 on. But you truly can’t be taller than 5’6. I feel like I don’t even love where they hit at the ankle and I’m 5’6. One more inch on the cut and I could have worn them with heels. Be prepared for length, feel of denim, and sizing.Look at the photos. Side profile is a 25, front is a 26. Exact same wash. Exact same jean. They both hit the same at my foot. So strange they are so short. Levi's Women's 94 Baggy Wide Leg Jean (Also Available in Plus) These are close to perfect. The only thing is they are a bit short so you can only wear a kitten heel or flats with them. Stilettos would not look good. But I’m 5’6, so if you’re shorter, you’re all good! The fit is amazing. I got a 25 and they are a little baggy but I love that while they still hug your hips. I have jeans like this that cost $250 and aren’t as good. Levi’s for life. These do not disappoint. I’m typically a size 24 or 25 depending on stretch. These have NO stretch. There are a ton of reviews about sizing down but the I’m glad I didn’t. And I weigh 113 pounds. Maybe I got a color where the fit is perfect but I wouldn’t recommend going down two sizes like people are saying. Again- zero stretch. These are soft and comfy light denim. UPDATE - I ordered another pair in a dark wash and it’s like I’m wearing weird super wide leg uncomfy jeans with zero movement and the sizing is wildly different. The first pair I got was a light wash. Super soft and comfy. I got another pair in a much darker wash. They are twice as thick. Twice as uncomfortable and are tighter. Just be careful as this is true denim- no stretch whatsoever. Can’t wear this darker color. Feels like I’m wearing cardboard. Truly. Maybe they will get “worn” if I wear them more. Not sure yet. Update three- literally doing this for all my ladies. And men that would like this cut. I ordered a third pair in the light wash I first got. I am really small but do have hips. I got a 26 because I wanted a the super soft light wash and supper low hip hugging baggy. And sadly they are the exact same size (I measured) as the 25. Exact. In every dimension. One pair says 25. One says 26. If I put them on there’s zero difference. I actually think the 25 is bigger than the 26. The 26 is not longer. Not looser. And I’m wondering at this point if they are real Levi’s. I have about 10 pairs of Levi’s all different cuts. I love these ones so much but was confused that there was zero difference in the 25 vs 26. I will wear them both. But just be mindful of washes and thickness and length. This review had originally four stars. I can’t give it that anymore. The price is Incredible as they look like wide leg baggies I’ve spent over $200 on. But you truly can’t be taller than 5’6. I feel like I don’t even love where they hit at the ankle and I’m 5’6. Levi's Women's 94 Baggy Wide Leg Jean (Also Available in Plus) These jeans make me so happy and excited! They remind me of my old favorite pairs from my high school days in the 90s! Very 'skater-girl/boyfriend jean vibes'. I recently lost over 100 lbs in the last year and these are officially my new favorite pants I'll be wearing to show off my new slim figure. I've been so used to wearing stretchy type pants for so long, I'm looking forward to wearing real jeans again.

I am so incredibly happy with this product, I had to take pictures and do a review. I don't do this for every purchase (I couldn't possibly) but when I have an experience this positive, I have to acknowledge it so I give credit where credit is due and also provide other shoppers with information to help them with a decision. I bought these pants because I was searching everywhere for pants to match this bright purple blazer I had bought and it is just impossible. I saw these, and I knew it was a gamble, but they showed up in the mail the next day, and it was exactly the same color and hue as it was pictured on the internet. It is a perfect match! Not only that, but the fit is absolutely perfect. I think these are quite possibly my most comfortable pants in my wardrobe now. They are stretchy enough to not need a zipper or button (the ones you see are fake) but the pockets are REAL! All four pockets are nice and deep and functional. The fabric is firm enough so that it doesn't wind up looking like leggings (they look like fancy jeans.) They are formal enough to wear with professional attire. They are the perfect length for me (5'2") and they do not fit too tightly around the waist (a problem I find with way too many pants and skirts. They fit just like a glove. In fact, I was shocked at how perfectly they fit. They were almost out of my price range, but I am very very glad that I sprang for a few extra bucks and I may purchase a few more pairs in different colors now that I see how great a product this is. For your reference, I am 5'2", 122 lbs, 31" waist.

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans For reference, I am 5' 2", 155 lb,s and ordered a size 9 (L). I fluctuate between 150-155, and Amazon suggested XL for me. I'm happy with the 9. Though they are a bit snug, they are not uncomfortable in the slightest. An XL might have been too loose. I was looking for a snug/slim fit. In the photos, I am wearing 5" heels with a 1" platform. I like the fit and feel of these pants. They have a good stretch to them and are very comfortable. They didn't have any smell to them. They have a great flared leg to them, it's very dramatic, but not clown-like. The color is a true white. I haven't washed them yet, so I can't comment on shrinkage. You can see right through them. I am wearing black undergarments, and you can tell. I first tried them with nude undergarments, and I didn't notice the transparency. It was only when I went back with my shapewear for the full try-on that I could tell how bad they are. I have nude shapewear, but if you have colorful undergarments, be aware of the white because you can see right through them. The good thing is that the pockets and zipper blend in nicely. I tried two other brands, and you could really see the outline of the zipper and front pockets.

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans BEFORE I BEGIN- I want you to have details on the photos I added, for an accurate view. First photos are without shoes, last ones are with 2" heels. Close ups are for you to see the color change after 1 wash. It may be hard to tell but originally they were that deep beautiful black color. They are still black but much more faded then before. We just moved so don't mind the mess behind me 😛 Now for ACTUAL REVIEW! :-) I do really like these pants! High waisted flare pants can do wonders for your body! There are a few negatives but hopefully I can fix/prevent them. FYI I am 5'7 151lbs 36B 29/30" waist 39/40 hips and 31/32 inseam and I have 16" calves... I am curvy and have thick thighs and thick calves..I normally wear a 6-8 in women's pants/ M in shirts. I purchased a 9, they are 41" in length and have a 34.5" inseam. Here are my PROS AND CONS PROS- I look awesome in them! Perfect amount of stretch without getting saggy( even after a 10 hour shift where I had to bend alot) Love the flare, they sit high so you don't have to worry about butt crack sam. Material is sturdy but not heavy. Can be worn in any weather CONS- Zipper keeps coming down, tried to size up but they were huge on me, could just be my body but it's an easy fix I washed them in cold water on the delicate cycle, air dried them and they already are slightly faded. I do use color safe detergent that is supposed to help fading. I am 5'7 and have a 32" inseam but I cannot wear these without 2 in heels. I hate high waters so I like my pants to be even with my shoes or slightly above them but even with heels/ wedges on they still touch the ground. Perfect for girls 5'8-6 ft. I'm going to use temporary hemming tape when I don't want to wear heels. Sizing is a bit off, I got a 9 but normally I could fit a 7 in juniors. They are a jean material, you don't notice as much in the beginning but after your first wash you will. Now I am still going to buy multiple pairs of these in black and almost every other shade bc it's hard to find flare pants that fit so nicely BUT I will not machine wash these pants again! I will find a detergent for black clothing and hand wash them bc I cannot stand faded black pants, but 2/3rds of black clothing fade, so I don't blame the seller for this is Overall them! Be careful not all shades of these pants are the same style, some have buttons, some have odd light/ bleached lines across the front..but if you pick the ones I got, they'll look just like my picture. I chose the black pair that has the model with a white shirt on and some of her belly showing. They have another pair of black ones, with a different model but I do not like them. Those have bleached lines across the front. Avoid that selection.

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans First off, the obligatory relevant measurements so people can get an idea of the fit (I know I was skimming the reviews for this exact reason). My waist is anywhere from 28 to 31 inches depending on my bloat, hips are 38 inches, and 22 inch thighs. I also have square hips (aka, short waist, high hip flare). I bought a size 9 in "Super Dark Denim". Definitely snug on my high bloat days, but there's a good amount of stretch so I can still easily button these up. I very intentionally posted pics at my most puffy so you can see - these jeans got give! The fabric, besides being fantastically stretchy, is also remarkably soft to the touch. Indeed, if you've got on itch, you can effectively scratch through the jeans (I know you know what I mean about scratching an itch in fitted jeans!) I really can't make any sense of the fabric - it's thick and sturdy enough to hold you in and actually be called "jeans", stretchy enough to make you think they're jeggings, and soft enough to wear all day long. And they make my back end look FABULOUS despite the reality of my flat mom butt. By the way, I'm only 5'0", so be warned, fellow short ladies - these pants are LONG. I had to bring them in to get them professionally hemmed like 5 inches, and that's with extra length left to wear in heels! Absolutely worth it.

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans Oh my goodness.... I bought these on a 1:00 a.m. on a can’t sleep shopping binge and I woke up the next morning filled with regret. I am a size 12 pant and about a 6-8 top so very pear shaped with big hips, butt, thighs, and a little pooch from having a baby almost 2 years ago. When I opened up the package I knew this could go one of two ways - VERY bad or AMAZING. Thank god it was the latter, I will be ordering these in a couple more washes! I bought the black and sized up to a 13 and they’re perfect, I think an 11 would have hugged the pooch too much. If you have a similar body type I would suggest the black because it definitely hides the little tummy very well whereas I do not think the light wash would! I had my daughter at 21 and have been really struggling to find a good balance between young cute clothes and mom appropriate, these are high waisted enough that almost any size could pair with a crop top or body suit and they’re flattering. I am 5”1 and with 3-4 inch wedges these are the perfect pant length, just barely covering up the top of my shoes. Don’t buy these if you’re short and don’t plan on wearing heels or altering because you will be disappointed!

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans In love with these jeans! Definitely going to buy in the lighter color as well. Very 70s with bell bottom style that’s long enough for my 5’8” tall self to even wear heels with them without them being highwater flares. The flare isn’t too ridiculously huge, but just right and they are super flattering as well. VERY high waisted, which I LOVE. Keep in mind they don’t have front pockets, but the stitching makes it look like they do. It does have back pockets. They also aren’t super heavy material so I could wear in the summer with a crop top. I am usually a size 8-10 or 29-30 in women’s pants/jeans and I ordered these in a size 13 in the dark denim. Honestly, I could’ve gotten an 11 for a super duper tight/snatched look but I like breathing and these are tight enough. I’m no longer at the age (32), where I’m willing to sacrifice comfort just for my jeans to be a little tighter. BUY THESE! They are a staple. Can’t wait to get the lighter pair. Great price point too! Well worth it.

Vibrant Women's Juniors Bell Bottom High Waist Fitted Denim Jeans I’m gonna start of first with a warning for fellow short girls, these are LONG. Im about 5’4 and they were dragging on the floor, I put a pair of 4ish inch heels on and as you can see in the first pic you still can’t see my feet. As far as the sizing goes I do think it runs small, I’m about 155-160 Ibs. I went up a size and got the 11 even though a lot of people said it stretched well and it still wasn’t the right size, it’s not digging in to me or uncomfortable but you can see in the second pic I should have went 2 sizes up. The pants are really soft and hug the butt area really nicely. I’m just not sure if I want to keep them. If I can find a way to get the bottom to be just a little bit shorter I’ll probably keep them. I’ve only had them a couple hours so I can’t say overall what the quality is like but right out of the bag they seem to be ok, I definitely would hand wash these though, they seem fragile.

I have wanted to try the barrel jean trend and thought these would be nice to check out. These jeans fit me perfectly. I tried a size medium. I am 5'3" 145 lbs and typically wear an eight or a six. The waist is elastic. The pants were not too tight or too loose around the waist. I did find the inseam to be a little short, but that may be because I am used to wearing my jeans higher on my waist. When I pulled them down a little lower, they fit the length of my legs better. I would say after wearing these, I am not sure that the barrel jeans trend is for me. I felt a little like a circus clown while wearing these and not flattering. However, these are very comfortable. I also found them easy to clean -- I put it in the washer on cold with my other clothes. I then tossed them in the dryer with no shrinking. The jeans did hold their shape through the day and are quite soft. I paired with a sweater. I think they'd also look nice with a tight v-neck shirt or a crop top.

For reference, I am 5'3" and 175 pounds. I ordered an XL. The jeans fit well. They are stretchy. I don't care for the elastic in the back of the waist, but it does help create a nice silhouette. The length is perfect for a flat shoe. I do not like the "patches" of what I will describe as acid wash. It is on the waistband, strips across the bottom of the torso, huge patches from the thigh area to below the knee, and on the backside. Too much. The shape makes me look bowlegged. Overall, they are just okay.

FLYING BANANA Women's High Waisted Butt Lifting Stretch Bootcut Jeans These jeans are really nice and I like them alot. They are well made. Overall though they are really nice jeans.

I have mixed feelings about these jeans. They come very well packaged, even a tissue covering the button. And they are straight leg as written in the description but I find that they do not fit quite like the picture on the site. They are the same width all through the thighs, knees and ankles which to be fair IS the description of straight leg. However, the picture shows a jean that is slimmer on thigh and opens below the knees. That said, I do not have a figure like the model in the picture and although different, the jeans fit me well. They look good both with trendy white sneakers as well as with heeled boots. I am including pictures both with a T-shirt and a little more dressed up with a black top. The size 6 was good for me (5’ 6” 145lbs) and the length was perfect. They are made of thick jean material and feel comfortable, not heavy and weighty.

These pants are a good value. The tag says the material is a cotton blend with some stretch, and that is how the material feels. It is a thicker denim material, a good quality that I expect will wash and wear well. The stitching looks well done. I like that the washing instructions do not indicate any need for special treatment. I can wash these with the rest of my clothes. As for the fit, these fit true to USA sizing. Buy your usual size. These are a good length for an average height woman. I am just shy of 5’4”, and the hem grazes the floor at my heel when I am not wearing shoes. The waist is on the higher end, falling at the natural waist. The fit does provide light tummy support without being uncomfortable. The lower leg is a slightly wide fit, in line with the current style and the ad image. The one negative with these is that the color is a more drab olive than the ad shows. I would have liked the brighter shade, but it is a decent color that I will wear. All in all, I would recommend these.

The waist is on the higher end, falling at the natural waist. The fit does provide light tummy support without being uncomfortable. The lower leg is a slightly wide fit, in line with the current style and the ad image. The one negative with these is that the color is a more drab olive than the ad shows. I would have liked the brighter shade, but it is a decent color that I will wear. All in all, I would recommend these.

Jeans fit great with just a bit of stretch. Nice flair in legs so not tight on calves. Would recommend.FIT: I am 5'5" and 180 lbs and would wear a 12 or 14 depending on the cut. As I recently gained weight I was concerned that the 12 would be too tight, but was pleasantly surprised that they fit. The slight stretch in the waist really helped. For someone who falls in that 10 to 12 range, I would suggest going with the 10. The jeans are slightly long on me, but not long enough that they will touch the ground when I have shoes on.FUNCTIONALITY: Solid jean with just enough stretch to make it comfortable to move in. Stitching was solid with no loose strings. With the olive color it is a nice alternate to your typical blue jeans. Has a wider pant leg (not a boot cut) which allowed me to comfortably wear over ankle boots.FINANCE: In comparing prices, this is priced competitively with other similar products on Amazon.

Overall, this is a good pair of quality jeans. The stitching and rivets are durable. It has a good enough stretch in order to fit most body types and not leave a gap around the waistline. The color is a dark blue denim. Not really a black as in some of the photos. Out of all the photos I have submitted with this review, the close-up of the front zipper photo is the true color of the jeans. I normally wear a size 16 and have a 32 inch inseam, wearing size European 32 jeans, weighing 223 pounds with 46 inch hips. These jeans came in a size 14 and fit as expected. I was able to zip them, even though I am a size larger (16), and the length is made for a taller girl or a short girl like me can wear these with boots, high heels, or platform shoes.

I received this product for review as part of the Amazon Vine program. This review reflects my personal experience with the product. I selected the Amzdora High Waisted Jeans because I wanted a pair of pants in a fun color to wear this Autumn. Orange is a fun color. These pants are soft, before and after washing. They are very stretchy, so stretchy that I wish I had ordered one size smaller. Pull on pants FTW! The fringe on the bottom of each leg is super cute. There are no front pockets, but the back pockets are roomy enough for my phone. On my 5'4" frame, the pant legs come to just above the tops of my shoes. I may roll them up occasionally to fall above my ankles. These pants are all around comfortable.
reviewer_name_raw: Comfy pants in a fun color

The fraying at the bottom is a nice stylish touch. Great pants, I want to order more colors!!

My wife absolutely loves these jeans!!! She got a size 12 in these jeans and is 5ft 2 inches tall and 168lbs. The back pockets are real and the two front pockets are not (the tiny front pocket is real).

user_comment: Hello Amazon family! I have been searching for a pair of white Capri jeans for a long time. I am athletic, 5' 2 at 125 lb and finding a pair of anything is super challenging. These fit in all the correct places! The fabric is super thick and buttery soft. I got a small white and let me tell you you cannot see through them. Included a picture with flash so you can see the quality and thickness of the fabric as well as how well it is. Stitched. Even in white, these are very flattering, especially on a short person like myself. The way that the waistline fits correctly, it does not poop out so I did not even wear a belt. They are form-fitting but not tight so they have great stretchy give. Also, and this is what I think is most important, I wore them from 4:00 in the morning until 8:00 at night on a flight to Las Vegas and they did not stretch out. Out they kept their form beautifully. The fabric is so awesome that by the time I got to the hotel I was expecting them to be a filthy mess and they were not! I have also washed them twice and they wash perfectly. They retained their form and go back to a bright vibrant white! So if you too have been searching endlessly for a pair of white jeans these are for you and they are priced so well. You won't even feel guilty getting a second pair And they are so versatile you could wear them with sandals and a bikini top or tank top, heels and pearls with a fancy blouse, tennis shoes and a t-shirt or a sweatshirt or a sweater! Put your hair in pigtails, flat ironed, classy waves, or a bun. ! Remember, when you feel good on the outside, you make better choices for your health on the inside! Yours in health, Dr. Navarro.👒👑👖👟👠👛👚👙📿🎽👝👜

This pair of Capri jeans are well made of 75% cotton, 23% polyester, and 2% elastane. The fabric is soft and has a good amount of stretch to make them comfortable for all day wearing. The thickness is between medium and thin, so they will be nice for temps in the upper 70's until the humidity arrives. They have nice side slits on the bottom of each leg to step up the style a bit. The button and zipper fasteners work well, without any stiffness or catches. They have 2 regular sized front pockets, the small watch-pocket on the front, and 2 back pockets that are regular sized. I like the Bay Blue color. It's a nice, middle shade of blue with a sort of washed look. They are easy to launder with a cold wash cycle and tumble dry on low heat. I ordered these in a size XL, which is my regular jeans size when going by letter sizes. They fit me nicely without any tightness or pinching around my waist. I think they would have been nicer looking with a slimmer fit in the legs. Due to my shortness they are a bit longer on me than a typical Capri length, but I get that with most all Capri length pants. It's not a fault of the jeans. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips and 182 lbs with an apple shaped midsection. These Capri jeans are good quality and I will recommend them. They are a comfy, relaxed fit. The current price (2-5-2026) of $29.99 is a fair value, but there is also a coupon available at this time, lowering the price to make them an even better value. There are several shades of blue and couple other colors to choose from, as well.

The thickness is between medium and thin, so they will be nice for temps in the upper 70's until the humidity arrives. They have nice side slits on the bottom of each leg to step up the style a bit. The button and zipper fasteners work well, without any stiffness or catches. They have 2 regular sized front pockets, the small watch-pocket on the front, and 2 back pockets that are regular sized. I like the Bay Blue color. It's a nice, middle shade of blue with a sort of washed look. They are easy to launder with a cold wash cycle and tumble dry on low heat. I ordered these in a size XL, which is my regular jeans size when going by letter sizes. They fit me nicely without any tightness or pinching around my waist. I think they would have been nicer looking with a slimmer fit in the legs. Due to my shortness they are a bit longer on me than a typical Capri length, but I get that with most all Capri length pants. It's not a fault of the jeans. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips and 182 lbs with an apple shaped midsection. These Capri jeans are good quality and I will recommend them. They are a comfy, relaxed fit. The current price (2-5-2026) of $29.99 is a fair value, but there is also a coupon available at this time, lowering the price to make them an even better value. There are several shades of blue and couple other colors to choose from, as well.

These capris are well-made from sturdy yet slightly stretchy denim. The blue ("Darkness Blue"), looks darker than in the picture, and apparently without the white stitching that are obvious in the image on the listing. To me they actually look better this way, but just wanted to note that for anyone expecting exactly what the pictures show. They're also a bit longer on me, but I'm only 5'4"-5'5" and wearing flats, unlike the model, so they are really more cropped pants than capris. They would be great for transitional weather in spring and fall, due to the longer length and somewhat heavier fabric (they really feel like true jeans). I normally wear an 18-20 which is really more like 20 in pants, so I got the XXL, and they fit perfectly. (They didn't look like they would, but they stretch, and they are comfortable). I can sit and move in them fine. The dark color is nice for dressing up to go out at night, with flats or cute sandals and a dressy top. I like them a lot and would like to get the other colors as well!

They're very comfortable and they have a moderate amount of stretch to them. I ordered them in XXL which is my typical size. I'm about 5'8 and they pull up halfway up my stomach. The waist isn't super tight or loose. The legs are a little roomier than expected. In that way they're not that flattering on me but they're a great pair of pants to lounge about the house or run to the store in. They just look look like "mom pants" on me. The material is a medium thickness and it's actually pretty smooth. They're easy to move around in and to sit on the floor or on a chair in. They don't have a gap against my back and they don't slide down at all. There's no worries about bending over and having any visible skin privacy issues. The little belt holder straps along the waist have space in the loop areas and you can definitely wear a belt through them without a struggle. I can fit most of my hand in the side pockets and curl them up. They're tight enough to trust putting my keys in to go for a walk without being paranoid about them falling. It's not my stomach that makes the button/zipper area pop crazy as it does in the pictures. I didn't pay attention to how they looked pre wash but post wash the button flap just kind of protrudes and that's the way it is. The zipper works well and zips all the way up. Super casual.

I really liked these jeans, but they just didn't work for me. :( Quality is excellent; they're a good, stretchy jean with a wide leg--which is so on-trend right now. The front pockets are adorable and are actually useable. The length is ok for flats, but would be too short for taller shoes. The downside and the reason for the 4-stars; they run SUPER big. The waist was so large on me that if I were to sit down, you'd see my backside goodies. And, because they were larger on me, they made me look a little fuller than normal. I wold definitely SIZE DOWN in these. I wish there was an X-Small because I would totally wear them. For reference, here are my stats...I’m 5’4” and I’m 123 lbs. I have a short torso, so longer legs for my height. My weight is pretty evenly distributed, so I’m a natural hourglass. I’ve got a slight mom tummy going, but you can’t really tell too much in the jeans. My measurements are 34” (or 34D), 27”, 34”. I ordered the SMALL. NOTE: I paired these jeans with the CIDER Striped Mock Neck Sweater Women Long Sleeve Pullover Knit Top ( which is a winner!

These jeans are a nice lightweight material with just the right amount of stretch. I really like the wide hemmed cuff and the quality stitching—it gives them a polished, on-trend look. They are definitely a true high-waisted style, which isn’t the best fit for me personally since I have a short waist. I find they look best worn with untucked shirts, but not as flattering with tucked-in or cropped tops on my body type. For sizing reference, I’m 5’5” and 165 lbs, typically wear a 12–14, and ordered a large. The large fit but felt a little roomy, especially since they stretched out more after wearing for just a couple of hours. I would recommend ordering a size down if you’re between sizes. Overall, these jeans are stylish, comfortable, and a great value for the quality.

These jeans are very stretchy and a large fits well on my 140 pound body, but since I am 4’11” they are about an inch too long. The sewing is not the best on my pair, one of the back pockets is noticeably crooked. The fabric is on the thinner, extra stretchy side which I don’t love, it doesn’t offer much support and deforms after wearing for several hours. They look cute and the large fits great, but I probably wouldn’t order another pair of these
reviewer_name_raw: Wonky pockets and cheap thin fabric

user_comment: Very happy with these jeans. The denim material is lightweight and has a nice stretch to it, making them very comfy to wear while still looking slightly more dressy. I am 5'4" and the length is perfect on me with flats or a slight heel; they may be a bit shorter on anyone with longer legs. The waist hits higher and the pockets on the front and the back are flattering, along with the wider/thicker hemmed cuffs which adds a slight retro look to the jeans. They do run slightly loose, which I liked in terms of the fit, but if you are between sizes and want them more fitted, I'd suggested going down a size (unless you're tall, then they may be too short as well). Overall very happy with the fit and quality of these jeans for the price.
reviewer_name_raw: Great fit, nice and stretchy denim!

user_comment: The pants fit well and were comfortable. I ordered a size medium. I'm 5'5" and weigh about 140 lbs. The only reason I didn't give them five stars was because in the photos the pants looked like they were solid black. When I opened the package, I didn't love that these have a strong woven look and the pants are definitely not solid black. It makes for a much more casual look than I wanted. I do love the no zipper fit and felt they stayed up well and were flattering, though!

Wrangler Women's High-Waisted Fierce Flare I really like this particular blend of denim and the Constellation color. This color is more of a soft, muted black and not a dark black color. The stretchiness accommodates my curves well. I've got more of a posterior and the biggest problem I have in all jeans is that if they fit my posterior they are gaping in the waist or/vice versa if they fit in the waist well they are super tight on my posterior. I have none of the problems with the stretchy and accommodating Wrangler Constellation denim (see pic of denim composition tag I've attached to this review). I'm 5'8" with weight bouncing between 155-160. I can wear the Wrangler Constellation 12/34 for a casual, work, comfortable fit and also the 10/34 for a tighter, going-out fit. One thing I've found with jeans that are too long (we all have varying lengths of legs individually) is to find someone that hems jeans to your specific, individual length. It's relatively inexpensive and gives you that personalized, tailored fit. I highly recommend the Wrangler Constellation jeans for all my curvy friends out there!

There are 4 pockets and they just hold my phone. These capris are 70% cotton with some polyester and spandex. I washed them in cold water and dried them on low heat, like most of my clothing. They had very little shrinkage. A good value.

Levi's Women's Classic Skinny Jeans, (New) Nice fit & good quality denim. I like that they are a bit heavier denim for cool weather/winter bc I wanted the skinny leg to tuck into boots. The dark denim in the photos is a 4S & the lighter is a 6S. I honestly couldn't tell a difference btw the 2 fit wise. I'm very pear shaped. I'm 5'3", 117 lbs with a 26" waist & 37½" hips. I thought the 6S were a bit loose around the waist so I ordered the 4S thinking the fit would be better but like I said, to me, they seemed the same. So I kept both 😁 Just FYI, with a belt they wear comfortably without " slipping" & they didn't start to sag like some jeans will around my waist, a common problem for pears.

Levi's Women's Plus Size 721 High Rise Skinny Jeans Love these! I’m 5’4” and 130lbs with a bigger waist/hip area than most who are my same height and weight. The size 27 is perfect! There’s not so much stretch that they look/feel cheap, but there’s just enough that they don’t flatten out my backside or make it hard to get into. I’m very pleasantly surprised with how well they work for me! The rise comes just to my belly button so it’s not super high and definitely not low. Very happy with this purchase, they are really flattering on, so I will likely buy another pair in another color.

Well these fit. I usually wear between a size 4 and a 6. I would say these are closer to a size 4. I would prefer they were a bit bigger because they’re very snug. In my pics I didn’t iron these so keep that in mind. These are right out of the package. I would definitely iron these prior to wearing them. The denim is super soft and worn feeling but there’s no stretch to it, especially in the waist. They fit well everywhere except the crotch. There was something kind of weird going on there. I have this problem with jeans sometimes. There’s a lot of extra room in the crotch area and I’m not quite sure why that is. They definitely don’t fit me the same way they fit the model in the photo, that’s for sure. I would definitely consider these kind of a boot cut or maybe closer to a bell bottom. I don’t mind the cut and the length seemed ok. I’m 5ft 4 and they landed right at the top of my feet. I don’t know if it was all of the holes but something felt strange like there was some pulling in the leg. These wouldn’t be my preferred fit of jeans but they’re not bad. I would definitely read these reviews and try to find someone with a similar body shape as yours to see how they’d fit on you.

user_comment: Levi's Women's Ribcage Straight Ankle Jeans These jeans are very comfortable. I got the wash “cool agenda” for the record. But they’re almost too stretchy. Reminds me of the millennial skinny jeans stretch and I like a little bit more stiffness so that they stay put. I say that because as I walk, I feel them riding up which then makes them feel too short. I got inseam 27 and I’m 5’1 128lbs and got a size 28. I think inseam 30 would work better. If you’re wanting jeans to lounge in then these will fit the bill. They make my butt look amazing as my husband would say and they have washed well. I’m just still unsure if I’ll keep.

As an Amazon Vine reviewer, I’ve built my share of couple’s wardrobes—helping my wife Emily hunt for jeans that flatter without squeezing, flow without flopping, and transition from coffee runs to date nights without a second thought. Emily’s 5'7" with an athletic build, and she’s tired of stiff skinnies that dig in after an hour or baggy cargos that swallow her shape. When these ZPIUO Women's Wide Leg Jeans arrived through Vine (high-waisted straight-leg stretchy denim with patch pockets in a medium wash), I handed them over for her real-life test: farmers market strolls, park picnics, and a casual dinner out. After weeks of rotation—from fall layering to solo errands—they're the versatile vogue that's won her over, blending '90s nostalgia with modern ease for under $50. Set Up and Fit - Slips on smooth: Button-fly closure cinches secure at her natural waist (11-inch rise), no gapping or muffin-top drama. Size 10 fit her 38" hips with breathing room, straight legs draping to full-length (30" inseam) without pooling—petite-friendly with a cuff option. - Patch pocket perfection: Flap details on the hips add a trendy cargo touch without bulk; rear pockets swallow her phone, and the stretch blend (2% elastane) flexes for squats or stairs. Washes true—no shrinkage after a cold cycle. - Layering lightweight: At about 12 oz, pairs under boots or with sneakers; folds flat in the drawer, no hanger marks. Performance: - Moves like a dream: Stretchy denim recovered shape through a 3-mile market loop—no sagging knees or thigh rub, even after a big lunch. High waist smoothed her midsection subtly, and the wide legs swished cool in 65°F breezes without overheating. Wore them tucked into a sweater for dinner—flattered without fuss. - All-occasion ace: Held a tote on one hip during shopping sprints; patch pockets gripped keys without jingling. Post-wear, no creases or pet hair cling—shakes off dust like a pro. - Daily drape: Flowed with a blouse for brunch, cinched with a belt for errands—versatile enough for travel without packing wrinkles. Build Quality: - Sturdy stretch denim: Mid-weight twill with reinforced stitching at pockets and hems—resists pilling after two washes (gentle, air dry), and the wash stayed even, no fading streaks. Patch flaps are functional (snap-close), not flimsy; high-rise belt loops grip without stretching. - After a month's mileage (including one muddy park mishap), no rips, loose threads, or color run—feels mid-tier Levi's, not fast-fashion fleeting. Slight distressing adds character without wear-through. - Eco-ish edge: Cotton-heavy blend, machine-wash tough—hangs dry overnight, ready for round three. Pros: - Flattering high waist with built-in smoothing - Generous wide legs that move freely - Practical patch pockets for everyday essentials - Affordable stretch that holds shape wash after wash Cons: - Runs slightly long on shorter frames (easy cuff) - Light wash might show dirt faster (dark options available) My Take: Emily's denim game just leveled up—these ZPIUO wide-legs are the high-waisted high-note: comfy, current, and curve-kissing for the win. They elevated her basics from blah to boss, proving patch-pocket power doesn't need a premium tag. If wide-leg whimsy has your wife jean-jaded, leg into these—Vine stride for stretchy supremacy. ? Yes No (Reviewed in the United States on November 11, 2025) Disclosure: I received this product for free through the Amazon Vine program in exchange for my honest review. All opinions are based on actual wear by my wife Emily during daily activities and casual outings.

Emily’s 5'7" with an athletic build, and she’s tired of stiff skinnies that dig in after an hour or baggy cargos that swallow her shape. When these ZPIUO Women's Wide Leg Jeans arrived through Vine (high-waisted straight-leg stretchy denim with patch pockets in a medium wash), I handed them over for her real-life test: farmers market strolls, park picnics, and a casual dinner out. After weeks of rotation—from fall layering to solo errands—they're the versatile vogue that's won her over, blending '90s nostalgia with modern ease for under $50. Set Up and Fit - Slips on smooth: Button-fly closure cinches secure at her natural waist (11-inch rise), no gapping or muffin-top drama. Size 10 fit her 38" hips with breathing room, straight legs draping to full-length (30" inseam) without pooling—petite-friendly with a cuff option. - Patch pocket perfection: Flap details on the hips add a trendy cargo touch without bulk; rear pockets swallow her phone, and the stretch blend (2% elastane) flexes for squats or stairs. Washes true—no shrinkage after a cold cycle. - Layering lightweight: At about 12 oz, pairs under boots or with sneakers; folds flat in the drawer, no hanger marks. Performance: - Moves like a dream: Stretchy denim recovered shape through a 3-mile market loop—no sagging knees or thigh rub, even after a big lunch. High waist smoothed her midsection subtly, and the wide legs swished cool in 65°F breezes without overheating. Wore them tucked into a sweater for dinner—flattered without fuss. - All-occasion ace: Held a tote on one hip during shopping sprints; patch pockets gripped keys without jingling. Post-wear, no creases or pet hair cling—shakes off dust like a pro. - Daily drape: Flowed with a blouse for brunch, cinched with a belt for errands—versatile enough for travel without packing wrinkles. Build Quality: - Sturdy stretch denim: Mid-weight twill with reinforced stitching at pockets and hems—resists pilling after two washes (gentle, air dry), and the wash stayed even, no fading streaks. Patch flaps are functional (snap-close), not flimsy; high-rise belt loops grip without stretching. - After a month's mileage (including one muddy park mishap), no rips, loose threads, or color run—feels mid-tier Levi's, not fast-fashion fleeting. Slight distressing adds character without wear-through. - Eco-ish edge: Cotton-heavy blend, machine-wash tough—hangs dry overnight, ready for round three. Pros: - Flattering high waist with built-in smoothing - Generous wide legs that move freely - Practical patch pockets for everyday essentials - Affordable stretch that holds shape wash after wash Cons: - Runs slightly long on shorter frames (easy cuff) - Light wash might show dirt faster (dark options available) My Take: Emily's denim game just leveled up—these ZPIUO wide-legs are the high-waisted high-note: comfy, current, and curve-kissing for the win. They elevated her basics from blah to boss, proving patch-pocket power doesn't need a premium tag. If wide-leg whimsy has your wife jean-jaded, leg into these—Vine stride for stretchy supremacy. ? I really like these. The fabric is stretchy, but still has a good amount of structure to it. I like the color of the light wash. It’s not artificially blue, just a nice natural looking wash. The length is good for me, I’m 5‘7“ tall. And I wear a size 10, in order to 10, and they fit Pretty well. They’re a little bit snug in the waist, and the front pockets look a little odd in real life. I’ll probably wear it with longer sweatshirts, anyway, so it’s not a huge problem. I like the cut of the legs, a boot cut, it’s not overly tight in the thigh. I like these a lot.

**Excuse the crazy hair! It’s a little “breezy” in Kansas today!”** A new twist to the barrel Jean trend??? Maybe!!! If you haven’t decided on the barrel jean trend! This pair of jeans may have you deciding….YES!!! This pair of barrel jeans seem to be a slimmer cut than my others. It’s like if straight cut and barrel had a baby! The jeans have a very noticeable darting at the knees in the front and back and an addition seam at the side making this a unique design of barrel jeans! I am 5’4” 145 lbs wearing a size large (10-12). I normally buy mediums (8-10) but noticed the sizing chart for this brand is a little different. The jeans are a nice length and can be wore fall/winter and rolled for cuffs spring/summer. The fabric offers a nice stretch without stretch out and losing the fit of the jeans! 70% Cotton 20% Polyester 10% Spandex I made this purchase in Black Friday, so today is my first time wearing them. They seem to be a good quality jean! Very comfortable to wear. I honestly think they could easily make my top 10 favorites!

These barrel jeans are surprisingly comfortable with a lot of stretch. The quality is very good with reinforced stitching at stress points and pockets. The fabric is quite substantial not flimsy at all. I got the dark blue wash and am quite happy with it. It has some distressed features at the hem and pocket edges with some faded areas that adds a vintage esthetic. The sizing was a bit of a gamble. At 5'4" tall and 157# with 36.5" waist 43" hips, I opted for the size L becasue that is typically my size. My measurements put me off the chart >2XL which is completely out of touch for sizing. In any case, the Large fits pretty good because they are so stretchy. They are not baggy on me except in the legs. For a baggy look, the XL would have been better. The length given in the size chart is the outseam not the inseam. These jeans are little long for ankle length, but that is my lot in life. This is my first pair of barrel jeans, and I quite like them.

Comfortable, soft, stretchy fit. Definitely includes an elastic waist band that I’d be embarrassed to show. But not to wear - as long as you have a top that comes down over the waist, these are flattering jeans. A bit too roomy in the crotch and waist for me (I’m 5’7” and a size 6) but otherwise good on length. Good price, reasonable fit, pleasantly accommodating for a large muffin top. But not a pair of jeans to wear with a short cut too or blouse. Deep pockets - a big plus. But one of my pockets looks perpetually turned over to the lining. That makes me feel frumpy.

- Very soft, smooth, stretchy, lightweight material, doesn't look/feel cheap. I like the mesh material in the pockets - Front and back pockets are long enough for my phone which is nice (although I have an iPhone-8-sized model). Anything in the pockets will stick out quite a bit though and looks a little weird. - I do wish at least one of the pockets was zippered. It would make these a lot more practical for outdoor activities. For loungewear, comfortable wear in public, and light exercise with nothing on your pockets though, these are pretty good. - Will most likely show underwear lines slightly, depending on type. Not too bad though - The rise is great, doesn't chafe my nether regions and gives some leeway for my wider hip circumference without being loose at the waist. - The length comes just slightly short on me but it's mostly okay. I'm 5'7" with a long torso so if you're taller than that these might run a little short. I usually wear a size 4 in pants. I ordered a small in these. - The front pockets do show through the fabric a bit - Nice that it can be dried in dryer

These jeans are true to size and have a wide-leg fit without being overly baggy, which I really appreciate. The leg opening is relaxed but still polished, not huge or overwhelming. The ankle-length cut is perfect—I’m 5’3”, and they hit exactly where I want them to. They’re not too short, but also don’t drag on the ground. More like a longer crop that looks intentional and flattering. The blue wash is really nice, easy to dress up or down, and the overall fit is comfortable while still looking put-together. A great find if you’re petite and struggle with lengths.

I love the fabric for these jeans. There's a little stretch and they feel like real denim. I'm not a fan of the front pockets, but I'll just wear sweaters and things I don't tuck in when wearing these. Sizing is off though. The size 18 for instance says it has a 36.2" waist and it's actually just over 38". I was concerned about the waste so I had ordered the 18 when I usually wear a 16, sometimes a 14. I wish I had ordered the 16. The cut is flattering and I like the high waist. I like that I put them on in the morning and really didn't think about them for the rest of the day, no tugging, punching or pulling up all day.

The material has lots of stretch, making them very confortable and definitely flattering. They're high waisted and the flare at the bottom is just the right amount. I have gotten this brand in other jeans and they didnt have the belt loops, this pair does which makes me so happy as i can cinch in when needed or leave as is when Im feeling bloated. They run just a bit big - maybe its the stretch but for the smallest size I definitely felt like i had a little extra room. If you're tall, they might be a little on the shorter side. But overall, they're stylish, comfy, and easy to dress up or down. Recommend! For reference - i am 5'5'', 135lbs and wearing a size 4.

I ordered these in Black, and sized up one size. They are well made, but the curved style did not work for me, but may for another body type. They were large in the hips, but tapered so narrowly between the hip and knee that they were uncomfortably tight. Then, they flared out to an exaggerated bell bottom. If I tried to settle them down my waist to make the thigh area more comfortable, the rise was too long. I am 5’2, 118 lbs and ordered a size 6. The length was barely long enough for me. The fading of the black denim was also more exaggerated, and striped looking.
reviewer_name_raw: Not great for my body type.

user_comment: These Hybrid & Company stretch pants seem like a solid pick for work or casual wear. The fit was great overall, but the length was too long for my frame (I’m 5’4”), so they bunched up a bit at the ankles. I do like that they have pockets (major win), and the fabric is thick enough to feel professional. The material is a little stiff, not super soft or flowy, but still comfortable enough to move in. I haven’t washed them yet, so I’m not sure if they’ll shrink — fingers crossed! 👖 Fit: Great through waist and hips — too long for petites 🧵 Fabric: Thick and structured, a little stiff 📏 Length: Too long for 5’4” 👜 Pockets: Yes, and functional! 🧺 Wash Note: Haven’t washed yet — not sure if they shrink ✅ Overall: 4 stars — good quality, but would love a shorter length option

I ordered these in a regular medium size. I was wanting a more snug fit, but these were not snug at all. They are nice, slightly thick material, not a thin, flimsy material. They do have some stretch to them as well, so I may order another style or color in small to see if those fit better. These washed and dried well with no noticeable shrinking, pilling or fraying. They didn't wrinkle terribly either, so I only had to hang them after taking them out of the dryer and didn't need to iron. For reference, I am 5'4", 140lbs, 28" inseam, 38" at my hips and 33" at my waist and a medium was a bit baggy, not too long, but did bunch up at the cuff when I wore flats.

For reference, I am 4’11”, 110lb, so definitely on the petite side. I normally wear a 2P in pants. I ordered these pants “wished black” in Small. I hesitate to call these pants “jeans” because the material doesn’t feel denim at all, but they have the look of denim. This fabric is much lighter, relaxed, and airy than a true denim, and much softer as well. It is important to note it has NO stretch. Personally, I loved the fabric…it’s the star of the show on these pants. It’s perfect for spring and fall here in the Midwest. As expected, these were a little big and about 2 inches too long on me (see my pic), so they will need hemmed, but the fit was good everywhere else. They fit my 5’2” 110lb daughter perfectly, but these are so cute and comfy, I'm hemming them and keeping them for myself. The relaxed fit, high waisted, wide-leg style is versatile enough to dress up or down. The “wished black” color is awesome…it basically looks like a well-worn black. The drawstring is functional, or it can be removed if you don’t care for that look. It has four pockets that are actually roomy enough to be useful. If you’re looking for a stylish, soft, breathable alternative to heavy jeans, these are a steal ($30 at time of review).

I really wanted to love these jeans, and honestly—they are so close to being perfect. For reference, I’m 5’11” and planned to wear them as a slightly cropped, relaxed style with sandals, which would’ve been super cute. The overall look and design are absolutely on trend. The fabric is soft, comfortable, and feels well-made—not stiff like some denim can be. Quality-wise, they definitely feel worth the price. However, the fit is where things fell off for me. These do not run true to size. They were tight in the butt and upper thighs, and the waistband made my stomach look more pronounced than it actually is, which wasn’t flattering. If you’re considering these, I would definitely recommend sizing up for a more relaxed, comfortable fit—especially if you want that easy, wide-leg look. Overall: ✔ Super cute style ✔ Soft, quality material ✔ Worth the price ✖ Runs small — size up for best fit I really wish these had worked for me because they’re adorable, but the sizing just wasn’t quite right.

Hybrid & Company Womens Super Stretch Comfy Denim Skinny Jeans 5ft 150, comfy and stretchy, I wish the waist was a little higher so I could cover my mom belly more but if I pull them up then they ride up the front so that’s a no go! Otherwise I like them and have them in two other colors

Hybrid & Company Womens Super Stretch Comfy Denim Skinny Jeans I am 5'7", 260# and I ain't no model. I typically wear size 22 pants but bought the size 24 based on their chart. My waist is 52", hips 54", thigh is 27", calf is 17" and inseam is about 30". The size 24 pants has a 41" waist, 50" hips, 28" thighs, 18" calf and 31" inseam. So with this fabric being stretchy, I should have kept to the size 22 so it would be a better fit at the thighs. The waistband sits above my navel and does not coincide with the model's pic that shows it at almost hip height and under the navel. The crotch to front waistband is 14" and crotch to back waistband is 20". The inseam is a bit long for me but its no problem if I wear shoes or boots. To give you an idea of its stretchiness, I can put the pants on without unzipping it, showing that the 41" waistband can pass my 54" hips. Nice. The front pockets are deep enough to completely hold my smart phone in sideways(4"x7"pocket- fully concealed phone). It also has a small change pocket in the front. Material is similar to the Lee skinny jeans I have - good quality/thickness. Attached are some pics with the pants on to give you a visual of the description..... I am buying a size 22....Follow up 11/14/18: Size 22 fits better and I can still take the pants off without unzippering them....may try size 20....

My FAVORITE pants even after two years! I’m not exaggerating, I sometimes wear these about 5 times a week. I’m 6’1 and have the hardest time finding pants that are long enough, but these are great because they cinch at the bottom, so they don’t look odd sitting above the ankle. After two years, they are slightly faded, but not to an ugly dingy color. I’d still consider them a dark black and not a grey, like some black fabrics can fade to. After washing, they can feel slightly tighter but they loosen again after wearing for a little while. These pants are high quality, super cute, very comfortable, and a great length. The strings that cinch the legs are also adjustable if you don’t want it cinched, both ways look cute.

Levi's Women's Plus-Size 315 Shaping Bootcut Jean Absolutely love these jeans! These are my first Levi’s I’ve purchased in many years but love the fit, style and color. When I first tried on, I thought I’d have to get them hemmed but with a pair of 3” heeled boots, the length is perfect! For reference, I’m 5’3”, 140 lbs and pear shaped. I ordered a size 28 and they fit perfectly. I typically wear either a 27 or 28 (4 or 6). They have a good amount of stretch, and are high waisted without being too high (like the ribcage fit ones) and I like the pocket placement. The pockets sell me on jeans, they can be flattering depending on pocket size, placement, etc. When I lose my winter weight will probably purchase this exact pair in a size smaller.

Levi's Women's Plus-Size 315 Shaping Bootcut Jean I LOVE these jeans!! I usually buy Levi jeans, but the skinny cut or most recently last year, the straight leg. The fit around my waist is always perfect. But I’m now 60 and at 5’5 1/2” and 155 pounds my body has changed :(. So realizing I have a pear shaped body (small up top and round at the waist and hips) skinny jeans look funny on me and Straight leg makes me feel like i’m wearing “Mom” jeans. These denims fit me perfectly even though I sized down, and they are super comfy and cute. They have a lot of stretch to them and feel airy & lightweight, especially for denim. They are a nice compromise between a heavy winter denim and summery linen capris. I am 5'6" tall, long waisted, with legs on the shorter side, and the hem of these pants end just above my ankles. They look good with my sneakers, sandals, flats and espadrilles. They are wide legged but not baggy. They are not what I would call high-waisted because they cinch just below my navel, giving me a slight muffin top. I am sure they will stretch out quickly. Because I sized down, I will be wearing my shirts outside the pants. I ordered the pants in off- white , the muted color looking better on me coming out of winter without any tan whatsoever. Once again, this pair of pants makes a great transitional garment during spring. It is fair priced and has good value.

Wow! The reviews for these are all over the place! I normally wear Judy Blues, size 5(27) 5'4 approx 149 lbs at the moment. I’m 5’7”, 129 lbs, 29” waist, 38” at the widest part of my booty. Size charts always seem to be so misleading. I can never rely on them. I read reviews trying to figure out what size to order. Several reviews said the size chart was pretty accurate, which meant I would have needed to order a large. I’m usually a small, but in considering the reviews I ordered a large and a medium and figured I’d return whichever didn’t fit. I ended up ordering a small after they arrive because the medium was too big. In the photos, I’m wearing a small, and you can see the pants could easily fit someone 2-3 inches wider than I am. They stretch big time. I’ll probably keep the small but not sure because the length is right at where I would want it to be and after washing/drying, it seems like this fabric would shrink quite a bit and then be too short. Either way, I would order a size down or at the very least your regular size when ordering these.

I was really hoping to love these jeans. The reviews were mixed so I knew it would be a crap shoot whether they worked or not. Unfortunately, these are a swing and a miss for me. I’m 5ft 7in, 165lbs, carrying most of my weight in my belly. I wear 8/10 bottoms and M/L tops. I ordered size Large. I liked how much stretch there was but it’s almost too much. I suspect these won’t hold their shape over time and jeans this stretchy tend to accentuate my belly vs slimming it. Cute from the front, not as much from the side. The fabric is a bit rough and scratchy on my skin. The belt is super cute. These just touched the floor with bare feet, but became too short with a sneaker on. Because of that I would only wear these with flip flops and that’s just not versatile enough to add this piece to my wardrobe. Will be returning. Bummer :(

user_comment: I can't decide what I like better, the cuteness or the comfort. Both are amazing. Starting with the comfort: They are really soft, like those old jeans you've worn for years and are your favorite pair. They are already broken in and have just the right amount of stretch to hug the curves without binding or being uncomfortable. The front of the jeans hit right below my belly button, which is perfect for me. They aren't low cut, so aren't going to fall off my butt when I bend over. I ordered my usual size (small) and they fit like a glove. For reference, I am 5'2" and 115 pounds. The worn/ripped parts are all in good spots, so they look good without showing skin where it could be embarrassing. The stitching is neat and spaced well, none are loose or look like there was an issue when they were sewing it. The inside seams are all reinforced at the stress points and even at the hem line where the side seams are, there is a spot near the bottom where it's stitched and reinforced. That is quality and attention to potential problem areas. There are rivets on the pockets, also to reinforce the pockets and insure they don't rip when you are trying to shove something big and bulky in them. The front has the usual metal button and zipper. Again, it's the zipper is sewn in well and even after doing a lot of up and down while pulling on it to see if it would easily break or jump the teeth, it kept working and didn't jam. The button slides in and out of the buttonhole easily, but not so loose that it comes unfastened on its own. When I washed them, I treated like my other jeans, cold water wash and regular cycle for the dryer. As expected, there was a bit of shrinkage but just like other jeans, they stretched out and were comfortable with in half an hour of wearing them. The washed-out color and the rips make these look (and feel) like jean shorts you have worn for years; I really LOVE them and recommend them.
