LEVI STRAUSS SIGNATURE Women's Heritage High Rise Straight Jeans I am 5’3”, 155lbs. This pair is a size 8 (I am always able to wear a size 8 in Levi Signature - as they all run big IMO). The waist measure 15.5 inches flat, the inseam is 27 inches (ankle length, if you are taller than 5’4 these are gonna be short, almost a perfect length for me at 5’3”), 11.5 inch rise, the leg opening is 14 inches. They are a mid-weight cotton blend with plenty of stretch. The rise is long, maybe a little too long, so the crotch hangs a little low if you don’t keep them pulled up. They are a nice slim straight style, that are a pretty classic cut that will likely always be “in-style” They are comfy. I have washed themed and they really didn’t seam to shrink and they have done a decent job of holding their shape throughout the day. Overall a solid pair of not expensive jeans, great for wearing around the house, but definitely don’t do anything to enhance your figure; like you don’t put them on and think “wow, these look great” it is more like “wow, these are a perfectly serviceable pair of jeans”. I mean for $23 you can’t go wrong really.

LEVI STRAUSS SIGNATURE Women's Heritage High Rise Straight Jeans I am 5’3”, 155lbs. This pair is a size 8 (I am always able to wear a size 8 in Levi Signature - as they all run big IMO). The waist measure 15.5 inches flat, the inseam is 27 inches (ankle length, if you are taller than 5’4 these are gonna be short, almost a perfect length for me at 5’3”), 11.5 inch rise, the leg opening is 14 inches. They are a mid-weight cotton blend with plenty of stretch. The rise is long, maybe a little too long, so the crotch hangs a little low if you don’t keep them pulled up. They are a nice slim straight style, that are a pretty classic cut that will likely always be “in-style” They are comfy. I have washed themed and they really didn’t seam to shrink and they have done a decent job of holding their shape throughout the day. Overall a solid pair of not expensive jeans, great for wearing around the house, but definitely don’t do anything to enhance your figure; like you don’t put them on and think “wow, these look great” it is more like “wow, these are a perfectly serviceable pair of jeans”. I mean for $23 you can’t go wrong really.

Gap Women's Mid Rise Baggy Denim These Gap midrise jeans should be labeled as "ultra baggy" in my opinion. Or maybe it's just me not being used to the current baggy jeans fad. I grew up in the 80s & early 90s and used to wear baggy jeans, but they weren't THIS baggy back then. The fabric isn't stretchy, but it is super soft against my skin which I love. I'm just under 5'2" and around 125 pounds, and size 27 short fits well in my waist but is a tad too long so I'll need to wear these with heels (which I typically don't wear). I'm wearing 2.75 inch heeled boots in the pics I posted with this review. In my experience, Gap jeans are always high quality, and these are no exception. I just wish for my own preference these weren't quite so baggy. But if you're looking for a high-quality pair of ultra baggy jeans because that's your normal style, these will likely be a great fit for you. I recommend ordering "short" if you're under 5'4".

Gap Women's Mid Rise Baggy Denim These jeans are everything I hoped they would be. They fit a little snug at the top, and if I ordered again, I would go one size up for extra comfort. Once I wore them in, they loosened up just enough to feel relaxed without losing shape. The dark rinse looks polished and makes my figure look slimmer. I wore them all day, and while they rode up a little at first, that stopped after a few hours. I also love that the length works perfectly on my 5’5” frame and does not drag the ground. The denim feels sturdy, the stitching is solid, and they look effortlessly chic. They are quite stylish and I'm very pleased with them.

I am 5'5" and 145lbs with slender curves. Disappointed that these have a weird waist band and unevenly cut. The waist on these jeans leave a gap in the back between my butt and the jeans. It's very unflattering and doesn't do any "tummy control" . These fit great around my butt and thigh but not my lower back. I have plenty of belly to hold most jeans so I was surprised that these couldn't handle the small booty I have. I know washing and drying these on hot will not shrink the waist enough. These jeans require a belt or you'd have your panties or butt crack hanging out. I would have gone for a 4 but I would have been pouring out of the front area.

Hue Womens Ultra Soft High Waist Denim Leggings Update: purchased size small and they fit great. They wash and wear well. I own multiple pairs and wear them all the time. They hold up nicely. They are very easy to move in and I enjoy wearing them to the park to play with my toddler. They are also nicely made and good quality. I think ladies with a curvy bottom half (like myself) will enjoy these! The accommodate your butt and thighs in a flattering way. The waist doesn’t dig into me or cause muffin top. They don’t bag out or sag during the day in size small. Original review: I’m 5’4” 130-135lbs (pear shaped) and normally wear a 4-6. The size medium was too big. They felt like they where going to fall down during the day and the crotch was baggy. Overall just not a snug fit. They look ok in pics, but def are not snug enough. Like the color and style. They came up to cover my belly button. Def a true high rise! Will need a size small! They have a generous amount of stretch. If between sizes I would size down. I’m sometimes a small and sometimes a medium in leggings. More often than not a medium in leggings. They are what I would call a jegging. They aren’t as thick as a true Jean material, but not so thin they show all your lumps and bumps. I like that they have actual pockets on the back. You can wear them on their own with any shirt or also as a traditional legging with a tunic or longer top. They don’t look like leggings so I believe the black or dark wash (or whatever conservative colors) would be appropriate to wear with a nice blouse and dresser shoes for work or a nice restaurant. In my opinion at least, for most occasions.

user_comment: Hue Womens Ultra Soft High Waist Denim Leggings the fit, feel and everything else about these leggings I love. They aren't so tight you have to pull them on like pantyhose, more of a loose legging. They look more like jean than leggings, but they are as comfortable as leggings. - I ordered these because I have a large waist-to-hip difference/ratio and these looked like they could accommodate that. - The waist is unfortunately larger than expected, but that would probably be my biggest complaint with these. - They could be a tad bit more high-waisted for my taste, but they are advertised as mid-rise and I would say they are almost more high-rise. Just about perfect though. - They do also arrive with a slight chemical smell, but not too bad, and it will most likely come out in the wash. - Nice that there are no weird big logo names/patches on the jeans - The material feels like sturdy denim to the touch, but is somehow also very stretchy!? I can squat with no problem as if I were wearing leggings! Since it's not as rigid as regular denim it is much more comfortable overall and reduces crotch chafing. The stretch also helps it to not pull/ride down as you move, making the larger waist not as much of a problem. The waist feels large but doesn't fall down on its own. These jeans also accommodate my larger hip area without making things look too baggy. The straight leg (which verges on bootcut since the leg is pretty wide overall) will be flattering on anyone like me who needs to balance out broad shoulders with more volume on the bottom half of the body. - They do run wide overall, so it does make the knees look a little "fat" - The length is perfect for me and hits right on top of my feet and 1/2-1 inch from the floor in the back. I am 5'7", 135lbs, 25-27" waist, 35-36" hips, longer torso than legs. I usually wear small or size 4, and Small in these was true to size and good for me. - The front and back pockets are all REAL and big enough to fit my whole hand (still not as big as I'd like but that's good enough). - Overall they're not miracle jeans but I'm pleasantly surprised and happy with them. Decent quality for a pretty decent price. - I'll update my review if anything changes!

The straight leg (which verges on bootcut since the leg is pretty wide overall) will be flattering on anyone like me who needs to balance out broad shoulders with more volume on the bottom half of the body. - They do run wide overall, so it does make the knees look a little "fat" - The length is perfect for me and hits right on top of my feet and 1/2-1 inch from the floor in the back. I am 5'7", 135lbs, 25-27" waist, 35-36" hips, longer torso than legs. I usually wear small or size 4, and Small in these was true to size and good for me. - The front and back pockets are all REAL and big enough to fit my whole hand (still not as big as I'd like but that's good enough). - Overall they're not miracle jeans but I'm pleasantly surprised and happy with them. Decent quality for a pretty decent price. - I'll update my review if anything changes!

I was surprised how soft and stretchy these jeans feel when I first tried them. The fit is relaxed but still look neat, not too baggy which I like. The length was good for me (I’m around 5'5), just touch top of my shoes. The denim fabric feels nice quality and a bit stretchy so easy to move and sit. The color looks same as picture, maybe just little lighter under sunlight but still pretty. They look stylish with tucked shirt or simple tee, I wore once with sandals and once with sneakers and both look good. Very comfortable for long day wear, no tight waist. I hand washed first time and it didn’t bleed or shrink, so quality seem fine. Overall simple but nice pair of jeans for daily wear, I might get another color soon.

Dickies Womens Women’s Duratech Renegade Pant These fit perfectly and feel absolutely amazing! I'm just under 5' 3"/weight 110 and the size 4 was perfect. I can wear them with my boots, Romeos or Danner shoes and they don't touch the ground. I tried size 2 originally, but I new once they shrunk, they'd be a tad too small. I've been looking for a great pair of pants for at deer/elk camp and these are it. I was setting up camp, gathering and splitting wood all weekend and they felt great. I really like that they aren't too high waisted, as I personally don't like pants sitting at or above my belly button (but I also want to be decent when I bend over). They aren't stretchy, but they aren't stiff like other canvas work pants and don't bunch up when working. They aren't baggy or skinny like skinny jeans, they're right in the middle and also have lots of practical pockets. Finding quality, well fitting womens work pants hasn't been easy. Can't say enough good things, I will be buying several more. One year later, I've ordered 3 pairs in different colors. The tan color is a really nice color, not a super vibrant/bright, it's a bit subtle and perfect. The green and gray colors are just as beautiful. I just love these pants and I get so many compliments every time I wear them. Highly recommend them!

Very pleasantly surprised! I normally do not like clothing with elastic waist bands. Aside from regular leggings. But I took a gamble with these because they looked so nice and crisp. I love the fit. They are just a tiny bit flared at the ankle and they are just cropped enough - not too short. Short pants not long shorts. I like that the pockets are classic jeans style pockets with the two in the front and the two square pockets in the back. No zipper or buttons. The jeans are decently stretchy. The one thing I dont like about many white garments is that you can see the pockets through the material since its such a light color. And I can see the outline of the front pockets through the jeans and I really dont like that but its only noticeable in certain light. I ordered a medium and I love the fit. I'm 5'9 and about 170 pounds. And I'm 36, 32, 42. These are very classic looking jeans that can be worn with a t-shirt and flip flops to the beach or with dress shoes and a blouse for a summer outing. Very versatile because even though they are jeans they have a very nice shape and fit.

These jeans are very comfortable and flattering. The stretch in the legs makes them easy to move in throughout the day. However, the listing describes them as cropped, but on me they were full length. I’m 5’1”, so that may be why they didn’t hit at a cropped length. Petite shoppers may want to keep that in mind. The pockets are functional, which is always a plus. One thing to consider is the high-waisted design. Because of how the waistband fits, it may not be the most forgiving option around the midsection. Those who prefer a more relaxed waist area might want to consider that when choosing their size or style.

So, a few issues with these jeans. First is the strong odor. It has a very strong dye scent, even after washes, that is not necessarily unpleasant but it is noticeable. The odor is recognizable to me as a typical blue jeans dye smell which is a little sour and earthy. I feel that the jeans run a little small. I ordered the Large. I am 5'4, 145 lbs with a thick hourglass shape. My bum and hips and lower belly are pudgy while my upper waist is smaller. The jeans are very tight on me and my lower belly pooches out like a balloon when wearing these jeans since the wide waistband is tight. I need to wear long loose tops with it or else it's very unflattering. The tightness of the jeans do get a little more comfortable with wear, but I wish I had gotten the XL instead. The tightness makes them annoying to pull down and back up such as when you need to relieve yourself. I do like that the jeans have pockets. The "cropped" length is not very cropped on my stubby legs, but I like that I am not stepping on the hem. They have a good deal of stretch while also having a high percentage of cotton, which is important to me. They have washed and tumble dried no problem. Aside from the stinky smell and the way they highlight my most bloated body area, I feel these are OK jeans for around the house and for times when looking your most presentable is not a priority. They do not look like high end jeans by any means. Just a basic choice for doing chores, cooking, gardening, etc.

I think I must have received the wrong item. I ordered cropped jeans, but received full length. Aside from the length, they appear to be exactly the same. I followed the size chart because I sometimes wear a small and sometimes a medium. Size chart told me I'd fit in a small and it was correct (I'm 5'3" and weigh 150). Jeans are more like a jegging, but I think that's the case with anything that is "pull on." Having said that, these are very comfortable and look great. I ordered classic blue color and they are just like any jeans. They are a little bit lighter in weight than button/zip jeans. They look great with a button down shirt, sweater, or a simple tee shirt. They wash well with no fading. These will work nicely in my denim rotation.

I absolutely love these jeans. I naturally have wider hips but slimmer calves, and this cut works perfectly for my body type. The slim straight leg balances everything out nicely without feeling tight. I’m 170 cm and about 160 lbs, and I ordered an X-Large. It’s slightly loose on me, but in a very comfortable way — not baggy, just relaxed and easy to wear. The fabric is soft with a bit of stretchiness, so it moves with you and doesn’t feel stiff like traditional denim. The 26.5" inseam hits right around ankle length on me, almost like a perfect cropped 9/10 style, which makes it great for summer with flats or sandals. The high waistband is smooth and flattering, and I love the pull-on design — no digging or bulky closures. The color is rich and true, giving it a polished look. Overall, stylish, comfortable, and very wearable.

I used to wear jeans all the time in my younger days, but I slowly stopped wearing them because -- let's face it -- yoga pants and sweatpants are much more comfortable when lounging around the house, but I have to say that these straight leg stretchy jeans are in fact quite comfortable and very flattering on. I am 5'4", 127 pounds, and usually wear a size small or medium, but I chose the medium size, and they fit perfectly fine. The inside tag instructs to machine wash cold, wash inside out with like colors on gentle cycle, and do not tumble dry. It also says that these can be dry cleaned. The material makeup reads 75% Cotton, 23% Polyester, and 2% Elastane. Overall, I think these are comfortable jeans that would be great to wear to potlucks, picnics, and get-togethers -- basically, anywhere where yoga pants and sweatpants are too casual to consider wearing.

These white pants have a clean, polished look and decent fabric quality, but the fit is very body-dependent, especially through the waist and lower abdomen. The 75% cotton / 23% polyester / 2% elastane fabric is thick enough not to be see-through (a must for white pants) and has a bit of stretch, but the waistband itself is quite firm and unforgiving. The leg cut is streamlined and flattering, but on my 5’5” height they were not cropped, hitting right at the ankle bone. The waistband sits directly on the lower abdomen and tends to compress rather than smooth, which creates visible tension lines for curvier or softer midsections. I ordered my usual size Large; the legs fit well, but I would personally want more give through the waist for comfort. These work best with longer or structured tops that don’t require tucking in. If you prefer a polished, tucked-in look, the waistband may be a dealbreaker. Overall, they’re decent white pants with good opacity, but the waistband fit will make or break them. If you have a flatter lower abdomen, they’ll likely work well; otherwise, sizing up or choosing a different cut may be the better option.

I first ordered these jeans in my normal size (4-6), but although I am 5’8” tall, I am short-waisted, and I did not like the high-waisted fit on me. I returned the original pair and re-ordered a new pair 2-3 sizes larger than my normal size. I also ordered it in the color “Blue” instead of “light blue” because they were lighter than the photo. Now I am super-happy with these jeans and love where they hit my waist! The fabric is soft and comfy, and I’m so glad I gave these a second chance! I hope this is to you in achieving the fit you’re looking for. :)

Love these pants! Being only 5' 2.5", they are a tad long , but that is typical of every pant I put on. I wish they would offer them in differing inseam lengths. But otherwise, they fit well and have just the right amount of stretch. They are slimming, without creating muffin top pinch in the waistband. The reason for a missing star is that they do create a bit of "camel". I tend to wear a longer top that covers that area anyway. But maybe the front seam could be done a bit differently

NOT SHORT GIRL FRIENDLY. I was to start this review with saying do not order if you’re a short girl. I, thankfully, know how to sew and was able to hem the pants myself. For the average shirt girl, I would strongly avoid, unless you can sew or want to pay a tailor to alter them. I am 5’2 and had to cut off five inches from the bottom. You can see in my photo that I have it pinned in one, I was measuring for the hem. The length look ridiculous, honestly it looked like I was wearing scuba fins with how much fabric there was at the bottom. To GTP: either shorten the length to adjust to the average woman’s height (5’3-5’5) or provide a petite option because it truly was ridiculous. The pros are that they are stretchy and indeed comfortable. The quality is as expected from TGP, as always it’s not great but it’s not bad. They look cute, but they don’t look like real jeans either.

These are a great alternative to wearing jeans! The leggings are soft to the touch and made of a very durable stretchy material. The leggings do have two very deep front pockets, as well as, two deep back pockets— which can be very useful and is a positive! Upon wearing them, they look as though one is wearing actual jeans. They run fitted from the waist down to the thighs, then loose towards the bottom. These are full length leggings, and not short at all. For reference, I am 5’5 and the size Large provided me with great length.

So, for the past couple of years I haven't worn anything other than sweatpants. I have Crohn's disease, and often have bloating and overall abdominal pain, in addition to frequent weight loss and weight gain. When I saw these had an elastic section of waistband, I thought I'd give them a try. I'm so impressed with these! They're soft and comfortable. Not like traditional denim, which I'm so used to not wearing these days that I can't stand the feel of it anymore. I'm 5'5" with a shorter body and longer legs, and these hit just around or slightly below my ankle. This works for me, because the length in the listing images was the one thing that made me think twice before ordering, and the current length will look great with my croc platforms. I'm assuming they're longer, partially because I got the size 17. It seems that some companies add length as the size goes up, as if larger waist size means you are taller as well. Like I said, the length works for me, but it's something to keep in mind if you truly want the length shown in the listing. I don't have a full length mirror in my house, but when I do wear these out and about, I'll have someone get a picture so I can update this review with it. I love the look of them and they aren't oversized in the booty, hip and thighs, like most larger pants are. It's difficult to find things that fit my body type since when I gain weight, it's all around my middle, never, unfortunately, in my legs and behind. In fact, the straightness of these helps me to look a little better proportioned, which I'm thankful for. Overall, I'm extremely pleased with the value for cost, the fit, the fabric, and everything else about these. I would definitely recommend, and would consider ordering again. Think I'll try the darker ones next time.

I have Crohn's disease, and often have bloating and overall abdominal pain, in addition to frequent weight loss and weight gain. When I saw these had an elastic section of waistband, I thought I'd give them a try. I'm so impressed with these! They're soft and comfortable. Not like traditional denim, which I'm so used to not wearing these days that I can't stand the feel of it anymore. I'm 5'5" with a shorter body and longer legs, and these hit just around or slightly below my ankle. This works for me, because the length in the listing images was the one thing that made me think twice before ordering, and the current length will look great with my croc platforms. I'm assuming they're longer, partially because I got the size 17. It seems that some companies add length as the size goes up, as if larger waist size means you are taller as well. Like I said, the length works for me, but it's something to keep in mind if you truly want the length shown in the listing. I don't have a full length mirror in my house, but when I do wear these out and about, I'll have someone get a picture so I can update this review with it. I love the look of them and they aren't oversized in the booty, hip and thighs, like most larger pants are. It's difficult to find things that fit my body type since when I gain weight, it's all around my middle, never, unfortunately, in my legs and behind. In fact, the straightness of these helps me to look a little better proportioned, which I'm thankful for. Overall, I'm extremely pleased with the value for cost, the fit, the fabric, and everything else about these. I would definitely recommend, and would consider ordering again. Think I'll try the darker ones next time.

I really wanted to love these jeans, I love the cut and the length is perfect for my 5’2” height. I decided to return them as they’re a bit tight on the crutch area, would probably bother me during long sitting. Thin material, not sure if they would shrink. My waist line is 23 and I got size 1.

I love my new pants. They are soft and have enough stretch when I move in them that they don't leave me feeling constricted but still snug enough to give me shape in all the right areas. The pocket size is perfect and I think the overall quality is good but I did see a small stitch in the corner where it's showing a small hole through the pants. These pants are fairly light feeling which is what I like, but if you're looking for something to wear while walking through a blizzard....yup these are not for you 😂. My only concern for others (unfortunately I'm just used to it) is that in the stock pictures it has the pants slightly above the wearer's ankles. So I'm about 5'4/5'5 and these fit like normal floor length pants. So for those that are vertically challenged like myself and were hoping for higher up the leg pants then you will be fairly disappointed. But I am 100% thrilled with these pants and I'm glad I bought them.

I really like these dark blue denim capris. They are a mid rise with a relaxed straight leg fit, though the leg opening is definitely wider almost more of a flared style capri. The denim color is a very nice, rich dark blue, and the material has a good amount of stretch, making them very comfortable to wear and move around in. I ordered a size XL and it fit me very well not too tight and not too loose. For reference, I usually wear a size 16, and I felt these were true to size. They button and zip easily, and the overall quality of the material feels solid and well made. I’m 5'2" and short and curvy, and these fit my legs perfectly. The length is just right for capris and very flattering on shorter girls. The only downside is the chemical smell. Even after washing, there was still a slight chemical odor, which I didn’t appreciate. That said, aside from the smell, these capris are comfortable, stylish, and fit great. Overall, a great pair of capris with good stretch and a flattering fit just be aware of the lingering smell.

I love these chinos. They’re cut really well and very cute. I got them at a great price. I usually am a size 6 in stretchy jeans and 10 with no stretch/100% cotton, but in the Amazon essentials chino shorts I’m a 10 so I ordered these in 10 but these are definitely cut roomier than the shorts. Still, they don’t fall down at all throughout the day and my only other white pants (jeans) are butt tight. These as with the shorts have a little stretch. I showed close up of how long they fall on me: 5’9, long legs. I wore black underwear with them and although you could tell a little my husband said you couldn’t really unless you were staring. Don’t do what I did and cut the tag out carelessly! I cut a hole right in the fabric (not on seam!) and had to sew it. It is tiny and no one would notice but I mean I do. The one criticism I could maybe have for Amazon brand chinos (these and shorts) are that the button seems to be sewn in flimsily. I’d get these if went on sale in another color, but would probably get an 8. I’ll dry these in dryer since they’re big and come back to update if the fit changes drastically. They’re very comfy and I even took a long, hot walk in them. They don’t get too wrinkled (I didn’t steam or iron these I just wore them out of the package; I know they’re not perfect but good enough for the day I wore them!). Edit: I tumble dried these on low heat and they shrunk a TINY bit, just enough that the size 10 is now perfect. Not too short or too tight after the dryer. As long as I hung them up immediately, no ironing needed.

I like these cargo pants a lot. They are really comfortable and roomy. Just what I was looking for. As for picking a size, I would suggest deciding by your waist measurement. My weight is 136 and I am 5’4 and a half inches tall. My waist is 28.5” (in the morning LOL! )and my hips are are 37.5” I ordered a large because the waist size looked like it would be more comfortable. The medium waist only went up to 28” inches. Even with elastic in the back I was worried it would be cutting in to me. I really hate anything that cuts into my stomach. I also despise rolls and muffin tops. This waist size lays nice and flat and gentle around my middle not creating any layer spill over or fat. 🤣. I was looking for roomy and these definitely fill the bill. I like the bottom both cinched and not cinched. The fabric is a soft slightly brushed texture yet has a slight stiffness so they don’t just hang and flap in the wind. Over all a nice pair of cargos. I gave them 4 instead of 5 stars because they have elastic in then back of the waist and I am not a fan of that but everything else is what I was looking for.
reviewer_name_raw: Choose size by your waist size

Hybrid & Company Women Ultimate Comfort Flattering Fit Stretch Skinny Jeans I bought a size 7 (which I normally wear), and I was somewhat disappointed with the fit. The pants aren't stretchy at all. The fabric is scratchy, tough, and unyielding. Hopefully some wear will soften it out. Additionally, I'm 5'8" and the jeans seemed to be designed for shorter women. They reached my ankles, but the waist of the jeans barely reached my hips. It took a lot of wiggling to pull it up to a comfortable level, and bending caused the back of the jeans to slip dangerously low. You can also see (in pics included) that the back pockets don't fully reach my butt. These jeans are definitely usable, maybe for short outings to the grocery store. I'm normally to lazy to go shopping at actual stores so I suppose these are fine for the convenience and price. I was happy that the color matched the image shown (I picked indigo).

Let's start this by saying that I have a small-ish butt and am 5'6" at 130 pounds so my tummy is noticeable to me. I got these thinking "Butt lift? YES! Tummy control? YES! Flare Leg? YESSITY YES!" Unfortunately, it didn't lift my butt or provide any semblance of tummy control. I've worn butt lifting jeans that do exactly that job and they're fantastic. These are not those jeans. They are very stretchy and comfortable though. Seriously stretchy... as in, I don't even need to undo the button to slide them on. I'm a women's size 6 so I got Medium size in these jeans -- in my experience, Small is typically too small. HOWEVER, I do love the cut of these jeans. They look so good with heels (see photos) because they're a little longer. I'm hesitant but I'm actually going to put these in the dryer after washing them to see if maybe they'll shrink to that magical zone between Small and Medium and maybe, just maybe, they'll lift and control!

But overall they fit good and fit the price.

They fit true to size (I’m only 5’3, so they’re not as cropped as in the model photos) and are extremely comfortable. They have an elastic waistband with a good amount of stretch. They are soft and don’t feel like jeans… I’m comfortable in them all day. I am still clinging to my skinny jeans, but these are an acceptable “in fashion” alternative for me. They seem to be of fair quality and have held up in the wash on a normal wash and dry cycle (no shrinking or anything).

The barrel jeans in rustic brown color are cute. I like the brown color which looks more like copper with some shades of brown in the background. The jean fabric is not very thick and has some stretch. The jeans shape elongates my hips and legs without looking boxy. I am 5'2" tall, 130lbs, 29" waist, 41" hips and size 12 fits well from waist, hips and length. The front and back are comfortable, but I get always some creases at the hips on the back. The length of the jeans is great for my height, and I don't need to adjust the hem. I wash the jeans using a cold, normal cycle and I dry them using low heat. The jeans didn't have any smell when I received them. I prefer wearing the jeans untucked.

Gave these barrel jeans, a 4 star because the upper-back seam has a slight puckering Pooch. When I added a belt, it didn’t seem to be as noticeable. I really like them in the black color & it makes them very versatile with multiple shirt options. Fit true to size. I got size 4 & they fit perfect. Length is just right on me I’m 5’ 2 & 113 lbs it hit just above my ankles, with a cute cinched accent at the hem & knee patch’s give it a trendy/sporty touch. I got compliments on the style when I wore them out to an event. I styled them w long sleeve shirt, belt and trendy track shoes. The back elastic waistband allows for stretch, allowing you to wear them higher or pull them down lower on your waist.

Though these were called barrel jeans, I'd say it's more of a straight legged look when actually worn. They're comfy. The denim is soft and flexible, medium weight. The fit is true to size, but the elastic back waistband gives you a little "wiggle room." The pictures shoe these as a cropped or what we called "highwaters" when I wore the style back in the late 80s/early 90s. But I am only 5'4" and have short legs. They land right at my ankle bones. As for quality, they seem to be well-sewn and sturdy. I'll amend if I encounter any condition issues after multiple washings. They have decent-sized pockets at the front hips, but none on the back.

These pants are adorable and the quality is surprisingly good for the price. The material isn't too thin, so they don't look cheap. My advice on sizing: I’m giving 4 stars only because of the length. I am 5 foot and I definitely have to wear these with platform sneakers or heels, otherwise, they drag on the ground. If you are on the shorter side, be prepared to hem them or wear heels. If you are tall, these will be perfect for you! Also size up on these as they run small. I bought a 2xl and they wear like an xl.

These jeans looked so comfy, I ordered immediately. Upon receipt, the material is soft and stretchy and loved the elastic band to fit different sized waistlines. I’m 5’2” and as usual ordered size small but unfortunately, they were way too long! I do normally wear heels with jeans but these are so long that the highest heels I had still didn’t work. I will definitely get them hemmed to make it fit. To the Vendor’s defense, they do provide accurate inseam and length measurements in the size guide - something I completely missed and did not take into consideration! There’s even a picture of a model who’s 5’8” and wearing a small so that should have been a clear warning how long it would be. If it did fit, I have no doubt I would have loved it and probably wore it all the time. So since it was completely my fault for overlooking the size guide, I really can’t fault the vendor. Putting aside the size issue, these are made with a unique blend of cotton, polyester, and a generous amount of spandex and they offer a four-way stretch that truly moves with you. The fabric has a cloud-like softness that sets them apart from stiff, traditional denim, making them a perfect choice for anyone who prioritizes comfort but doesn't want to sacrifice style. The design is specifically engineered to be figure-flattering for all body types. By combining a high-waisted silhouette with a pull-on elastic waistband, these jeans provide a smooth fit and subtle tummy control without the hassle of zippers or buttons digging into your skin. The wide-leg cut does more than just offer breathability; it helps elongate the legs and creates a balanced, stylish frame that looks just as good with a tucked-in t-shirt as it does with a professional blazer. Just make sure to take measurements and if they fit you, I’m pretty confident you will like these jeans!

TO say I love these jean is an understatement! They are so comfortable!!! Only downfall is that at 5'2", they were a little long on me. I weigh 122-125 lbs and ordered a medium, I'm going to have to hem the jeans to shorten them a bit. Otherwise, they are very comfortable. They have some stretch to them, so they are great for wearing when your going out to a big meal. LOL For me, I tend to fluctuate on the border of 2 sizes, so I love a pair of jeans like this that has that stretch so I don't have to worry about a little fluctuation in my weight from one month to the next.

The jeans are so easy to slip on and go! They are made from a lightweight denim material and feature a wide-banded elastic waist. I received compliments on them the first time I wore them out to an event. I appreciate how the flat elastic waist provides a smooth middle section -- no lumps and bumps from a button, zipper, and belt loops. These jeans are ideal for a fitted top, and perhaps, a shoe with a bit of heel for elongating the leg. That said, I'm 5'5 and could wear these jeans with flats, and it would be fine. I will note they had a fairly strong chemical smell upon arrival. Even after washing, the chemical smell lessened, but it is still there. It may take a few washings to rid them of it entirely.

I'm 5'3, roughly 120lbs. I ordered medium jeans, I really like the way they fit for the most part. I'm returning them and ordering a small, just because they are just the slightest bit loose around the waist.

Color is good, and packaging. I’m 6’2 and 155 lbs and u got medium, but it’s really short and like small and doesn’t have oversized look that I was going for🥲 But material isn’t too thick, not as great quality but color looks good, sizing off tho. So I’ll return them

Amazon Essentials Women's Low Stretch Mid-Rise Regular-Fit Jean Short I'm 5'6", ~130lbs and waist size 27. i ordered a size 2, which is not my normal size (I'd say I'm usually a 4). The waist of these fits great. However, the legs -- and even some of the hips -- are weirdly baggy for this type of short. I like the wash (medium blue vintage) and I like the cutoff look, but given that the fabric is on the sturdier side (something I'd say is good under normal circumstances), the fabric kind of bells out instead of slouching. They're 99% cotton and 1% elastane, so I'll give them a hot wash and see if it makes a difference. If it does, I'll update the review.

Amazon Essentials Women's Low Stretch Mid-Rise Regular-Fit Jean Short 🔹INTRO & DESCRIPTION🔹 The ladies Amazon Essentials shorts are available in four colour choices and a good selection of sizes. I wanted a more traditional look, so I chose the Dark Midnight shade. They arrive in a clear plastic bag. The shorts have the standard five pockets, four of which are a good size. They fasten with a zip fly and button and have belt loops. The hem has a “raw” finish for a more casual look. 🔹OPINION🔹&🔹CONCLUSION🔹 Although I am no spring chicken, I do like to wear shorts in the Summer both in my own garden and on my allotment. These shorts arrived on a freezing cold February afternoon, so trying them on was not something I was looking forward to. However, I tried to remind myself of the long hot Summer last year, as I slid my pasty white legs into the shorts! I am a UK Size 14, and approximately 5 ft 4 inches in height. As I mentioned I chose the size 14 – 16, as I didn’t want anything feeling too tight. Regarding the fit, they do fit well, but they are slightly big in the waist. I do have a fairly slim waist, but big hips and an ample backside. They fit well on the hips and the bum, and the waist can easily be taken in with a belt. The fabric feels soft and comfortable against the skin, and I do like all the pockets. I also really like the colour, as it will match most of my tee shirts. In conclusion, I am happy with these short, and I will certainly look forward to wearing them once the warmer weather arrives. Price wise they are a similar price to what you would expect to pay on the high street, but these get delivered to your door, so you can try them in the comfort of your own home with the added benefit of free returns if needed.

Silver Jeans Co. Women's Plus Size Suki Mid Rise Slim Bootcut Jeans I ordered my usual size. I should have gone down a size but I do like the cut. These jeans have quite a bit of stretch. I wore them around the house for an hour and they did loosen up with wear unfortunately. What I like best though, is how the waist band is cut. It cuves in the back so it sits flat on the waist, no gap. The sides curve up a bit so there is no muffin top (can see in the pic) and the front sits about 1 inch below the belly button. I am 5'3 and ordered the short length which is listed as a 31in. inseam. They are how I like but some might still find the short too long for them. To wear with tennis shoes I will have to cuff up the back or they will drag but should be perfect with a platform. Update: I decided to return as they were just too baggy. I tried the Elyse in the same cut in a size 20, too big, then a size 18, too big. I switched to the infinity cut and those fit how I wanted.

Love these pants. Super lightweight fabric, not too thin but not as thick as my regular Levi’s. Just depends on how you want to wear your clothes.

My daughter is a size 4 at 5'5 feet and 117 lbs, and these size 4 fit her tts! They're comfortable in the waist, and she liked that they're stretchy! The material feels durable, and she loved the color! Great capri jeans!

Love these pants! They are stretchy, comfortable, and the color is just as pictured. They fit well and true to size, with a little wiggle room. For reference I am 5’3 and around 215 lbs. and got a size 18.

I grew up wearing wide leg jeans in the 90s- early 2000s and I'm glad to see that they are back in style! These wide-leg jeans are amazing! I’m 5’4 and 115 pounds, and the pair I got runs a little oversized on me – slightly too large – but I actually love the relaxed, baggy vibe it gives. My sister is 5’9 and 127 pounds, and it looks perfect on her too – but then again, she makes everything look good, lol. The fit is flattering, the fabric is soft and substantial, and the design has a cool, Free People-inspired look without the high price tag. The elastic waist of these jeans is a nice touch- they are basically like sweatpants and just as comfortable, but have the look and style of jeans. I absolutely love them! These jeans are versatile, trendy, and comfy – perfect for casual outings, lounging, or pairing with a cute top for a more put-together look. Definitely recommend grabbing a pair if you like wide-leg, laid-back denim with style! 5 stars!

AUTOMET Women Long Cardigan Sweater Fall Lightweight Outfit Chunky 2026 Button Up Clothes with Pocket Coat Trendy Fashion Very soft on the outside, and true to size. I am 5' 6", and weight 175, and selected the large. This cardigan is definitely priced at a good buying price, and the design is very flattering. The buttons on the cardigan give the outfit a nice pop along with the pockets. In terms of quality the cardigan is solid, however the inside is not as soft as the outside layer. If your looking for a light layer to add on for spring to early fall this is a great choice. As you can see in my images, having this buttoned up, or open looks just as nice. Pro's: Great purchase price for a cardigan of this quality, buttons are a nice pop on the outfit as well as the pockets. Light weight, and comfortable. Con's: The inside layer isn't as soft as the outer layer. Overall: This is a great purchase for those who want a light weight layer, or a little extra warmth when the wind picks up. Beautiful and soft on the outside with the perfect length.

luvamia Women's Wide Leg High Waisted Cropped Capri Jeans These pants are AMAZING! Instantly fell in love with them! They’re the softest denim - like your favorite pair of jeans that have been washed and worn a million times. They have a good stretch to them. I ordered my usual size medium (typically wear a size 6-8, approx 28” waist) and they fit slightly loose. luvamia Women's Wide Leg High Waisted Cropped Capri Jeans These white jeans are great for summer. They fit true to size and are cropped at about mid calf (I'm 5'7"). They are a high rise waist which I usually don't like but these have enough stretch to still be very comfortable. There are 2 front patch pockets that are really cute and flattering.
I ordered a size 2. They're lovely jeans, but I'm 5'3" tall. I was sent a pair of size 2 Long. I'm not sending them back because they'll fit my sister! Great birthday present for her!

I am 5'10" tall, 150 lbs. My waist is 31, my hips are 39, my upper thigh is 23.5, my knee is 16.5, my calf 14.5, and my inseam is 34.5. I ordered an 8 long. (My legs are large, so these are the closest I can realistically get to a somewhat skinnier, non-baggy look.) This is online shopping, ladies: let's help each other out with stats! My local stores don't carry tall jeans, so I can only order online, and I need all the help I can get choosing! Buyers of this Levi style say the denim fabric is thin - it certainly is - and it is probably the reason it does not cut into my waist like the Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda classic straight jeans, which I wore in my 50's, do now. I am switching to this Levi style now I've entered my 60's; my waist is bigger and more sensitive to pressure from stiff and heavy fabrics than it was in earlier stages of my life. For someone my height, the pair I ordered in 8 long covers my ankle bone, so my socks won't show when I wear shoes. This is all I was looking for in that regard. Bottom line: Yes, the waist runs big - hallelujah! - and there is some stretching out, but, I personally have never ever owned a pair of jeans that hasn't stretched out over a day of wearing, so this is not a deal breaker for me. Yes, the denim is thin, so if I want to wear these and be warm here in Michigan in winter, I will have to wear tights under them if I spend any significant time outside, which, I rarely do. I also wear a very long coat, so I expect to be fine regardless.

So I purchased a 2L because I have long legs. They are long enough that they hit the top of my feet and half of my heel. I have a thick heel though. lol. I weigh 163 right now. Even if a gain or lose a pound or 2…they will still fit fine I’m sure. My waist is usually 28inch but I sometimes go for smaller waist sizes for the nice cinch it can create. Due to what I read in the reviews before I purchased I opted to try a size 2 long. These jeans feel like a super stretch light material. Like a begging but heavier. Not too light that it doesn’t hold but feels good and comfortable. It almost covers most of my belly button and covers my little booty. I love them. I was a little worried when I pulled them out the bag but I’m pleasantly surprised and pleased when I tried them on. They need to make more colors!

I'm excited for these. I've only tried them on but they have a nice high waist and long inseam. I'm 5'9," and 32" inseams don't quite cut it. There was the option to buy 12 Long or 12 Tall, but under product description, it didn't have a definition for what tall meant, which is frustrating. But I bought the Tall size and when I got the pants, the sticker tag said 34" L which is what I was hoping for. I'm 180 and I got a size 12. They fit good now but I think I will probably want a belt because they are quite stretchy.

I wanted to get some straight leg jeans and these are exactly what I was looking for! For reference, I am 5'3", 133 lbs. The length is perfect to wear with boots, flats or sneakers. I read someone else's review and it was most ! I ordered the 4S, W27/L28. Perfect fit, great quality and great price!

Love the fit. Signature jeans are a little thinner and more generous than normal Levi's. These fit really well. Nice length. I'm usually an 8 in other Levi's but in Signature I easily get away with a 6L. I'm 5ft 9" and generally need a 34" inseam on straight leg jeans. My waist is currently 32-33" and my hips are 43-44". Hopefully that helps someone more than the usual height and weight which tells us nothing about weight distribution! 🩷

roswear Women’s Capri Pants Trendy Mid Rise Skinny Stretchy Front Seam Denim Jeans These are so attractive….. LOVE them! I’m 5’5, and 172 pounds. I ordered an XL based on size charts mostly, and a few reviews, but, because of the stretch, I could’ve gotten a large, so if you’re on the border, keep that in mind. None of us want stretchy pants that are going to fall off of us after we wear them all day.!Just got them May 4, and trying the decide whether to return them for a smaller size large. Regardless, they are very well made and and good looking Jean capris. I’m going to take more revealing photos to share when I have someone come home to do it.

roswear Women’s Capri Pants Trendy Mid Rise Skinny Stretchy Front Seam Denim Jeans 10/10 get your exact size. The jeans do have some stretch to them. I was able to move how I wanted to in them very comfortable coming from a big girl. You get what you pay for and it was worth it. I’m 5’2 so I had to pull them up some to get the full capris look.

roswear Women’s Capri Pants Trendy Mid Rise Skinny Stretchy Front Seam Denim Jeans I weigh 185 pounds and I’m 5”11 and bought a medium. They fit perfectly!!! I love them and they are super comfortable.

Social media kept blasting me with ads for these jeans, and I finally gave in. These jeans are flattering, VERY stretchy, and lighter weight for spring. The higher waist balances my normal length torso with my shorter legs! I’m picky about how jeans lay on my crotch, bum, and thighs - I like baggy jeans on the legs, but not baggy in the hips and crotch! These jeans fit the bill. The stretch made the jeans comfortable to wear with the look of being smoothed without being constricted. I felt the jeans held up to all day wear and didn’t get loose/saggy as some stretch jeans do. I don’t mind that there aren’t any pockets in the front as I like the extra smooth look. The pockets on the bum are ample and deep and held my cell phone perfectly. I bought the light blue first and liked the fit so much I bought the light green and dark blue (all three at ~$36 sale pricing) and am waiting to buy the pink or white (got to wait for that sale price to come back again - I got the purple on sale for $15!!!!!!!) My only con is these jeans only come in a raw hem which is too casual for my business casual work setting. However, again I like the fit so much I can accept just wearing for home and weekends! These are just a great comfortable pair of casual jeans! FYI, I am 5’3”, 115 lbs, 4 months postpartum with twins. I’m typically a size 0/25 and in these I’m wearing an XS.

I bought the light blue first and liked the fit so much I bought the light green and dark blue (all three at ~$36 sale pricing) and am waiting to buy the pink or white (got to wait for that sale price to come back again - I got the purple on sale for $15!!!!!!!) My only con is these jeans only come in a raw hem which is too casual for my business casual work setting. However, again I like the fit so much I can accept just wearing for home and weekends! These are just a great comfortable pair of casual jeans! FYI, I am 5’3”, 115 lbs, 4 months postpartum with twins. I’m typically a size 0/25 and in these I’m wearing an XS.

omg where have these been all my life!?! these are the best pants ever! sorry this ins't the best photo but i am 5'7 and 125 lb. i have most of my height in my legs. these are not good if you have short legs probably because they are the highest waisted pants i have EVER found. also you are going to want to size down. i originally bought the small and had to return for the XS. they are SUPER stretchy. i like they still have structure though. just a nice stretchy denim feeling. they definitely are unmistakable denim though. i dont think i could get away with wearing them at work (denim is not allowed). i would love another pair but tbh i dont like the colors that much otherwise i def would!

I ordered these jeans in pink, size XS, and I’m really happy with the fit and color. The light pink is exactly what I was hoping for—soft, pretty, and easy to style. They fit me well and are very comfortable to wear. They’re advertised as cropped, but since I’m only 5'1", they actually fit more like full-length jeans on me. That’s the only reason I took off one star. Otherwise, they’re adorable and feel great on. Definitely a good buy!

I am 5’3 and about 155lbs. I ordered these in a medium and they fit great. They are the perfect length to wear with heels or flats. They have just the perfect amount of stretch.

Gloria Vanderbilt Women's Amanda Capri Jeans I thought these skimmer shorts were super cute! I’m 5’8 around 155 lbs and I got a size 8 and they fit really good. They have a stretchy waist & are loose in the legs. The length hit right below the knee. I’m definitely going to get another pair!

Gloria Vanderbilt Women's Amanda Capri Jeans The cat does not approve! If you’re looking for bell-bottom cropped jeans, and you’re more than 6ft tall with seriously big calves, these are great. I was hoping for Capri jeans that fit close -as these are pictured in the item description - but these are not even close to that in reality. These are going back and the search continues!

Gap Women's Mid Rise Legging Pant Listen, I didn't ask them to, I didn't know about it in advance, heck, I didn't even know they were aware of my existence. But obviously, Gap did & does. They went ahead and designed a pair of custom jean leggings made to my exact measurements. Now, even though I plan to buy every single pair of size 6/28, I decided it would only be fair to share my stats with you as well as the stats of MY custom made, comfortable, flattering & 100% perfect fitting jean leggings. Me: 140-145# 5'3" 33.5" waist (measured at the bottom of my bellybutton because that's where MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS sit) 40" hips (measured at the widest point, mid-bum) MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS (Measured flat) 9" rise 14" waist 17" hips They have a REAL zipper fly, not one of those "just for show" kind commonly found on jean leggings. Every pair I've tried in that past with a "fake fly" always had extra fabric that bunched up at the crotch & made me look like I was smuggling oranges whenever I sat down (& they didn't look any better when standing up, either). The traditional 5 pockets are fully functional (real) as well. The stitching/color are absolute perfection & the plain, proportionately sized back pockets are what my denim dreams are made of. If you have time pls check out the pic I attached to see another one of the reasons why these fit so well. I hope my humble opinion helps you in some way. Thank you for reading.

Gap Women's Mid Rise Legging Pant The first wash really helps the fit on these jeans - GAP Mid Rise Legging Pant - Dark Willoughby - 31 Regular. I tried them on after delivery and the legs were super tight (never had that problem) and the waist stretched to huge. Fortunately, after the first wash and dry (warm and medium), the fit was back to normal. I wear a 31 in all jeans and these are to size. However, there continues to be lots of stretch at the waistband, I notice significant droop as the day wears on. An initial high-rise becomes a sinking mid-rise, a belt may be useful. As a positive, they can handle generous weight fluctuations. I like the Dark Willoughby. I’m 5”5” 135lbs.

Wrangler Women's Retro Mae Mid Rise Wide Leg Trouser Jean First off, I LOVE these jeans! Secondly, the reviews I read on here were a huge help in sizing. It’s weird because this are definitely Women’s jeans, but the sizing is in Juniors. For reference I am 6’0, 170-175 lbs, and a solid Women’s size 10/30, which would translate to a Juniors size 11. Per the suggestion of previous reviews, I reluctantly sized down to a 9. The last thing I wanted was for the jeans to fit for an hour, only to stretch beyond repair. I ordered a size 9 with 34” inseam. I am SO happy I did! When I pulled it out the bag I thought to myself, there’s NO WAY this is gonna work. But I squeezed myself into the initially doubtful fit. Wisely I decided to wear them around the house for a couple hours, w/o removing tags (just in case.. again, because of reviews). And to my pleasant surprise this wonderful pair of jeans decided to form to my figure in the best ways possible. So if you’re in doubt and you got some junk in the trunk, size down and trust the process. Because I could totally see if I had bought these in an 11, I wouldn’t like them now. Also the 34” inseam does almost hit the floor for me, but the next pair I order will be a 36”, just so I have some extra length.

I ordered the brown cheetah print and they are now my favorite jeans. The fit is awesome with a bit of stretch. The high waist and flare leg are very flattering and stylish. I ordered a small. For reference I am 5’4 and 120 lbs.

What I love most about these is for someone who is on the shorter side (5'1-5"3) the bottom of the pants fit very nicely and do not drag on the ground and get dirty or ripped. The waist is a nice height not being too high or low and fits snuggly without any gap around th top waist perimeter. The fit is form fitting while still having stretch however the fabric is not a soft stretch but more of a firm stretch because the fabric Is just slightly firm. It'll hold up well against wrinkles and iron well. The print is bold without being overbearing for people of a smaller frame or build which is a plus. I would pair it with either a light plain colored shirt or a dark or black plain shirt and dress shoes or sandals. Not sure I would purchase again as my wife prefers very soft materials in general. Other than that these are really nice pants

The material quality and stitching are good quality for the price. They are right on par with typical paints in the $20 price range. They should last for a fair bit but are not as durable as high-end pants. The print patterns look good and clear. The shaping is a little different in reality versus the listing photos. They are not quite as tapered at the waist, and not as flared at the feet. They are true to the size measurements so if your measurements match the listings size chart, you should be good to go. If you’re on the short size they will not ride high above the ankle like the picture. The listing says the model is 5’8” tall and my model is 5”2” tall so the pant length is pretty true to the photos. Overall at the price they are a good value and fit correctly.

This pair of leopard print cropped jeans are nice quality made jeans. The fabric is 98% cotton and 2% elastane. There is quite a bit of stretch in the material. The thickness of the fabric is between medium and thin, in my opinion. They are soft and comfortable to wear. The have 4 functional patch style pockets, 2 in front and 2 on the back. The button and zipper closures work smoothly without any sticking points. I ordered these jeans in a size XXL, with my regular pants size being an XL. I sized up because of the 98% cotton fabric, but missed reading that "stretch" was a part of the description on the item page, lol. I really should have ordered my regular size of XL for these jeans! While they don't fall off me, there is definitely a loose feel in the fit around my waist. I will likely being wearing a belt when I wear these, to be on the safe side. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. For my height, I think the crop on these doesn't look so good, but perhaps wearing them with flat shoes or sandals will work better for me. Or go the other way and wear a bit of heels? These are good quality, fashionable jeans and I will recommend them. They have plenty of stretch in the fabric, so I would advise to not size up. Check the luvamia size chart to get a nice fit. Their sizes are true to size. The current price (1-9-2026) of $35.99 is a fair value, and there is a coupon available at this time that would make them a good value, in my opinion.

I am obsessed with these pants. Finally my leopard search is over and couldn’t be more happy. The denim fit of these is seriously perfection. They actually feel like denim, granted there’s a 2% context of elastic - just enough to make them comfy when bending or sitting. I had no issue with them bagging out by end of the day either. I chose my regular size small. I’m consistently a US 4 at 122lbs with my measurements at 27”W/36”H @ 5’ tall. While advertised as cropped length, let’s face it at my height no much is cropped on me. So these hit just about the top of my foot. I’ve paired them with Slingbacks and also a pair of ankle boots with a shorter heel. Super cute with so many styling options. Dressed up or down works well. They washed up great also. I normally wash in a cooler temp and tumble dry on low. No issues with wrinkles out of the dryer. Well made, great quality and so super cute!
reviewer_name_raw: Versatile styling options, great fit TTS.

user_comment: I ordered a S based on the size chart and they're perfect (133#, 5'3", 27"W, 38" hip for reference). They're fitted without being too snug, a M definitely would've been too big. The length is also great, they hit right above my ankles but are just long enough to cover the top of my chelsea boots so I can wear these year round without showing any skin in cold weather. The bronze leopard print looks just like the listing images. The fabric is a nice, weighty denim and they're well constructed. I love that these can be dressed up or down, they're SO much fun! Honestly, I've been looking for any excuse to wear these out of the house. For around $35 these are a steal.

LOVE these and this brand so much! I’ve been on the hunt for a good pair of leopard jeans and these are it! They are so similar to a pair that’s almost $200 that I’ve been wanting. They have just enough stretch that makes them so comfortable all day long. I’m a preschool teacher, so I am up and down and kneeling all day long. These hold their shape and have the same comfort as wearing leggings. Normal pockets on the back that I can slip my phone into smaller pockets on the front. The cropped kick flare is super flattering! I am 5’4, around 185 lbs, usually a size 14. I ordered an XL. They fit well, but I probably could’ve ordered the large.

Lee Women's Petite Relaxed Fit All Day Straight Leg Pant Let me start first by giving measurements for reference: 5'3" , 137lbs , 29" waist, 38" hips, 27" inseam That being said, I have tried all 6/8 short/petite relaxed/curvy pants on Amazon (Rafaella Gaberdine curvy, Signature Levi curvy Straight, Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda & GV Curvy Skinny, Dickies Curvy Straight, Lee Relaxed Side Elastic Straight, etc) and ALL of them either had a gap in the waist, were too tight across the rear, or were simply just too long.. however THESE pants fit great and had neither issue! I finally found a pants that not only fit, but are modest for work-- flattering but not figure hugging. I bought these in size 6 petite in colors (pictured from left to right) Indigo Wash (like a blue jean color/type fabric) Black Imperial Blue Mini Glenplaid (Black on light grey- NO BLUE in this, which i actually prefer) Cypress (Dark Olive Green) Jet Black/White Rockhill Plaid Falcon (pinkish brown) Upon close inspection, I noticed 1 of the 6 pairs I had purchased (Falcon) were defective. On the back leg, it appears as if holes were accidentally cut in the pants and then attempted to be "patched" by resewing it back together. (see pictures) Very disappointed that Amazon would allow this and have reordered/requested a return on this one pair. Because of the issue and trouble, I am deducting 1 star.

I am in the middle of my weight loss journey and needed something while I am in between sizes. I am 5’3 and weigh 216lbs (down from 262lbs!!) and I ordered size 18 Average. They are a little loose in the waste and legs and are a little long, but will be perfect with a pair of boots or heals. They are super comfortable and I am hoping that they will shrink a little in the dryer. Can’t beat the price for an item I won’t be wearing for very long. I will be buying more as I loose more weight.

I’m adding this info for future buyers because I myself like to read a lot of reviews before buying clothes lol.I’m 5’7” and fluctuate between 145lbs - 150lbs. I bought a size 8 (regular length). I’ve recently gained weight so I don’t know my true size right now, I just ordered one size up from what I used to be. I still fit in Free People size 28 jeans, if that’s of any help to anyone.These jeans fit comfortably enough BUT they’re also pretty snug so if they shrink in the dryer they probably won’t fit me anymore. I honestly think I could’ve gone with a size 10 in case they do end up shrinking.. I’m still considering exchanging them. I haven’t washed or worn them out yet, so I don’t know how well they’ll hold up. I will say that the material seems to be thicker and better quality than I expected. They have some stretch but not enough to be considered that weak jegging material (I don’t like), they actually feel like jeans. I also like the length, they’re not short and not too long, as you can see in the picture (I’m not wearing shoes). As for the rise of the waist, I'd say it's mid-rise because it sits about an inch below my belly button. They're definitely not low-rise on me, which I'm glad about because I hate low-rise.All in all, they seem like a pretty good pair of jeans, and if my first impression is correct, they’ll be sturdy and last a decent while.I hope my review helped someone out!

First I love the look and feel of these skinny jeans. I am normally a 18 and ordered the XXL, however the crotch is stretched too tight and so there is a gap there. I am 5’4” and 217#. I am long waisted with a lot of my weight in the belly area. I am going to try sizing up to the 3X but I often have this problem with being between sizes. I will update my review after ordering the larger size. **Update** I have worn the larger 3x size now twice and I can’t rave enough about these jeans. They are really comfortable and you don’t have to constantly pull up the waist. They sit above the belly button on me. I will be buying more of these and I highly recommend them but I would size up if you are tummy and bottom heavy.

Ok the important thing first; I'm 5'7", 138lbs and usually wear a comfortable "29", or 6-8, in jeans. I'm a pretty solid medium, sometimes a large, never a small. I got a size 8 in these jeans and they're definitely too big. The size chart has the 8 at a 29.5-30.5 and the 6 is 28.5-29.5, so i think me getting an 8 is my bad lol. You'll see in the pics that I added a belt & it definitely helps? But they're still super big. I do think if you're in between sizes, it's safe to size down in these!Moving on, they're DEFINITELY baggie and super soft. If you get the right size I think they'd be so cute for a baggie, comfy, casual look. The only thing I don't like is how LONG they are though, and I'm decently tall. But again, sizing down and wearing them a bit above the midrise length they're advertised as may fix that too.The price is also a plus. A nice, comfy pair of stylish baggie jeans for under 30 bucks is a great deal i think! I'll probably come back & buy the size 6 soon because they are so comfortable and affordable.ps- I didnt realize my mirror was so dirty please don't judge me lol. Have a great rest of your day!

When I'm at home, I avoid wearing anything that feels tight, though I do prefer fairly close-fitting clothing for ease of movement. These leggings are stretchy and soft. I'm between M & L sizes in pants, and since these will be mostly for wearing at home, I sized up. They turned out to be just the right size for me for comfort. The waist band keeps them in place without squeezing. The straight leg gives me some space, but doesn't get in my way. This loose fit of the size L on me doesn't offer much support, so I wouldn't wear it in public without a fairly long tunic over it. I'm not sure whether a size M would be any more supportive, considering the soft fabric, or just feel constricting around the waist while showing even more of the shapes I don't want to show. To me, this sized-up pair are perfect as lounge pants. The front pockets are just for decoration. The back pockets are usable, but not as deep as I'd like if I were to wear them while out doing errands. They are very wrinkle-resistant. For length reference, I'm 5'1", and I expect to need to hem about 5" from the cuff. I recommend these as lounge pants for all body types. I recommend them as ready-to-wear for taller women who aren't seeking support from their leggings. (Vine review)
I wore these pants to a concert and was not disappointed! Very stretchy and comfortable I got lots of compliments I am 5’8 280 pounds and the 4x I got was falling off so maybe down size if you can!

At the time of this review, I’m 135 lbs,, 26", 37" hips, 5’ 7” and the size 4 fit is lovely. I love the high waist. I have really wide calves, 16" so I give them a cuff to make them deliberately more above-ankle length and balanced looking for my big legs. These are a medium denim weight with good stretch. They didn't "stretch out" after wearing them all afternoon, into the evening. I am not blessed my caboose area, and these are slim all the way thru my hips and behind. They've been washed on cold gentle cycle and tumbled dried on low - easy care, makes this pair a big win!

*****UPDATE***** [Changing my review from a 5 to a 3] After I left this review, I went back and forth with the sellers. (I know this isn't a seller review but this information is necessary because the product does not match the information posted online. This also isn't rating the quality of the seller, it's rating the quality of the product and the amazon listing.) I sent them the pictures of the capris I received and the description online. They said I was right, the jeans are mislabeled and what I received was dark blue. They told me to re-order them and choose light blue and this time, they would ensure I was sent light blue. I did as they said and they sent me another pair of navy blue capris. I contacted them again and their reply was that "there was a discrepancy between their color name and EDITED PRODUCT PHOTOS" and the real color. Basically, light blue doesn't exist and the photo that's posted is not real. To summarize: there are five blue colors they have listed online and literally four of them are NAVY BLUE and the images online are edited/fake. This is a big problem: when people shop, they choose the color they want. They expect to receive a color that matches what they select. Because these images on the amazon page are edited, the listing is inaccurate. *****ORIGINAL REVIEW***** I love these jeggings. They are NOT thick tough denim and they have a ton of stretch. Personally, I prefer them higher on my calf so I cuffed them and although the cuff certainly reveals they're not true denim, it looks okay. They're SO EXTREMELY COMFORTABE I can wear them all day. I do not have much of a defined waist and sometimes, the waistband can dig into me. Not these! They don't roll down at all and, if I had to, I bet I could sleep in them. Yep. They're that comfy. The only problem is I was looking for a medium blue. I chose the color "light blue" which, according to the picture, should be a true medium blue. What I was sent was a very dark blue. I cannot determine if I was sent the wrong color or if the listing was very off with the pictures of their colors. Anyone know about the color thing? I'd keep these but I'd still like the lighter ones too... Me: 5'2", 155lbs, apple-shaped, fat thighs.

This is my first time ordering jeans online because I can never seem to find ones that fit me even at the store. I'm 5 2", 120 pounds, and ordered a size: 8 Petite. The attached photo is how they fit. It's pretty good, except I had to fold in the hem (so I will be fixing that myself, yup! even in petite size), but that is a minor thing) the rest of the fit is good. They are comfortable. If you're on the edge, definitely worth trying these since they're easy to return.

As a 40 yr old mom, embracing the wide-leg jeans trend feels like stepping back into my early 2000s high school years. It took a few outfit trials before I felt comfortable. The fit was great, the high-waist is comfy, and the jeans themselves are stretchy--all pluses as a mom of 5. The length works great for my 5'2" height. I love that they don't drag umder my shoes ( I wore them with Adidas Court shoes), so I'm hopeful I can avoid the shredded hems I rocked back in the day. My husband is still on the fence about them. but my youngest sister-in-law and my nieces assure me I am "on-trend" which probably hasn't happened in a while! Definitely stepping out of (or maybe stepping back into) my skinny jeans comfort-zone, but I'm glad I gave them a shot!

These are so nice to have on hand because I really dislike denim and these are the perfect option for those casual Fridays. But although these are pretty stretchable they’re just not that flattering for me. I have prominent hip dips and a very strong postpartum mom pooch that this can’t contain and I had hoped it would be a little more snug around the waist area at least. The length is way too long for me because although I do have a shorter torso and longer legs, I have a petite body type (123-5’2”) that the hems are visibly too long, and being rolled up has to go halfway up my shin or else it’ll be rings around my ankles lol. I am normally a size 4 but the smalls feel like a 5-6 and sort of wish they did have an XS so it would fit my body a little more snugly but they’re not bad and being of stretchy material I had hoped it would fit more snugly like a 2-4 and not like 5-6. I think the price is okay but for the quality they’re at I think they could be a little better as I found a few stray threads and the fitting isn’t as advertised. It is very high-waisted which I love but I do have to wear a long tanktop or shirt because I feel like my c-toe and my hip dips are a ton more visible wearing this lol. It’s not that big of a deal since I tend to wear long tees and tanks anyways but I do wish it was a little more flattering. These are comfortable to wear and super easy to put on. I would probably rate these a 4.5* for convenience and stretch and color but not fully 5* because the sizing, length, thickness and flattery categories aren’t great. I still highly recommend these because if you don’t have jeggings and dislike the conventional feel of denim, these are a great option to have in faux denim blue and faux denim black on days you might need them.

Okay, listen up fellow Amazonians: I am 6'2" and 210 lbs, and these pants are so close to being long enough it’s almost disrespectful. Like… they ALMOST understood the assignment. Stupid close. Close enough that I’m willing to forgive them because everything else about them is fantastic. They’re thick but breathable (in a good, quality-fabric way). They’re wrinkle resistant, which is great because ironing is a chore I absolutely refuse to participate in. They have the perfect wide leg that makes it look like I’m purposefully elegant instead of floundering to both have pants long enough that are also stylish. Thy are pretty sturdy and not very stretchy so buy your actual size. And the navy color? Chef’s kiss. Honestly, if Lee ever decides to add two more inches of length, I will personally write them a thank-you letter and bake them cookies. Until then, these are the best “not flood pants” I’ve ever found. Would buy again. Already did. In black. Same review but you know, I'd say black instead of navy. Duh.

I am 5’11” and 185lbs. I have ALWAYS struggled my whole life finding jeans that were comfortable around my thighs and that were long enough. I normally would buy silver jeans but those are very expensive, I found the jeans to be a good quality at a great price. They were not suffocating my thighs and were a little bit stretchy which I liked a lot. Also I usually have troubles finding jeans that fit my thighs and hips as well as my waist and these jeans were not too big on my waist! I do have a 36” inseam and I got a Large. I am definitely buying another pair! It is really a dream come true

I'm 5'5, curvy fit, 135ish. Not long legged. I normally wear a 27/6 in jeans ... I sized up to an 8 in this. Nothing was tight but I didn't live the look. I've seen barrell jeans (not tried on) and mom jeans--each a baggy look. I do own the Free People knit jumper and a FP denim oversized set of bibs and like both. Each of those are an obvious oversize. These jeans, on the other hand, just were not baggy looking as the model shows. I read many reviews and hoped they'd work but they literally look like my mom wore in the mid 80s and hers fot about like this. Just not a cute look, imo, if they are not baggy enough. Go big, or go home. I still wear skinny jeans. I have wode keg jeans. I have cropped flares. I cannot wear straight leg or boyfriend style bc I'm a curvy fot (the waist always sticks out too badly). Providing this info, as I think it is all relevant. Last, I'm almost 50. This look might seem cuter on a gal my size but in her 20s. Or with no curve and less boots. Material was fine; blue color a tad off, seemed almost frosted-ish.

I'm a 40 yr old mom who keeps wanting to try all the new trends like cropped wide leg jeans (aren't these just capris?) like I see in Mom-toks and they all look great in their baggy jeans. I wanted to love them but I'm just not sure they're made for my body type.I'm 5'2" and 130lbs and got a small. The length was what I expected for a cropped jean but I felt like the waist just made me feel wide. Shirt tucked or front-tucked didn't really work for me either, maybe it's the shirt? I pulled back one of pant legs so you can see just how wide-legged these are on me. It's not a crazy palazzo but comfrotably loose. Overall, I still want to like them. I'm going to hang on to them and see if I can find the right combination of shirt/pants ration and the wide legged feel will grow on me.

These are definitely high-rise and the material feels really nice. Great stretch. But they are definitely not belly friendly. If you've got a fupa and are looking for pants that are both comfortable and flattering, you're only gonna get comfortable here. If you don't care about showing some gut and your only concern is good fitting stretchy jeggings that won't show off your granny panties, these are a solid option. I'm 5'7" with hips and hormonal weight gain currently at about 185 (thanks, thyroid), and carry most of that extra in the lower tummy area. I also walk around with it really sucked in so it isn't as prominent here. I like a longer inseam, and hate ankle length pants. They fit perfectly, are a great length without bunching, and don't cause mooseknuckle.

These jeans are comfy, and the material feels nice against my skin. Sadly, though, the fit isn't quite right for me, and they feel and look a bit bulkier than I had hoped. I'm about 120# and 5'4", and my waist is 29-30", so I went with the medium according to the size chart, and really wish I went with the small instead. Not only do they feel a fit bulky, they are super wide in the hips, which I don't fill out, so they kind of just hang out loosely, which is my least favorite thing about them. They did wash and dry in the machine without any issues, and they are comfortable, so I'll probably just use them for around the house. The drawstring waist makes them easy to adjust and add to their comfort.
