I’m 5’6”, 155#, and normally a size medium in bottoms for reference. I ordered these jeggings in a size medium and they fit true to size. Very comfortable, no binding. Fabric lays on the body nice, no baggy spots. While I do like the look of these jean style leggings, I still think they are overpriced. The 39.99 price point is a bit much for the quality and not being a name brand. The waistband looks a little odd and is a dead giveaway that these aren’t real jeans. The rear is a little flattening and the waistband comes just up to the belly button. I wore these on a long walk the other day and I didn’t have to pull them up or adjust at any point. Decent leggings, but pricey.

I am 5’7” and weigh 140lbs. I am curvy. I wear a medium in everything, except jeggings. I ordered a large in these, and they are perfect. I wear a lot of jeggings and they are all not created equal. Some have very thin fabric that stretches out and then sags in the crotch, and I have to keep pulling them up all day. Some have so much Lycra that they start to hurt at the waistline after wearing them for awhile. Some hit that side bulge right in the middle and now the side bulge can be seen through a tshirt. These, however, are the Goldilocks of jeggings. They are just the right weight fabric, just enough Lycra to hold me back a bit, but not cut off circulation. My favorite quality, however is the high waist! I have taken two pictures of my side: one where I pull the waist down a bit to show where normal jeggings fall (blubber bulge), and one shot of where these jeggings ride above the bulge. No more fatty roles to see through my tshirts!! I am super happy with these. These are my new go-to jeggings.

Some have very thin fabric that stretches out and then sags in the crotch, and I have to keep pulling them up all day. Some have so much Lycra that they start to hurt at the waistline after wearing them for awhile. Some hit that side bulge right in the middle and now the side bulge can be seen through a tshirt. These, however, are the Goldilocks of jeggings. They are just the right weight fabric, just enough Lycra to hold me back a bit, but not cut off circulation. My favorite quality, however is the high waist! I have taken two pictures of my side: one where I pull the waist down a bit to show where normal jeggings fall (blubber bulge), and one shot of where these jeggings ride above the bulge. No more fatty roles to see through my tshirts!! I am super happy with these. These are my new go-to jeggings.

Favorite Jeans & affordable! First time ordering jeans online & after I’ve lost 40+ lbs, at that! Loves: I am 5’8” and these go below my ankle, so I can cuff them at my ankle, if I wanted & wear cute bootie shoes. I have SKINNY legs and they are very fitting & flattering! Material, zipper, & button all feel very durable. All 4 pockets are REAL! Also, the price is not bad at all compared to other pairs of jeans on here with much thinner material. Possible inconveniences: Not a lot of stretch in the waist area, but it works. I’m 168-170 lbs and a size 31 was suggested . I got a size 30 because all other stretch jeans I own, usually loosen up throughout the day; idk if these will, honestly. The material is on the thicker side. If my thighs and calves weren’t skinny, these would not fit, so they are SKINNY. I could have felt very comfortable in a 31, but I am expecting to lose additional weight, so these will do, but whatever size is suggested to you, these are true to size in the waist. BUY THEM!

I am a size 16 pant and ordered the large. I am 5’4” and weigh 185. The large fits well overall. I don’t think the waist is as comfortable as the popular H brand but these are a great alternative. I ordered the dark blue and they are very dark. There is no stone washed look on the thighs or calves. It is a true color drenched dark blue. There are four pockets. Two on the front and two back pockets. These are a cotton blend so will be breathable but wash in cold to prevent shrinkage. There is plenty of stretch to the material. The length is great for me but anyone taller may have an issue with them fitting their leg length. All seams and stitching were straight and closed well. There were no loose strings. Overall these are a comfortable quality pair of jeggings. Price is comparable to other brands.

I love wide-legged jeans, so when I came across these, I knew I had to try them. I began by choosing my size, which I could not get on until I laid on the bed to be able to zip them up. After getting up, however, I was really disappointed. I honestly don't understand who these jeans were made for because they are so incredibly long. I am average height at 5'4", but I wouldn't be surprised if these jeans were so long that even a woman over 6 feet may have a problem with the length. I should also note that the jeans come with a note letting you know that they may bleed in the wash and should be washed alone. I appreciated knowing that but can't understand why they were not prepped so that wouldn't happen. Needless to say, I will be washing them and having them cut and hemmed. That means that the price point was not a deal as I originally believed.
They don’t really stretch. They do fit I’m 5’7 and 380lbs. I am a size 24.

I accidentally ordered this in a size large when I have other automet t-shirts in a medium, but it actually fit spot on! (so I do recommend sizing up one). For reference, I'm 5'3 145lbs with an athletic build. You can't really go wrong with the black color. I've washed it once and it didn't shrink or fade. The buttons are made to look like a faux cork and it even came with some spare in case these ones fall off (though they are sturdily sewn on)! The shirt drapes down in the front and back and then swoops up on the sides. I like my shirts on the longer side like this & I feel like it compliments my body well. The fabric is soft and there was no smell opening the bag! I can't wait until it's warm weather to wear this cute shirt!

For reference, I carry all my weight in my midsection so it's very difficult to find jeans that fit comfortably and don't show my apron belly. I typically wear a size 12 or 14 and am 5'1" tall, so length is also always an issue. I first ordered the $98 Free People barrel jeans in all the colors, hoping they would look cute. I tried one on and didn't even bother trying on the rest, I immediately returned them. Unless you're a size 2, the FP jeans were clownishly huge in the legs, but the denim was buttery soft. I am not typically satisfied with the quality of clothing on Amazon, but decided to try several barrel jeans because I wasn't having any luck from name brands. To my surprise, these fit GREAT! The fabric is a denim blend so not the same quality of designer fabric, but they are beyond flattering - comfortable stretch in the waist, slim through the hips, then a reasonable barrel flare with a tapered bottom. They skim the tops of my shoes so I have to cuff them up a little and they're perfect. Might hem them, but I hate losing the original stitching. I ordered 3 colors and love all of them. The Black is perfectly faded, the Deep Blue is a really nice medium blue, and I'm so happy they make the Retro Blue, which is just like the FP jeans with the slightest green tinge. I initially ordered a pair of barrel jeans from another seller, Grapent, and I kept them but the wash is not as nice, the pockets kind of stretch out, and these jeans are a better shape and quality of colors. If you're an apple shape and hard to fit, I really recommend these. I'm so happy to be able to buy into a trend for less than half the cost of the designer brands. What a find!!!

Let me start by saying, if the right person with the right figure gets the right size; these jeans would be amazing! I don’t think they look good on ME…. But the quality of them are actually quite nice and they have the potential to be someone’s favorite pair of jeans! Let me start with the material: it’s a thick & stretchy denim, a lot like jegging material but nice, thick, and slimming. I am 5’9, 148 pounds and I’m generally a size 8. I got a size medium. They fit but I think I’d maybe like them more if I had gotten a size large. I think these run a little small in length (27ish inch inseam) this could be a great thing if you are short! If you’re tall like me this might be way too short! The other place it runs small is the rise! I would describe these as more of a mid to slight high rise. The fact that these are “paper bag” style , the spot where they’re hitting me isn’t flattering. If the rise was 1-2 inches higher they’d hit me at the natural waist and possibly would provide a nice snatched look, instead; they hit me right in the middle of my “love handle” area, causing a bit of frumpiness. However, like I said, if I got one size larger I feel there would be room to pull these up to my natural waist and also get a bit more length! They don’t run small on the hip / thigh area though. So idk if sizing up would be a problem like in a saggy booty sense. I’d have to order and see. I do think though that if you are a person who really loves this style of pant for the paper bag waist, cropped wide leg, and trouser style pockets - they might be worth giving them a try!!!! Because the material is thick and stretchy so they honestly feel like a High quality pair of pants!!! Short girls will love these!!!

So idk if sizing up would be a problem like in a saggy booty sense. I’d have to order and see. I do think though that if you are a person who really loves this style of pant for the paper bag waist, cropped wide leg, and trouser style pockets - they might be worth giving them a try!!!! Because the material is thick and stretchy so they honestly feel like a High quality pair of pants!!! Short girls will love these!!!

Let me start by saying, if the right person with the right figure gets the right size; these jeans would be amazing! I don’t think they look good on ME…. But the quality of them are actually quite nice and they have the potential to be someone’s favorite pair of jeans! Let me start with the material: it’s a thick & stretchy denim, a lot like jegging material but nice, thick, and slimming. I am 5’9, 148 pounds and I’m generally a size 8. I got a size medium. They fit but I think I’d maybe like them more if I had gotten a size large. I think these run a little small in length (27ish inch inseam) this could be a great thing if you are short! If you’re tall like me this might be way too short! The other place it runs small is the rise! I would describe these as more of a mid to slight high rise. The fact that these are “paper bag” style , the spot where they’re hitting me isn’t flattering. If the rise was 1-2 inches higher they’d hit me at the natural waist and possibly would provide a nice snatched look, instead; they hit me right in the middle of my “love handle” area, causing a bit of frumpiness. However, like I said, if I got one size larger I feel there would be room to pull these up to my natural waist and also get a bit more length! They don’t run small on the hip / thigh area though. So idk if sizing up would be a problem like in a saggy booty sense. I’d have to order and see. I do think though that if you are a person who really loves this style of pant for the paper bag waist, cropped wide leg, and trouser style pockets - they might be worth giving them a try!!!! Because the material is thick and stretchy so they honestly feel like a High quality pair of pants!!! Short girls will love these!!!

I got these to wear to work, I work at the post office and so I got the navy blue color. Based off of the reviews I read about shrinking after washing, I went on ahead and bought the long and I am so glad I did. I am 5 foot 4 and I normally wear a regular length or a short. I washed and dried these pants and they shrunk about 4 inches. Other than that they are fantastic they have held up really well and I love how thick the fabric is but they are so soft and comfortable it feels like I'm wearing pajamas almost. I am including pictures of the pair that I washed and dried next to the ones that haven't been washed it so you can see the length difference. I don't know how they would do if they weren't put in a dryer if they would shrink or not, but I did put mine in the dryer and they are the perfect length for me now

I ordered these pull on jeans in the medium dark wash. I really like them color and they are easy to pull on. I’m currently on a health journey and in the process am losing weight. I like that these jeans are drawstring so that I’m able to wear them longer than traditional jeans as I lose weight. The elastic waist and drawstring are more comfortable than having to wear a belt to hold your pants up. I’m 5’6” and I feel like the length is perfect for me. I currently wear a size 18 and although the pants fit, they are a little snug in the thighs. But I am not surprised as I carry most of my weight in that area. So they could’ve been a little looser in that area in my opinion. The fabric is nice and thick, I can see these jeans lasting several years with multiple washes. The jeans are also versatile. Wear them the same way you would sweats lounging around your house, or dress them up with a nice blouse and pair of shoes. Either way you can’t go wrong with these jeans.

Prospective buyers should take note of this!

I love these! They fit true to size, are cute, and, most importantly, are comfortable. They wash and dry well-no shrinkage. I’m about 5’4-5’5 and they are a pretty good length on me. I think they are meant to be on the shorter side, but they hit me just past the top of my shoe. I don’t want them any shorter. The only small issue is that I have to keep an eye on the drawstring when I tie it because it will slip to the side throughout the day and I hate when things are uneven. It’s not a deal breaker and I recommend these jeans if you like this style. *sorry for the awkward photo-I don’t have a full length mirror so had to stand on a step stool in my bathroom.

I had read reviews before buying and opted to choose the smaller size than I have been buying for jeans with these (I have been getting size 8). If height helps to know too when comparing sizes to buy, I am 5’4”.

These jeans are very comfortable. The fabric is a 4-way stretch fabric that springs back into shape relatively quickly. The length is good (31" inseam), but I'd prefer them slightly longer. If you wear your shirt out covering the fake fly, no one would know that the jeans are pull on pants. The jeans have roomy back pockets and the front pockets are deep enough to be useful. The front pockets are lined with a soft lightly stretchy fabric. The pants did not shrink when washed in cold water and air dried. The fabric is a little lighter than regular denim, but still feels tough enough for jeans. The seams are overlocked and then coverstitched with mustard colored thread. The tops of the back pockets have reinforcements at the top edges to reduce the chance of them ripping out. The hem was overlocked, folded up once and coverstitched with the same thread.

Written by my wife: I am 5 ft tall, 130 lbs, and I was going to order these jeans in size medium until I read that they ran large. I’m glad I ordered size small because they fit me well with a tiny bit of wiggle room. They are pretty comfortable because of the wide leg. The dark color looks very nice in person and I didn’t notice an odor from the dye. I’ll have to get used to the pleats in the front. I don’t usually wear pleated pants, especially jeans, but I can’t say I hate it…it might grow on me. They are a tad long on me, but I’m petite and that’s expected. I think with the right shoes they’ll be fine. I had no issues with washing, and I hung them to dry, but I might try putting them in the dryer next time to see if they’ll shrink up a bit.

These are amazing! I was hesitant to buy with so few reviews, but I LOVE them. Comfy, stretchy belly. The jean material has stretch and is comfy but isn’t too stretchy to the point where it compromises a cute shape. Flattering in the butt and thighs. I wanted wide-leg jeans (I still wanna feel cute and stylish!), but many of them are too long for me. These are perfect. I’m 5’4”, so they would be too short for someone on the taller side. I’m 25 weeks and almost 170 lb now and I got a medium (I am a size 10 pre-pregnancy). I’m hoping my review and pictures helps other pregnant ladies who wanna be cute and comfy!

I ordered these jeans, not knowing what to expect and not really having high hopes for pull on jeans. I also tend to have issues with "high waisted" pants. Being 5'3", a lot of high rise jeans come up WAY too high and look terrible on me. When I first saw these, they kind of reminded me of maternity jeans, but I tried them on anyway. Wow! Was I pleasantly surprised! They are actually way cuter than I thought they would be. The XS fits perfectly. I got the standard size, and I probably should have gotten the petite/short size, but now I have jeans that look good with my platform sandals. I think I will buy another pair in a different color in the short size to wear with sneakers. I like that if you fold the waistband down, they are suddenly mid rise jeans, so there are two ways to wear them. These are going to be my new travel jeans. How perfect will they be for air travel with no zippers, buttons, or belts to worry about as you go through security? These also looked great after throwing in the washer and dryer, so no special care was needed. Overall, a great pair of jeans to add to your wardrobe!

I love these capris from Lee! The size 10 fits me perfectly. I’m 5’5” and 147. I live in Florida. This color is perfect! It’s funny, when I lived in NY, I only wore black. The longer I live in Florida, the more color I wear. This is my first purchase of colored capris (beyond Navy or Army Green). I absolutely love them! They just seem cheerful & happy! The length is perfect for my height. I like that the pockets are large & deep. They have a ton of stretch to them, which is perfect for my near 50 y/o figure. These capris are soft & thin enough to keep me cool. They definitely don’t help me in the butt department, but that’s probably more my figure & not the pants fault. I’ll definitely be purchasing these in additional colors!

The Metietila Pull-on Jeans for Women Straight Leg Elastic Waist Jeans are a medium weight jean made of 85% cotton, 10% polyester, and 5% elastane. They are machine washable, but be warned they do shrink. I laundered mine and though initially really baggy, they now fit better; however, they were the perfect length and are now too short. I’m only 5’3” so anyone taller than me will have cropped jeans. I like the pull on style with the elastic waist. The tie can be cinched, but it doesn’t really need it.

I got this tank top for my wife because we are going to Florida next month. She was looking for some basics that she can wear casually around town and at the beach. This one definitely hit the mark because it is simple yet chic and can be worn for many occasions depending on what you pair it with. She especially likes the henley style look with the wooden buttons. It adds an extra touch of style and gives her options to wear it buttoned all the way up or more relaxed with a couple buttons un-done. The tank top has a comfortable, light weight and loose fitting look that connects style and function. The only thing she would change is the length. It is a little long to wear untucked, but my wife is also short (only 5'2), so it may fall a little higher on another woman. It still looks great with a front tuck, or even full tuck, so it's not a deal breaker by any means. It's definitely worth the very affordable price and my wife will probably be ordering more in other colors.

I originally ordered this Lee Lux Comforte in the Navy in size 6. Amazon insisted 6 was the size for me. According to the size chart, it was close. I’m 5‘6“ tall. I weigh 135 to 140, waist is 29. hips are 38. I am also a little bit long-waisted. The waist of the 6 was OK. It was firm with not much give. However, the 6 was quite snug through the hips and thighs, and it made the pockets gap. I opted to return the 6 because these will primarily be for work and they were just slightly too small everywhere. I ordered the same slacks in size 8. The waist is still OK in the 8 a little bigger and a little looser, but I’ll still be able to wear them after Thanksgiving dinner. There is more room in the hips, but they still hug the top of the hip nicely before they fall to the wide leg. The leg in the 8 is loose at on my thighs, but the 6 was tight on my thighs. They are a bit longer. Unfortunately, they still make a funny wrinkle at the groin. This happens to me sometimes because I am slightly long-waisted. Because these are on sale for $16 and I really need navy slacks, I’m going to keep the 8. For anyone with a waist over 29, or hips more than 36, or generous thighs, or a long-waist, I think the 8 is a better choice. The fabric is not thin. It is a nice weight and it will be good for work. It might be warm on hot days.

I weigh 165ish, am 5'3", measuring 36DD-32-42, and wear a 12 in AE super high-waisted jeans. I bought these jeans in a size L. There's nothing technically wrong with the pants. They fit pretty well, minimal pinching at the waist, even with my belly. They're supposed to be ankle jeans, but if you want a length like the ones the model is wearing, you'll need to be a few inches taller than me, or have at least a 30-32 inch inseam. These pants touched the floor, and I have a 27 inch inseam, so I'd guess the inseam on a size L is at least 28 inches. Speaking of seams. The seams on the side of the pant leg start at the normal spot up at the top of the legs, and wind around to the front of the pant and end mid foot (pic included). I'm not sure what the purpose of that is. All the flare is in the back of the pant. Otherwise, they seem like a decent quality pant with the right amount of stretch to be comfortable. I also like the dark wash. I'm just not sure I like the side seams.

I almost didn't choose these VEAARDAMYE Women's Bootcut Yoga Pants, because I was trying to imagine the white stitching that shows on the model's pants. Upon arrival, I was eager to open the package to see how it looks. Thank goodness that there is no white stitching in this army green! They have that jean-like look that you could actually get away with wearing them to the office while feeling like you're in a pair of lounge around yoga pants. They're just that comfortable! These yoga pants are durably constructed with buttery soft and smooth fabric that's impeccably stitched. They snugly but gently hug your curves while allowing full range of motion, because they're pretty stretchy. The fabric is not too thin (not see-through at all) or too thick. I'm grateful for the high waist with a good amount of compression that slightly controls and smooths the silhouette. I feel great wearing them! I appreciate the 4 functional pockets. They are big enough for your phone and other essentials. These bootcut yoga pants are stylish and versatile, making them suitable for various occasions from workouts to casual outings to the office. I really think these yoga pants are flattering for many body types while dressed up or down by pairing them with various styles of tops and footwear for a fashionable look. I'm 5' 7.5" and the 30.5" inseam is the ideal length as the bottom hem falls perfectly on top of my feet. If you're searching for yoga pants that excel in comfort, versatility, and style at a fantastic value, I highly recommend these VEAARDAMYE Women's Bootcut Yoga Pants.

These Capri jeans from luvamia are well made. The fabric is 71% cotton, 27.5% polyester, and 1.5% elastane. They are soft and comfortable to wear. They have a good amount of stretch that makes them comfy for all day wearing, whether active or lounging around. The pants have 5 pockets, 2 back, 2 front, and the small pocket that I call a watch pocket. I like that the small pocket is tilted and not vertically straight. It adds a bit of style, in my opinion. I also like the bottom hem that looks like it's hemmed, but has a raw edge at the very bottom. The button and zipper work smoothly. The classic blue color looks good. I did not find any flaws in the sewing or material. I ordered this pair of Capri jeans in a size XL, which is my normal pants size. The waist and hips fit me very comfortably. The legs turned out to be a little wider than I expected from how they looked on the product page's models. I had the impression from the look on the models that they were almost like a skinny fit. That is my reason for the 4 star rating. By the way, each leg of the pants is actually the same length. They do not look like it in my photos, because I have one leg that is longer than the other, lol. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. Over all, these Capri jeans are good quality and I will recommend them. The luvamia brand is a favorite one for me to get comfortable, easy to wear jeans. The current price (12-17-2025) of $19.99 is a good value. There is also a coupon available at this time that will reduce the price to a great value! They have several other colors to choose from.

These Capri jeans from luvamia are well made. The fabric is 71% cotton, 27.5% polyester, and 1.5% elastane. They are soft and comfortable to wear. They have a good amount of stretch that makes them comfy for all day wearing, whether active or lounging around. The pants have 5 pockets, 2 back, 2 front, and the small pocket that I call a watch pocket. I like that the small pocket is tilted and not vertically straight. It adds a bit of style, in my opinion. I also like the bottom hem that looks like it's hemmed, but has a raw edge at the very bottom. The button and zipper work smoothly. The classic blue color looks good. I did not find any flaws in the sewing or material. I ordered this pair of Capri jeans in a size XL, which is my normal pants size. The waist and hips fit me very comfortably. The legs turned out to be a little wider than I expected from how they looked on the product page's models. I had the impression from the look on the models that they were almost like a skinny fit. That is my reason for the 4 star rating. By the way, each leg of the pants is actually the same length. They do not look like it in my photos, because I have one leg that is longer than the other, lol. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. Over all, these Capri jeans are good quality and I will recommend them. The luvamia brand is a favorite one for me to get comfortable, easy to wear jeans. The current price (12-17-2025) of $19.99 is a good value. There is also a coupon available at this time that will reduce the price to a great value! They have several other colors to choose from.

I ordered the indigo colored AVAMO stretchy jeans in a size XXL. I normally wear a size 18-20 or 2X in women’s plus size clothing. These jeggings fit me perfectly. The length was good and the hem sits right at my ankle. For reference I’m 5’6.5” tall and have a 30 inch inseam. I like the super dark indigo color of the pants and I was surprised that none of the color bled when I washed them. They also didn’t shrink when dried on high heat. The pictures I shared are after laundering the pants. The stitching looks great and I can tell that they are a quality made pair of jeggings. I appreciate that the pockets are all real pockets. I hate when pants have non-functional pockets that are just for looks. I carried my phone in my back pocket and it stayed just fine. The fabric is soft and stretchy, making them comfortable to wear. I wore these during a day of sightseeing and had no issues with them falling down, stretching out, or chafing. Overall I love these pants. The only reason for 4 stars out of 5 is because I feel the price is a little steep for jeggings. Although, I would buy another pair of they go on sale.

If you’re in between sizes or sometimes a 4 other times a 6, size down as others have said. The fabric is super giving and will stretch. The jeans are very light weight too, which is great for comfort and warmer weather. I ordered a 4 and they’re almost too big. I’m 125 lbs and 5”4”. Just received and so far I’m happy, but haven’t washed them yet.

Update: I dropped a star because these collect lint like nobody’s business and I have to spend almost 10 minutes de-linting whenever I wear them. The second thing is that the back pocket is too skinny. I put a Mac mini in my back pocket at work and these are the first jeans it hasn’t fit in. Original review: I wouldn’t necessarily say these jeans are “soft”; it’s more that they are thin (and a little stretchy). They aren’t the thick, rough work pants some jeans feel like. I’m 5’6 and between a size 16 and 18. These size 16 jeans fit very comfortably in the waist and tummy, snug around the thighs, and then long enough to need hemmed at the bottom. They are very wide at the bottom, almost giving bell bottoms. Other than that, the fit is pretty typical to size 16 jeans I own from other brands. Everything is double stitched and finished off properly with backstitching. I don’t expect these jeans to fall apart at all.

I have to admit I’m a Luvamia fan girl. I have SOOOOO many things from them. The only thing I don’t love is that sizing can be a little unpredictable What I have found it’s this: if you’re ordering something standard - regular shorts or regular blue jeans, get your regular size. They may be a little making at first but they loosen up quickly. If you are ordering anything “specialty” like colored jeans, striped, outfits, overalls, etc, then you should size up a little. For some reason this patterns run a little smaller. I ordered these in a medium which I knew would be a little small, but I was also having a tummy tuck in a couple days so I knew it would be worth it. As you can tell, I wore these pre-surgery. I am wearing a medium on my 5’5” 169# body. They would have stretched out enough while wearing them for them to be comfortable, though I would have wanted to wear a top over the abdomen to hide that area. Luvamia products last forever and wash up beautifully in your regular washer and dryer. These pink jeans are so cute - they remind me of an old school “beach comber” kind of look. I’m super excited to wear them and will provide an update on their look once I’m fully recovered and can try them back on again.

Right out of the packaging, the snap came off of these jeans and I had to replace it. The zipper seems to not be of the best quality either. That said, they are very flattering on and look really cute! They have lots of stretch and aren't too thick or too thin. I am 5'10" tall 180 pounds and the XL fits perfectly. The are very comfortable and at this price point, I didn't expect them to be even as good as they are!

My usual size is borderline between medium and large – depending on the cut. According to the manufacturer size chart, I ordered the medium size, and it was REALLY large. Returned it and re-ordered the small size, which fits perfectly now. I love the shade of Navy blue which is really close to the ad image. The waffle knit texture is not heavy or stiff, just nice and comfortable for colder weather. The henley neckline makes this shirt a little more versatile, lifting it a notch above an average sweat or tee shirt, and the longer, rounded, tail makes it perfect to wear with leggings – at least on my 5’3” frame. With the great number of color choices and a very reasonable MSRP for the overall quality of this shirt, I can definitely recommend this ** ANRABESS Women's Long Sleeve Waffle Knit Shirt**.

I love this shirt! It is so soft and comfortable. My bust is 47.5 inches, hips 49 inches and I chose the XL. However, I received a 2XL which actually worked out great. I don't like tight shirts and the 2XL was not too big, it was just right. I have worn it to work where I have to reach and bend all the time. I can bend over and even with the V-neck, my bust stays covered. The longer length is great because it still keeps my backside covered when reaching up. I have noticed that sometimes my other loose-fit shirts fit fine when walking around, but when I have to bend over to reach to the floor, the neckline exposes my décolletage. In the 2 weeks I have had this shirt, I have worn it 3-4 times and have washed it with no problem. I usually do not dry my favorite clothes all the way just in case they shrink. However, the last time, I forgot to take it out of the dryer but it did not shrink at all. I spilt mustard on the shirt. Luckily, I was at home and was able to rinse and prewash it right away. The stain came out with no damage to the shirt. As a Vine member, I only give 5 stars if I know that I would purchase the item. I am DEFINITELY buying some of the other colors! Note: I took the pictures soon after I opened the package and the 'line' across my bust is just a crease from the way the shirt was folded.

Took a chance and I love these. Fits on the hip and loose everywhere else as it should be! This is pretty relaxed, so if you like a snugger fit in the waist, then size down. I’m 5’2” and 125lbs and got a 27 short (US 4 Short), but could probably do a 2 short to be honest (historically I do recall Aeropostale running a little larger than other brands). I love the Sassafras color wash, it’s not too dark, but a classic deeper indigo, exactly what I hoped for. The tag does say 100% cotton, but there is stretch to the fabric. It’s not a stiff denim, but not a very thin one either, so it’s just right in my opinion.

These bootcut yoga pants are comfortable and best of all, the right length. I love that they have the choices of different lengths according to one's height! I'm 5' 4" tall and often pants are too long for me. They are made of a lightweight polyester/spandex blend. The fabric is thin but strong, and in no way see through. They are soft and easy to slip into. I did not find any flaws with the stitching or crooked seams. The army green color is true to what it looked like on my laptop screen. They have two pockets on the front and two on the back, just like a pair of jeans would have and they are full sized. I ordered this pair in a size XL, 30.5", Height: 5' 4" - 5' 7" and they fit me quiet comfortably. With the polyester/spandex blend for fabric content, they launder easily in a washing machine with cold water and tumble dry on low heat. That's another good thing I like about them is the ease of laundering them. I like this pair of yoga pants for the comfort and breathability they have so I do not overheat while wearing them. They will move in whatever direction you need to go without pinching or binding. I'm happy with them and definitely can recommend them. There are several colors, sizes, and lengths to choose from. As for the price, I feel it is a fair value for the quality and size choices.

I tried both the 0 and 00 sizes but neither one worked for me. I’m 5’4” and 112lbs. My waist is 24 inches. The 0 was way too long and too loose in the waist. I tried the 00, which was perfect in length but was still too loose in the waist, especially since the butt was more form-fitting in the smaller size. When I sat in the 00, the waistband rode up and gapped a lot in the back. I loved the color, style, and material of these jeans so I was very bummed I could not find a size that fits.

Beautiful and very soft tunic length with rich looking tortoiseshell type buttons is very flattering on the body. The L size shirt fits me and I’m 5’6” and 155 lbs. it extends down to upper thigh and covers crotch area-good for leggings. It’s heavier than a T-shirt and more like a lightweight sweater that may be worn in fall/spring or winter. The top has a nice stretchy fabric, room for layering and I recommend using the size from the company’s size chart. One could wear this top alone or wear in the winter for extra warmth as a layering piece over a button down long sleeve shirt with a collar or a regular long sleeve t-shirt. The buttons are placed in a way that is slenderizing to one’s body especially if one has a full bust and stomach bulging.

I recently ordered a pair of stretchy jeans that have a leggings-style fit, and I have to say they're incredibly comfortable. They really flatter my figure and offer me both comfort and freedom of movement, plus the high waist provides excellent tummy control. While they’re fitted, they aren’t too tight, giving them more of a jeans look rather than that of leggings. The overall style is quite cute. Be sure to check the size chart before placing your order; I went with size XS based on my measurements. For reference, I’m 5'5" and weigh 135 pounds. The fit is perfect, the color is lovely, and the price is reasonable. I also appreciate that they have real pockets in the front and back, which are quite handy!

y'all...I'm pretty picky with my jeans, but was pleasantly surprised by these. They aren't luxury jeans, they aren't thick, and aren't going to be your sturdiest jeans...but they are going to make you feel good and they will be comfortable. They're not too tight, not too loose, not too anything. The waist is my favorite because it's a high waist without being unflattering in the girly regions (im talking no knuckles of the moose). Only warning I can give is if you're a tugger, again these aren't the sturdiest so they'll rip where you tug if not careful(see picture); But they're sturdy enough to enjoy and pretty forgiving. I'm actually going to purchase more since they're the only jeans I've enjoyed since I gained weight (currently 5'4, 180, with thick thighs and thickish booty. these are size 12). I plan to make more purchases as I lose the weight as well, to have a set on hand. Again, pleasantly surprised...and recommend if you're good with a basic, comfortable skinny jean. The color was just right as well (these are the medium wash[i will be trying others...to be continued...but yay for now]

The reviews are all true… you NEED these jeans. They’re as comfy as sweats or leggings but actually look like jeans. So many of the sweats jeggings don’t have actual pockets or the ones they have are just printed on. But these actually have pockets! They’re incredible. I’m 5’7, 135lbs and the small fits great. They are the perfect amount of wide leg/baggy without looking sloppy which is a line I find most baggy jeans to cross. I wore them to work all day and they didn’t stretch out or start sagging either. Love them. Seriously, just buy them if you’re considering… you will not regret it!

These wide leg pants are very well made and so comfortable. It’s got a nice stretch. I purchased in white and they are not see through which is a pleasant surprise! The white is a little off white but I’m good with that. I really like the front patch pockets and the back pockets are also placed well and flattering. They are wide leg but not baggy and high rise but not crazy high. High enough to smooth the mom pooch! For reference i am 5”2” 125 lbs. I know these are cropped but they end right at my ankle bone and i like this length for flats.i look forward to wearing them with some sandals or sneakers in the summer.

These jeans are super classy - especially with the taper. They are comfortable, and stretchy. The color is a bright dark denim. I want to be so happy with them! I'm petite, so I do expect most jeans to be a little long, which I can usually accommodate for with most wide-leg jeans wearing heels or planforms. These are a bit on the "longer than I can accommodate" side for me. They do seem about a standard inseam for those with average height. I ordered a small. I am 4'11", 118lbs, and I usually wear a size 2-4. These run fairly large on top of being stretchy. I would side down personally. The small seems to fit like that of a 6. Aside from that, they are adorable, and I would definitely recommend!

At 5foot, they are super long. However, when I pull them up to be high waisted (as they are) and add my 3-4 inch shoes, they are perfect and cute. They are comfortable, but I would not call them jeans, other than the look of them. I guess being short, these were not technically meant for me, but after adding the high shoes they are great. I did remove the string as it was just annoying and bulky. See my photos for without shoes, with shoes and as not high waisted.

There are several things I really love about these jeggings. First, they come in a 2-pack with faded blue and faded black, making it easy to pair them with all my longer shirts and sweaters. The colors are fantastic, with a stylish stonewashed look. The high waist is a big plus—no more muffin tops or exposing your belly or back when bending over. They also provide great tummy control by keeping everything covered. Being 5'6", I appreciate the long length. I can tuck them into boots or pair them with slip-ons for a polished look. They do gather slightly at the ankles, which I actually prefer. The sizing is spot on—I'm a true size 18, and the XX fits perfectly without being too tight or loose in any areas. When they arrived, there was a strange chemical smell, but a quick wash took care of it completely. The care tag is a bit confusing, as it says to tumble dry but also to hang to dry. I always hang my leggings because of the spandex, and these dried perfectly on the line with no shrinkage. At the current price of under $15 each with a coupon, these are an amazing value!

These jeans are definitely on the simple side — not super trendy, but if you’re looking for a basic wide-leg jean at a low price, they do the job. The legs are very wide, and the width stays the same from top to bottom, which gives them a boxy look. I’m 5'5", and they’re about 2–3 inches too long on me, so I’ll need to hem them or they’ll drag on the ground. The denim is a thicker, non-stretch material that feels decent quality for the price — not stiff, but not soft either. They’re not going to be my everyday go-to jeans, but they’ll work fine for casual wear or when I want something comfy and roomy. Overall, solid for the cost, just don’t expect a high-fashion fit.

I’m 5’5”, 138 lbs and ordered a size medium. It fits perfectly…when I put on my 2 inches wedge booties. As you can see from my pictures, they touch the ground without shoes and crinkle at the legs because my legs are not long enough, but as soon as I wear my boots, the pants look great! They’re very comfortable and stretchy. I love when jeans are stretchy! I want to get more in different colors but I don’t want to be limited in wearing only heeled shoes with these, and I’m not going to get them tailored. I think if you’re 5’7” or taller, these would be perfect for you with any height shoes.

I’m very happy with the find of these jeans. Even though they are zippered, they also have a thin line of elastic around the waist which makes them very comfortable. I usually do not like any jeans that have elastic in the waist, but these are oddly enough stylish with the elastic band. They are somewhat wide legged jeans which I usually do not like, but these aren’t so much that they feel cumbersome. I am 5’6” and weigh around 135 and the medium fit me very well. They come with a drawstring that I immediately removed just because I don’t care for them and I remove them from any slacks that I buy. If you are looking for a very comfortable, casual pair of jeans, I would highly recommend these.

These are nice stretch denim jeans, but they run quite big. I followed the size chart, where large is supposed to have a 29.1 inch waist. In fact, the waist is about 33 inches unstretched, and could stretch a bit further than that. The inseam is 31 inches. Do be aware that the bottom edge of these is unhemmed--just a plain cut that will unravel over time for that torn jean look.

These jeans are well made, feel durable yet fairly soft. I'm 5'5" and the length was perfect for me. I do not care for the elastic, drawstring waistband. If you are on the chubby side, it is not flattering. I would want to wear a shirt that hangs over the waist band, but it's very textured so will show under other material. I'm guessing it looks adorable on someone slimmer. Priced under $37, I do think it's a good value if you like that type of drawstring waist.

These fit perfectly on my 5’3” frame. No hemming needed. I ended up ordering another pair in a different color. Size large and I’m 155 pounds.

5'7", 115 lobs, 28 w 30" inseam; bought small/long and they fit me perfectly. Love the color and slightly "worn" look - excellent fabric weight that is not at all transparent. Great stretch and very soft. Fabric is thicker like regular jeans but not heavy, and not thin like some jeggings. Opaque fabric for those of us who are modest, but cotton/poly/elastane blend is breathable and comfortable. NO SMELL which is super important to me. Labels says made in India and I've often gotten clothing made there that smells skunky. These only have typical new clothing smell and I always wash new clothes before wearing. Not sure what the front "tummy control" panel on the inside is all about. Seems rather pointless to me. Why only 4 stars rather than 5: The so-called tummy control is a stretchy nylon piece barely stitched into the inside of the jeans which actually shows through a little if you look closely. You can see by the photos that the hip area bells out a little bit. I remember back in the late 70s/early 80s slacks often had that feature in women's sizes and I called them saddle bags. I don't have much of a figure so this part "bags out" a tiny bit on me. There's also a bit of slack just below my rear end. Again, I don't have much of a butt so if you're a normal sized girl this may not be an issue. I'm hoping when I wash them this will smooth out a bit. Pockets in front and zipper placket are non-functional. Don't really see the point of that either. The back pockets are not very deep. Personally, I wouldn't feel comfortable putting a wallet or phone in them as they are too shallow and risk of falling out might be an issue. The dreaded toilet phone bath... Overall, they are very nice jeans, I'm happy with the purchase and would buy them again. In fact, I think I did buy a pair similar to this years ago on Amazon. Price for the quality is very good. Cleo the Grey is a very curious girl. And likes a selfie. Update: after washing, the jeans did not shrink at all. Still fit like a glove. Unfortunately the "saddlebag puckers" and under-bottom wrinkles did not go away. Leaving at 4 stars. Ladies with hips and butts will probably be better suited for this style.

They are as soft and comfortable as everyone desvribes them. I usually wear a 6 in Gap/ON and I ordered a 6. Perfect fit. The waist is not loose nor is it tight. It's actually perfect. If I had a bit more junk in the trunk, it would have enough space for it as well since there is definitely room and these pants stretch beautifully. I don't mind the pleats at the ankles, I actually think they make the pants look great either down or cuffed. I'm 5'7"ish with long legs. These are the perfect slouchy, but not too baggy, jeans.

I want to say I love these pants much more than I expected to! I was a little weary over the stretchy waistband, but let me tell you after seeing it on and wearing these pants it’s one of my favorite parts! I think it helps give shape and some room to adjust the sizing if you gain/lose a little weight! I love the look of the pockets and the wide leg is so fashionable. I’m 5ft tall so I do wear a heel with them to keep them from dragging, but if you are tall these would look so cute at ankle length so you can’t loose! Another thing that surprised me is that they are not see through! Super thick fabric! In the picture I have dark underwear on and you can’t tell! I wear these almost weekly since I dress up for work and love how versatile these pants are and how I can dress them up and down. HIGHLY recommend! I have received compliments and friends and coworkers are surprised about the affordability for how cute they are! Win win! I’m 5ft tall 145 lbs in the picture for reference.

I have been wanting a pair of barrel jeans for a while now and was getting close to giving up after buying and returning several pairs from Free People and Anthropologie. Free People's Good Luck barrel jeans are impossible to button up and unbutton and they are SO heavy so I gave up on those. Anthropologie's Kenna Mid-Rise Heritage Barrel Jeans by Pilcro vary in size so wildly from one style to the next I gave up on those too. I was just about to give up on the barrel jeans hunt all together until I saw these on Amazon and figured- what the heck. What do I have to lose? They are at LEAST less than three times the cost of the other brands and they also came in a lot of different colors/washes so I decided to give them one more try. I ordered them in size 4 Retro Blue and guess what??? I LOVE them! I am 5 feet 110 pounds and I usually wear a size 2- but the size 4 fit perfectly with just a little wiggle room in the waist. They hit just above my ankle and the fabric is so soft and not heavy at all. Great barrel shape without being too dramatic. I am going to order them in more colors!!!

I’m not a big fan of the muffin top look but the material is nice and soft, not too thin, perfect for warm weather. At 5’11” the length is good for me and the color is true to the picture. I have the orangey colored pair of these as well, but in a smaller size (14plus) and I don’t recall fighting a camel toe when I first put them on, like I did with these 🤷🏻‍♀️ I’m keeping them because I know they’ll stretch out enough to be comfortable and the price is a steal right now.

I always have trouble getting real blue jeans to fit properly as a larger woman. When I saw these, I knew I had to give them a try. Right out of the package, your mind thinks "blue jean." From the color to the fit, they are perfect! I don't think even when someone is standing beside you, they won't think you have on regular jeans. I ordered these in an XXL and was really happy with the fit. They aren’t too tight at all and have a nice amount of stretch that stays comfortable throughout the day. I was especially impressed with how well they fit the curve of my back and hip without leaving an open gap at the waist. The item said it was a straight leg legging, but I found it to have a slight flare at the foot. I loved it. For reference, I am 5'7" and found that the length of these pants actually extends past my foot. I’ll need to hem them or wear them with a bit of a heel, but overall they are a great, no-fuss pair of pants for the shop or everyday wear. Value for the money, these are a keeper and I'd get one in every color!

THESE ARE A MUST!!! I’m 56 yo and was looking for a less expensive version of cuffed jeans similar to KUT, AG, etc and I found them in this nice Jean. They are generous and very stretchy. They sit at s comfortable mid rise fit and provide some yummy compression. I am a size 12 but with the extra large for a looser fit not understanding they were so giving. Regardless they still look tailored and high end. The denim is a medium weight, and soft which is perfect for me as I have enough natural “bulk” of my own. The above characteristics make them uber comfortable while being trendy. These are a very versatile Jean and can be paired with heels, flats, tennis shoes, sandals as well as any matching vibe, top. I ordered the dark wash for a casual NYE outfit and the y were great. I am bavk to by the light wash as well. Buy them you won’t regret it. Reference: 5’8” 163

I really really like this oversized button down shirt. I’ve not owned a denim shirt like this before, but I absolutely love the fabric. It’s thick, soft, and is not stiff. This blouse fits me perfectly. I’m 5’8” and ordered the large. The length in the sleeves fit great, and the length that covers my bottom is the perfect length for wearing leggings. This is a super comfy shirt and the Ecru color is fabulous. This is a super nice shirt to pair with a silk or cotton scarf. This piece will accommodate just about any color scarf. Highly recommend!

I'm 5'5 and about 210. 45-42-51 are my measurements. I got a size 37 plus and I'm normally I'm an 18/20 plus. These come up just above my belly button and are a bit loose honestly, but I expected that since the measurements for for these says they're 43". The band/seams dont have a lot of stretch but the denim does so for my bigger girlies I'd focus on your waist measurements and inseams more than anything else, I tried to show that in one of the pictures. As far as measurements go, these jeans seem to be true to size. I could have gone a size smaller for a tighter fit, but these are comfortable how they are now so I'll just wear them with a belt. The fabric is soft but seems durable enough, it definitely don't feel like rough denim. It's comfortable and I absolutely amazing not going to kind wearing them all day. The color is also accurate, but as you can see in the pictures, it really does depend on lighting. The pictures with my legs showing were taken in sunlight. These are my second pair of Levi's and I expect them to last a long time so I'd say they're worth the price as well.

These jeans are super stylish, but you need to be really tall in order to wear them. The leg is so, so, so wide! The fit around the waist and butt area is good, comfortable, nice drawstring waist. There is nice stitching down the pant leg and the pants are nicely made, good quality denim and stitching. The entire leg is just so wide that I think I'd have to be at least 5'8" to wear these. Even if I had them shortened for my size, they would look ridiculous because the leg is so wide. If you're tall, these could be really cute.

I’m very pleased with the look and feel of this blouse in my photos. It has a ribbed texture, and the raised portions almost feel like velour. Between each rib, the material is thin and slightly see-through, though I didn’t notice this while wearing it—only when I held it in my hand. That’s not a dealbreaker for me. The blouse has a flattering cut, and the added buttons give it a polished look. For reference, I’m 5'8", 144 lbs, and wearing a medium. It does fit a bit baggy on me, so I would size down if I ordered another. This blouse is definitely something I’m adding to my work wardrobe since I work from home and like to dress nicely for daily meetings. Overall, I’m very happy with this shirt.

They are high waisted and have good tummy control. They have 2 front pockets and 2 back pockets. The front pockets are surprisingly wide and deep and I can easily fit my phone in there. These pants look great, feel great and are everything I look for in a pair of leggings. 5 out of 5 stars for sure!

I was skeptical about these because I don't love the slight kick flare to them but then I put together a pretty cute fit with them and for the price I think I will keep them. I probably could have gone with my usual size 30 but I sized up to a 31 and they fit comfortably with extra room in the thighs. Pros: comfortable, good length for a 5'4" girlie looking for a crop hitting just above the ankle, nice wash, value for money, quality denim with a moderate stretch and decent drape Cons: run a bit small, wish they were straight leg instead of slightly flared

I'm pleasantly surprised with these jeans. They run slightly small but they have a lot of stretch so that's probably for the best. I think they are curve hugging in a flattering way. I am a pear-shaped size 10. They arrived a little wrinkled but other than that they look really nice. The patch pockets are a nice touch. They're just slightly cropped on me, and I have about a 28" inseam. I wasn't sure about the oversized cuff trend at first but for a fairly reasonable price I'm enjoying the look.

I love these wide leg baggy drawstring loose fit jeans! I am 5' 2" and 120 pounds. I ordered the size 6. They are a great loose fit, so I barely know I have them on. The length falls just below my feet and is perfect with sneakers or sandals, so they don't drag on the ground. They are so comfortable it is like wearing jogging pants. Though, they are denim jeans with an adorable style you can wear to go out. They can be dressed down with a t-shirt or up with a blouse, cardigan or blazer. The draw string allows me to adjust the waist to be a custom fit. The pockets are deep and are great to put my hands in, and also to store my cell phone and other items when I am working around the house and yard. The jeans have no odor at all. They are predominately cotton. So, they are authentic high quality denim jeans that are durable and hold up well to an active lifestyle and to washing to last many years to come. They have high quality stitching with a cute double seam that is very flattering. This is my new favorite pair of jeans to lounge and work around the house, as well as to make trips out to run errands. I am extremely happy with the SIDEFEEL Women's Wide Leg Baggy Jeans, and I would order from this company again.
Good quality, but very tight in the legs with very minimal stretch. Will have to return. A size up may be too big in the waist however. I'm 5'5 126 lbs pear shape. Wearing my normal size 27.

Just received the pants today, and I'm OBSESSED. These jeans are very close perfection in my opinion! For starters, I love the distressed look without having holes or looking TOO distressed. They look like something that would say Ralph Lauren on the label tbh. The fabric is soft (not a stiff denim), not too thin or too thick, and they have a nice amount of stretch to them. I'm usually a size 10 (but sometimes 12 depending on the brand). For reference, I'm 5'6" and 170 lbs. I ordered a size 12 because I don't like anything tight around my waist and I was concerned with them running small. The 12 fits nicely and I DO think that if I ordered a size 10, they may have pulled around the waist just enough to be able to see some of the white interior of the pockets (I hope that makes sense). The legs have just the right amount of "barrel" to them (not as barrel shaped as FP jeans). These jeans are PERFECT for anyone looking to try out the barrel jeans trend without committing to a full on barrel jean. I'd rate these jeans 10/10 and ended up ordering 2 more in other colors!!! WORTH EVERY PENNY!!!

This waffle knit shirt is really nice quality! I purchased a size medium in the color black. I’m 5’5”, 137 lbs and the medium fits me a bit oversized but I’m ok with that as I prefer that look/feel in my shirts. You could size down unless you have a large bust. The buttons are functional and cute! The inside of the shirt is insanely soft on my skin! The shirt has a rounded hem and would look cute with leggings (length hits below my bum) or jeans too! The shirt isn’t heavy so you are able totuck if you want to. It’s a nice weight material to add some warmth. It does have a bit of stretch to it. I absolutelyrecommend this brand and this shirt. I want more colors!

I ordered 36” inseam, color Blue, size L. I am 6’1” and 175 pounds. These fit WONDERFULLY and are so conformable. They hit the floor but don’t puddle which is just what I wanted. I don’t know how why they say 36” is for much taller women unless they want shorter pants - I do not want them any shorter. Or longer. They do stink and need to be aired out, like others have commented. They are lightweight and very stretchy, as others have said, too. One star off because I feel they are too expensive for the style (or lack of style), but there sadly is not a lot of options so they’re doable. They do seem good quality. I long for a high-waisted and/or tummy control version of these! I saw one comment calling out over-fading on the backside for this same color iirc. Mine are not overly faded at all, thankfully.

This pair of elastic waist with button/zipper fly and a drawstring are easily the most comfortable pair of jeans I own! The fabric is of a medium thickness and is quite soft feeling, inside and outside. The fabric has a good bit of stretch in it. Enough that I would say they could even be worn for lounge pants! They are well made and I found no flaws or loose threads when they arrived. There are 2 large/deep patch pocket on the front and 2 normal sized pockets on the back of them. The drawstring can be easily removed if it is not needed or wanted. They have belt loops for those who would like to add a belt to their attire. The bottom hems are a little wider than most, but I think that adds to the cute style of these jeans. The waist has sort of a ruffled look to it on the upper band part where the belt loops are. The elastic is a loose, easy feeling fit that does not bind or pinch at all. They sort of remind me of a Daisy Mae look, from the 'Lil Abner comics of old, except they are longer. I think the look makes them fun and the comfort makes them a favorite for me! I ordered them in a size XL, which is my regular size for pants of any type. They fit super comfy on me with perhaps a little room to spare due to the loose fitting elastic waistband. I usually never tie drawstrings and just let them hang, but this pair I think I will need that drawstring tied for the extra snugness. The length is also perfect for my shortness! This is a pair I will not need to wear heels or platforms, but can wear my "barefoot" type shoes with them. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. These are good quality made jeans and I highly recommend them. They are fun and so easy to wear for any occasion. The current price (12-26-2025) of $36.99 is a fair value in my opinion. I would buy these again, without hesitating.

I usually never tie drawstrings and just let them hang, but this pair I think I will need that drawstring tied for the extra snugness. The length is also perfect for my shortness! This is a pair I will not need to wear heels or platforms, but can wear my "barefoot" type shoes with them. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. I would buy these again, without hesitating.

Super comfy and stretchy. I ordered the 10 and it’s a 30” waist. Too big so I ordered the 8 and it’s a 29” (it says on the tag inside the pants). I could’ve even gone smaller but I like how they fit now. Only downside is that odd smell some pants come with from the factories.

These Vetinee barrel jeans looked so cute in the listing photos, and I really wanted them to work. Unfortunately, once I put them on, the fit just wasn’t flattering on my body (my husband immediately agreed), even though the sizing felt true to size. For reference, I ordered a size XL. The denim itself is a nice weight and the stretch is comfortable—honestly, it’s on the “very flexible” side, which helps because the fit is a little snug through the hips. I also like how the high waist sits. That said, the front pockets tend to gap open, which exposes the pocket lining and looks a little sloppy. Pocket-wise, the front pockets are well placed, and the back pockets are functional, but they sit lower than I expected. After washing, the top edges of the back pockets folded over, which I didn’t love (though everything else seemed to wash fine). For length reference, I’m 5’6” with long legs, and the 26” inseam hits around the tops of my shoes. It’s a little longer than what I’d consider truly cropped—more of an ankle-length fit on me. Also, I have noticed that the inseam seems very wide at the bottom, meaning that there’s a lot of fabric between my ankles. Overall, I think these could be really cute on the right body shape, but on me the barrel/balloon leg was a miss. If you’re considering them, I’d recommend thinking carefully about your proportions and how you like this silhouette to fit before ordering.

I haven't worn jeans in ten years. I had these in my cart for months and rly happy I decided to finally get them! They fit true to size. For reference, I’m 5’6”, 175lbs. 32 waist, 45 hips. Usually I’m a size 12/14, but I ordered these in 16 for a looser fit and glad I did! The front pleats are well placed for a flattering fit, especially if you’re curvier. The material is fairly thick, but also stretchy. Surprisingly comfortable! They hold up well in the wash & don’t shrink when dried. The rise hits at just the right spot — although the waist is a little baggy on me, so I have to wear a belt. Very pleased with the quality. I never spend more than $20 on pants, but these are worth the money for sure. 10/10 recommend. If you’re questioning getting them, do itttt:)

These jeans are cute and quite comfortable because of the slight stretch to the denim. And they're not too stretchy to the point that they feel stretched out by the end of the day. The style is cute, I love the pockets on the front and the seams above the back pockets, this adds a nice stylish touch. My one qualm with these jeans is that they are not full length as pictured in the listing. I am just under 5'6" and my legs are proportionate to my torso. I do not normally need to wear 'long' or 'tall' sized jeans. These could've definitely been longer. The cuff is sewn, so if I want to uncuff them to make them longer, I'll have to rip open the stitching, which isn't a huge stitch, but the pants could've just been a bit longer. I like the color and the fit overall. Again, they are comfortable too.

However, for $40, I won’t be able to keep them since I will need that money to keep shopping around for the look that I want. Cons: They run really small and they don’t fit like they do in the product photos. I normally wear an 8-10 in jeans, depending on the brand, and I sized up to a 12 because I wanted these to fit nice and loose like they did on the model in the first product photo. In person, they’re much shorter so I can’t roll them up at the ankle like I wanted to. I think the size 12 fits me like most size 10 jeans normally would. A little loose in the waste with some wiggle room. Pros: would be cute on someone shorter and very skinny, the material seems like decent quality and there is a comfortable amount of stretch to them If you want to achieve the look in the product photos, I would recommend trying to go up by 3-4 sizes. For reference, I am 5’5”, a pear shape with a short torso, and I was 165 lbs when this photo was taken.

These jeggings are a great find for the price. They are very comfortable and offer excellent value, especially for inexpensive jeggings. The fabric is soft and stretchy, making them easy to wear all day without feeling restrictive. The front pockets are a standout feature—they’re deep and actually usable, which is hard to find in jeggings. There are back pockets as well, though I do wish they were a bit larger so my phone would fit more comfortably. That said, the jeggings stay in place really well with no rolling or slipping, even with movement. They provide a flattering fit, smoothing the midsection and offering nice shaping overall. Length is another big plus. At 5'8", it can be difficult to find affordable jeggings that are long enough, but these fit well without looking cropped. The fabric isn’t very thick, but it’s breathable and comfortable, and still warm enough to wear year-round. Overall, these jeggings are comfortable, flattering, practical, and well worth the price. I would definitely recommend them for everyday wear.

I ordered these Sidefeel Women's Wide Leg Baggy Jeans in the (6, Dark Blue) version... and I like them, but they don't fit me exactly how I want them to yet. I sized down because I am on a weightloss journey, thinking that because they have an elastic waistband and wide legs that they would work. While they are passable, I will be letting my daughter wear these until I am ready. For reference, I am 5'5ish, 160ish pounds, 34D bust and 28" waist. Here's what I know about these baggy jeans: •Excellent quality garment, free of defects •Made of soft, stretchy, denim-like material that is medium weight •Elastic waistband with drawstring is extremely comfortable •The legs are baggy, but not super sloppy looking •Dark blue color is rich and vibrant •Versatile-- can be styled a variety of different ways •Wash up well •I choose to hang dry these and them fluff them in the dryer to remove the wrinkles Overall, these are fun jeans! At the time ordering they were a little less than $37-- because they are well made and on trend, I think they are a fantastic value for the money. MOM APPROVED! RECOMMEND!!!

I am surprised to find I love these jeans! Shopping for pants as a short, curvier (particularly in the thighs) woman can be difficult. I am also postpartum and finding jeans that fit and are comfortable is tricky at the moment. The elastic wasteband is fantastic. I ordered a medium since I am postpartum. I am 5'2" and 145 lbs (if I were my usual weight of 130-135 lbs I'd have ordered a small). The waist is very comfortable and the length is perfect. That said, the length may be short if you are average height or taller. My two gripes: the sewing of the elastic band in the front of the pants seems like a mistake -- the band ends in a different place on the right side and the left side. And the shoestring lace is just stylistically not something I like. It may work better with a lighter wash, but I'll likely remove the string and wear without.

I had low expectations for the price, but these are surprisingly flattering and comfortable. One caveat is that you need to size up. I usually wear a size 4 jeans so I ordered a size 6. They fit comfortably but are tighter in the waist and hips than my barrel jeans from Anthro. So if you want more of a baggy look at the top, you'll want to go up two sizes. I wish that I could convey to you how soft these bad boys are. You know when you have a pair of jeans that are getting almost threadbare in some places because they’re so worn in they don't even feel like the thick jeans material anymore? That’s what these feel like - only they aren’t threadbare, just soft. These don’t even feel like jeans to me at all. They’re stretchy from the top to your bottom, then they get loose and comfy from the legs down. If they feel a bit tight at first, just give it a minute, because they will conform to you. The fit is a little higher on the waist than I typically like, and I kept trying to pull them down more on my hips, but they’d just creep back up 😆. They have real pockets on the back AND front, which is nice because we sometimes have fake pockets on the front. There IS a fake zipper since these are pull on pants. I am 5’1,” about 120 and I ordered the XS. It’s possible that I should’ve ordered the small, but these still fit really well. They are a bit tighter on my bottom and around the thighs in the back than I’d like, but my husband thought they looked really good. When I ordered these, the petite wasn’t an option, nor was the dark color - both of which I would normally get. I purchased the blue, which is a bit lighter than any other jeans that I own, and which also is a bit more unforgiving in my opinion. However, I’m really glad I got them. I think once I get to my usual weight of about 107-110, I will, personally, feel more comfortable in them. They will still be a tad long, though, which I’m totally used to. 😁. However, if you are the same size as me, they will absolutely fit you - it’s more a “me” thing than a “not fitting” thing.

I wish that I could convey to you how soft these bad boys are. You know when you have a pair of jeans that are getting almost threadbare in some places because they’re so worn in they don't even feel like the thick jeans material anymore? That’s what these feel like - only they aren’t threadbare, just soft. These don’t even feel like jeans to me at all. They’re stretchy from the top to your bottom, then they get loose and comfy from the legs down. If they feel a bit tight at first, just give it a minute, because they will conform to you. The fit is a little higher on the waist than I typically like, and I kept trying to pull them down more on my hips, but they’d just creep back up 😆. They have real pockets on the back AND front, which is nice because we sometimes have fake pockets on the front. There IS a fake zipper since these are pull on pants. These jeggings are so comfortable! The fabric is heavier than most legging fabric, and has just the right amount of "give" to keep the leggings in place. Even after bending over, reaching, stretching, twisting - I didn't have to pull up or readjust the waistband. There is the typical jeans contrasting stitching along the pockets (2 rear, 2 side), the leg hems, and the fake zipper on the front. I machine washed and lined dried these with no issues. For reference, I am 5'4" and weigh about 150 lbs, ordered Large, and they are the right length and fit. Well made, comfortable, at a good price - very pleased with these jeggings.

These jeggings are a great deal for the 2 pack. They are super comfortable, and look cute too! They give fairly close to a "real jeans" appearance. The waistline and fake zipper area giveaways, but still close to the same look, just with more comfort. The length - is slightly long for me. For reference, I am 5'4". Other than that, the sizing is perfect. They are high waisted, so the waistband does not roll/slide down when moving around. Colors - are just as described/pictured. and the quality is good. The pockets are positioned appropriately and not too high like some others can be, which looks weird. Stitching for the most part, looks great, minus 1 loose thread but that wasn't too big a deal. Fabric - The medium weight/thickness fabric, is soft to touch, slightly stretchy and super comfortable to wear. I washed one time and did not notice any color bleeding (washed in cold water). Odor - There was a moderate odor when first opened, chemical-like, from the dye or textile probably. It went away after washing. Overall - this 2 pack is a good value, and these jeggings are comfortable, look decent too. I recommend trying them.

I liked how these looked online, but in person they definitely fit different. First, they are much longer than expected, I was thinking these would be slightly cropped. I am 5'7" so typically I have trouble finding pants long enough and when I'm actually looking for a cropped leg I get these that hit just at my ankle. The front pockets are so small that they are unusable, and look like they are on backwards. The back is not flattering. It is stretchy fabric but seems to suck in to the wrong curves. Wouldn't be able to size up because it'd then be too big in the waist, so I'm not sure what body type this is designed for, but mine isn't it!

This is a very nice top. Compliments first thing today from my clients! Highly recommend. I did order a medium for a little more room. I’m 5’2 124 weight.

This is my 7th pair of Sidefeel jeans so I'm clearly a fan, but I'm not as in love with this pair as I am with my other pairs. I usually wear a 12-14 and have gotten size 14/XL in Sidefeel jeans in the past, but sized up to a 16 in these (since I recently gained some weight) and they still feel snug in the hips. I opted for the dark blue color. They are made really well using nice material and the stitching is pretty perfect. The material is decently soft for jeans with a bit of stretch while still having a bit of thickness to it for durability. The length is great for me at 5'8" but might be a bit long for those who are more petite. The waist is comfortable and the drawstring is a nice accent. I love how the side seam crosses along the front for a touch of subtle style. The legs are super wide, reminiscent of the super flared jeans we wore in the late 90s/early 2000s. There are side pockets which are pretty roomy but none on the back which gives the jeans a dressier look, but I do love having back pockets. They laundered well on a regular wash cycle and drying on low heat. I didn't notice much shrinkage at all after laundering. Ultimately I think these are really well-made jeans that just don't look great on my current body. I think they'd look better if I was a bit smaller size. I will definitely wear these around the house because they're super comfy and hopefully once I get back to a lower weight these will look better on me.

I’ve got these skinny Jeans in size 8. At 5’1”, the size 8 fits true to the size chart, with the ankle-length cut hitting perfectly at my ankles. The dark blue color is true to the product images. The stitching is well-executed, with no loose threads. The fabric, a blend of 72% cotton-very soft, breathable, and comfortable with just the right amount of stretch and no weird smell. The designer front and back pockets add a unique, chic touch. Easy to care for with machine washing, these jeans are a practical, stylish choice for everyday wear or date nights. I paired them with a t-shirt for casual outings and a blazer for a more dressed-up look for a great price. Great value--a must have.

UPDATE: Size 6 fit great. Could maybe even do a 4 but I carry my weight in my waist so I'm sticking with the 6. These have a true wide, baggyish leg so if you're looking for a slim leg, keep looking. I hung out on these on a weene afternoon and they were super comfy. The black and the grey are barely indistinguishable - the black is a washed black, and the grey is pretty dark. I took a pic along with some of my other grey jeans. Original review: These are made of a thicker but still quite stretchy denim. I'm 5'6" 145-150 lbs and they're ankle length, hitting just a smidge above my ankle bones. The size 8 was a loose in the waist - I will probably return and reorder in a size 4 or 6 (probably 6) since they're likely to stretch out during the day. Waist is comfy though! The black is a washed black, not true black.

I recently purchased the Famnbro Womens Rhinestone Jeans Baggy Y2K Glitter Denim Pants In Large, and it’s quickly become my favorite pair of jeans. These jeans blends comfort and style effortlessly, making it perfect for casual outings, or even a cozy dinner date. 🖤 Fabric & Fit: Made from a soft blend of 90% Polyester, 10% Spandex, the material is soft, and lightweight with just the right amount of stretch. The Large fits true to size and offers a flattering silhouette without feeling tight. It’s loose enough to hide trouble spots but still gives shape thanks to stretch. I am 5’5, 160lbs, and they fit perfect! ✨ Design Details: These say they are baggy, but they are not, they are wider on the bottom. They have rhinestones, not glitter, which is a plus. These are high wasted so no worries about them sliding sown for those who have less hip 🛠️ Quality & Care: Jeans are well made and the rhinestones have not come apart. You must wash in gentle and inside out. 🌟 Final Thoughts: If you’re looking for a stylish, these comfortable jeans are that’s for fall and winter, these jeans are a winner. They arw flattering, versatile, and easy to wear—definitely worth adding to your seasonal wardrobe!
