The button and zipper fasteners work smoothly without any catches or stiffness. They have 2 front pockets and 2 on the back. All 4 pockets are roomy and functional. The bottom hem is an unfinished raw edge, which I also like. The Dark Blue color is nice and not overly dark, which I prefer. I ordered this pair of jeans in a size 16, which is my regular number size for jeans. They fit quite comfortably and do not pinch or feel tight around my waist or hips. There is enough stretchiness in the fabric, I may have been able to wear a size 14 comfortably. The length is a little too long for my 5' 4" height. I'm thinking with the raw bottom edge and they have the markings of a once upon a time hem, I will maybe cut them off one line up on those markings. For reference: along with the 5' 4" height, I have a 33-ish waist, 44" hips and weigh 182 lbs. Overall, these jeans are good quality, both in materials and sewing, so I will recommend them. A caution for shorter persons as they are longish. The stretchy fabric and nice looking style make them a good addition to my collection of jeans. The price when I place my order (1-5-2026) of $36.99 is a good value for a pair of jeans, in my opinion.

I really like these pants. At first I wasn't sure because the brown is a super unique color. It is brown that has a hint of mustard to it. They are super faded and have a pretty cool hem. These were a huge hit with my students. My middle school female students absolutely loved them and made me tell them where I got them. These fit really well, too. They really do match up to the size chart in the item description. The inseam was just perfect for my 5'6" height -- I don't have long legs, but they are slightly longer than most 5'6" persons. Take that into account. They are pretty comfortable on, and I think they'll get even more comfortable as I break them in and they go through the wash cycle. They also look more expensive than their $33.99 (at time of review) price tag. Overall, I'm super pleased with my choice to get these jeans.

Democracy Women's Petite Ab Solution Jegging I liked that these came in petite size. Jeans are always way too long on me as I am 5’1. I weigh 160 and size 10 fit perfect. The length was perfect. For the price, I’m not impressed and won’t keep. The color is way darker than pictured also. For almost $80 I had higher expectations for these jeans. They’re just ehh.

Democracy Women's Petite Ab Solution Jegging I love these jeans! Very flattering and soooooo comfortable! They are worth the money and I will be ordering different colors. I'm 5"3, 120 pounds and wearing a size 2 petite.

Democracy Women's Petite Ab Solution Jegging I love these jeans! Very flattering and soooooo comfortable! They are worth the money and I will be ordering different colors. I'm 5"3, 120 pounds and wearing a size 2 petite.

Democracy Women's Petite Ab Solution Jegging I love these jeans! Very flattering and soooooo comfortable! They are worth the money and I will be ordering different colors. I'm 5"3, 120 pounds and wearing a size 2 petite.

Democracy Women's Petite Ab Solution Jegging I am 5' 3" and curvy with thicker thighs. These are 14 petites and fit perfectly. Very soft, comfortable and don't slip. I love the Democracy jeans. These are my 3rd pair and I plan to replace all of my jeans with the Democracy brand.

While these are flattering and stretchy and super cute, get a size or 2 down. I got a L and could have easily fit into a S or M. They stretch and form to your body, which is always a plus. The quality of the material is great. Wash them in Cold. I have not had any fading issues at all. Though I have to wear a belt with them, the flare at the bottom is fun and stylish. Pair these with some platforms for a fun look.The length is great for my height at 5'5" and the small details on the overall design brings out a unique look.They're extremely comfortable and can be worn for hours. They do loosen up over time however, so I have had to wash them each time I've worn them, as the size is far too big on me.Which isn't a bad thing, but hopefully this review helps others size down and get the actual fit and look these pants are meant to show off.

I decided to go with a size Medium in these jeans because my measurements were between the Medium and Large size. I normally wear size 8 and was hesitant to purchase jeans sold in S, M, L size groupings because that seemed too general. However, the material is stretchy so they can accommodate a variety of sizes. Overall, I think they fit well. I'm 5'3" and barefoot in the picture. The jeans do drag a little on the ground, even after I put on shoes, but I am on the shorter side so that's to be expected. After a full day of wear, the stretch of the fabric does loosen, as common with stretchy jeans. However, I'm pleased to i did not need a belt to keep them up! These very much remind me of my favorite flare jeans from the late 90's. They are very comfortable! Why 4 stars? The laundry care instructions. Apparently they should be washed in the gentle cycle and NOT put in the dryer?! When I buy jeans, I do not expect to be line-drying them. I expect to wash & dry them on a regular cycle with the rest of my clothes.

Roswear Flare Jeans for Women Mid Rise Stretchy Skinny Curvy Denim Pants are wonderful jeans for going out or to wear on a casual work day. The flare is not so much as to look like a boot cut but just enough to give it some style. The exterior pocket design and stitching add style and function to the pants. They are zipper button closure with a quality zipper. The seams are stitched well and the leg is hemmed at the cuff. the fabric has some stretch to it so do not fear the size. Size is true to the chart, no need to order up. Length is designed for heels or ankle boots. Model is wearing 3.5 inch heels in the photos. Fabric care is simple machine wash and tumble dry medium. These pants will be a great addition to the fall wardrobe this year. Model 5’3” / 11b lbs / size 4

I've had really good luck with the Roswell brand in the past and this time is no different. I'm really loving these jeans! They have a great retro vibe but with a nice stretch that often wasn't present in the early days of this style! Based on the size chart, I ordered a large in keeping with my typical size 12. The fit is perfect with the right amount of stretch in places where I need it without being frumpy and baggy everywhere else. The fabric is medium weight...perhaps a bit on the lighter side and is extremely comfortable. Aside from the comfort and the look, one of my favorite things is that these jeans can be worn on my 5'4" frame without requiring heels. This might not be a great feature for taller women but if you're like me and often feel stuck between petite and regular length, these are perfect! I would definitely recommend!

These GRAPENT barrel jeans are such a great everyday staple. I typically wear a size 10 in pants, but I followed the size guidance and ordered a Large and it fit perfectly. For reference, I am 5’2” and around 155 lbs, and the Large gave me a relaxed and comfortable fit without feeling tight anywhere. Even though these are technically cropped with a 26 inch inseam, on me they hit right at the ankle, which I absolutely love. As a short girly, I usually struggle with jeans being too long, but these worked out beautifully with no hemming needed. The barrel silhouette gives a trendy shape while still feeling easy and wearable. I wore these all day and they were incredibly comfortable from morning to night. There is lots of room through the legs, the high waist feels secure, and the stretch makes them easy to move in. They are roomy without looking sloppy, and the patch pockets add both function and style. If you are petite and usually around a size 10, the Large was spot on for me. Super comfortable, flattering, and perfect ankle length. Summary Highlight True to size when following size chart Size Large fits perfect for size 10 Great for petite girls 5’2” Hits right at the ankle All day comfort with stretch Roomy barrel silhouette Functional patch pockets Trendy but very wearable

Loving these barrel jeans! They have great structure to them so they keep their barrel shape- even after washing! Just make sure you don’t put them in the dryer. I ordered the black size M and they are a great true black. I’m usually between size S and M but I sized up for these and at first, I was glad I did but after wearing them for a whole day, they’ve loosened up. I wish I would have ordered size S. They are a great length too. I’m 5’5” and they hit right at or a little above the ankle. I love the front pocket details and the waist cinches in just slightly so it gives you a great shape. The only issue I’ve had is the black dye rubs off on your skin a little bit if you don’t wash them first. I haven’t had that issue since I washed them. Great price too! Highly recommend.

I have been wanting to try the barrel Lake jeans for sometime now. Thought these would be a good one to try. They are made of a lightweight denim which is very stretchy and allows me extra room for my thicker waist. The waistband is soft so they are comfortable to sit down in, but is stiff enough that it doesn’t fold over. The barrel legs are not exaggerated, they just barrel out a little bit so they give you some extra legroom without looking ridiculous. They are tapered at the ankles, but not so much that you can’t pull them up if you have to. The color was as described, and I would say these are true to fit. I am not a huge fan of the patch pockets, but I knew that was the style when I bought them and they are very roomy. I am 5 foot four and they come just below my ankles. If you have long legs, they may look a bit cropped. For me, I found the fit very flattering.

I’m 5’2, and usually a size 6-8. I got these in a medium and they are very comfortable. The style is very “in” right now, but I did dock a star because they aren’t the most flattering jeans in the world. They gave me a serious mom butt, and they smell weird/bad. That is obviously something to be remedied with washing but it is a very strong smell. So I’m giving 4 stars because overall I do like them. I’d say $35 is a reasonable price for these.

These barrel jeans are super stretchy and baggy. I probably could have sized down but still like the look of the current size. They have 4 pockets. I like that the front pockets and the back pockets are the same. It makes it easier to slide your phone in your front pockets and also safer! The material of the jeans seem on the lighter side which is probably due to the stretchy factor but they feel soft. This isn't a big deal for me BUT it's important to note that they are not a heavy pair of jeans if you're packing them to travel. It's also important to note that if you are short, I'm 5'3, then these pants hit at a weird length so you'll have to roll them up or wear heels to make the length look proper. All and all, I think these are a solid pair of jeans that will be good for traveling to help with weight limits.

user_comment: I really love the fit of these jeans. They look trendy yet comfortable and effortless at the same time. The high waist and barrel style give them a modern, fashion-forward look, and the 26" inseam hits nicely above the ankle, which makes the overall silhouette look flattering. The fabric feels breathable and soft. The cotton blend with stretch makes them comfortable enough to wear for long hours, whether at work or out for the day. They don’t feel stiff like some denim can, which I really appreciate. The size chart was very in choosing the right size, and I found them to be true to size. That always makes online shopping much easier and less stressful. The only thing I personally wish was different is the front patch pockets. I would have preferred side pockets or just back pockets for a cleaner look. That said, the front pockets do add a unique style statement and may appeal to those who like that design detail. Overall, these are great relaxed-fit jeans that balance comfort and style. Perfect for work, casual outings, or everyday wear.

My 20 yr old daughter (5’ 6” tall, 170 lbs) loves these pants, and they’ve quickly become one of her most worn pieces. She says they’re extremely comfortable, with a soft feel and enough stretch to move easily throughout a busy day, whether she’s out shopping, running errands, or just spending time around town. The fit is true to size and proportioned well, giving a good fashionable shape without feeling tight or restrictive. They’ve maintained their structure while still offering flexibility, which makes them practical for regular use. The construction appears solid, with clean stitching and no loose threads. They’re machine washable and have held up well, staying relatively wrinkle-free after laundering. The fabric feels breathable and comfortable even during longer wear. The pockets are functional and comfortably positioned, easily holding everyday essentials. From both a style and practicality standpoint, they offer a modern look with dependable comfort, making them a strong value for the price.

SHOCKED how nice these soft jeans feel straight out of the package. I usually hate jeans, and find it hard to get a decent fit, but these are wide leg, but not TOO wide, long enough to style with short booties, and stretchy without looking too sloppy. So, I consider these a win for my closet and I will wear them very casually. I bought the dark wash in a size Medium, I am 5'5" and weigh ~ 148 and they fit nicely. They have pockets that are useable and back pockets as well. I don't usually wear belts, but they do have real belt loops so you certainly can wear a belt. I don't find them too long, but just right. Nice quality for the price. I do not consider them pajama or sweatpant feel, no fleece or inner french terry and thankfully, NO weird smells or odors. I can recommend if you want a casual pair of pull up jeans to head out the door.

I am 5 foot two and 180 lbs. I purchased size 12. Once they warm up a little they get a little bit too big so maybe a size 10 would have been better. The quality is great. I love the style, I wish I had gotten a ten It might’ve been hard to get into at it first, but they do stretch.

Got as a gift. My sister is 5'2" and they were way too long. She doesn't mind hemming them since they have a nice fit otherwise-- stretchy, comfortable and with usable pockets. She also is willing to line dry them but others might find that a drawback. Care instructions say "do not tumble dry".

These pants are very stretchy, they have a Jean look and feel, the waist is true to size, but boy the length. The length of the legs is so long you have to be at least 6 feet tall so they don’t drag on the ground. I really don’t understand why there aren’t length choices if the small had this long of legs. Disappointed because at 5’ tall I really have no idea what to do with all the excess fabric dragging on the ground.

These are my absolute favorite pair of jeans. They look like standard brands but have a lot of stretch and an elastic waistband. They are incredibly flattering but have the comfort of soft leggings. They are easy to wash and dry and come out of the dryer without wrinkles. The dark denim does fade slightly but I think it add to the character of the jeans. The length is perfect on my 5’7” frame but I could see how they might require hemming if you are shorter. I am definitely going to try to find these in other colors.

Just buy these jeans! Seriously, buy them and thank me later. I've always preferred my jeans on a thinner and stretchier side. I love pull-on jeans, not just for their comfort and convenience but I don't like the zipper/button bulging under a tight shirt. These jeans are SOFT! The fabric is on a thinner side and very stretchy. They feel absolutely wonderful and are the ultimate comfort jeans. It's as if your favorite jeans and sweatpants had a baby. I am 5'7", 130 lbs and size small fits me well. They are on a loose side both around the hips and legs. They are true high waisted, wide leg jeans. I highly recommend them and will buy more colors.

These jeans have plenty of stretch but they are also stylish and create a nice silhouette. I am 5'2" with short legs so I would need to hem these if I didn't wear them with heels. Love the dark denim color and find it really elevates a "jeans look". I do think I could have sized down one and been fine so if you're in between sizes, I'd go with the lower size. They are quickly becoming one of my favorite wardrobe pieces.

These jeans are super cute and very comfortable overall. I love the wide-leg fit and the high waist — they’re flattering and easy to dress up or down. The elastic waist with the drawstring is a big plus and makes them easy to pull on, especially for casual days. The denim is lightweight and perfect for warmer weather. My only downsides: the back pockets are placed very far apart and sit a bit too much toward the sides, which looks a little off from behind. Also, aside from the waistband, the denim itself doesn’t have any stretch, so keep that in mind if you’re between sizes. Overall, still a great pair of trendy, comfy jeans, just wish the pocket placement and stretch were a little better. Note - I’m 5’2 135lbs and I went with a small. Pants fall right about my ankles. Waist fits ok. I think a small or medium would’ve been fine

I am extremely pleased with this pair of casual summer pants. I ordered the XXL in cream white, and the color is exactly as described—a soft, flattering white that feels fresh and versatile without being overly bright or stark. It pairs beautifully with both neutral and bold tops, making it a great staple piece for warm-weather styling. Add a pair of sneakers or sandals or a sweatshirt on a cool night and you have a great outfit! For reference, I typically wear a size 16/18, and the XXL provides a perfect fit. The silhouette is intentionally relaxed and slightly baggy, creating a comfortable, effortlessly casual look. The wide-leg design enhances the laid-back summer aesthetic while still looking polished and put-together. I am 5’6”, and the length is ideal—the hem falls just above my ankle, which is a very flattering cut. It offers a breezy feel without appearing cropped too short. The proportions feel thoughtfully designed, especially for someone in my height range. The overall construction of these pants is impressive. The fabric is strong and sturdy, giving the garment structure and durability without feeling heavy. The stitching and finishing details appear well-executed, indicating quality craftsmanship. Despite the lightweight, summery style, the material does not feel thin or flimsy. Overall, these pants strike an excellent balance between comfort, fit, and quality. They are stylish, well-made, and perfect for casual summer wear. I would highly recommend them to anyone looking for a comfortable, wide-leg option that delivers both durability and a flattering fit.

The luvamia Wide Leg Jeans fit me, 5'4" tall, in a small length that hits my ankles. They're not too thick, like summer material, and they really bring out the natural texture of the jeans. However, the arrow part shown in the photo, just below the waist belt (as indicated by the arrow), has too many pleats, like shoulder pads, making my waist look plump. I wish they'd reduce the pleats around the waist. The overall length, pockets, and other details are fine.

user_comment: The fabric is nice and soft, perfect for the warmer days. I feel like they are true to size width-wise, but see the photo… I’m 5’9” and these pants end 2-3” above my ankles. If you’re a petite person, this might be your fit. Oh, and the fabric is white and thin (which again is a good thing), so make sure to wear nude color underwear. Otherwise great pants for the summer, relaxed fit, just not for taller girls.

My dimensions are 130 lbs, 5'3", waist 28-29", long torso, short legs and I ordered the medium in classic blue. I wear these pants when I feel like lounging around the house, but also want to be dressed for running errands. The inseam, of 26", has the hem landing above my ankles. The material is comprised of 90% Cotton and 10% Polyester and is lightweight, soft and thin denim. A composition that makes these pants ideal for Summer weather, or curling up on a couch with a blanket. Given that they contain Polyester which helps with de-wrinkling, I throw the pants in the dryer on cool air for 15 minutes to soften the wrinkles; the results are shown in my pictures. Usually denim can be tumbled dry, but the seller recommends only cold washes and air dry. The medium feels true to size, and are a loose, baggy fit. The pockets are deep and sewn into the material to lie flat against the garment which prevents them from adding weight to your waist or be visually distracting. The tie closure stays in place and adds texture and interest to the pants. The color of the classic blue is very similar to what is represented in the advertisement. The indigo dyeing techniques sometimes generate more than 30 shades of a blue color; this means that two pairs of jeans within in the same dying process can come out "similar" but not exactly the same. I am pleased with the pants and find the seller's description to be accurate. I like wearing these pants with tennis shoes, or flip flops. I do not like the way they look with boots.

How fun! These trousers...er....printed jeans....are ADORABLE! They are high waisted, wide leg (but not "flowy" - just a bit bigger than straight leg trousers), And for me at 5"4" skim the top of my kitten heels so they are visible and become a definite part of the ensemble. Fabric is a stretch jean, nice weight, with all sorts of animal prints to choose from: 1. Brown cheetah - nice warm brown background (almost golden bronze) with black / brown cheetah spots 2. a variety of leopard prints a) Large leopard print in dark brown on a soft brown background b) Mini leopard print in dark brown on the same soft background as above c) Mini leopard print in dark grey on a soft light grey backgound d) Large Leopard print in medium warm brown on a light sand tone background Construction is excellent: 1) Typical jeans construction with metal zipper and at the waist a metal stud closure. There are no stress point rivets so as to present as a more "dressy" finish. 2) Outside side seams are top stitched from top of the waistband to mid hip 3) Inseams are top stitched all the way down, (length for the jeans I received was 29.5 inches) 4) Back seam through the crotch and up to the front sipper is the typical flat feld seam one expects on jeans. 5) Hem is generous - if necessary, before washing one could let it out and gain that amount to the length (another 1.5 inches) 6) Front pockets are on a slight slant off the waistband - essentially top-down opening, nice and deep so as to be functional, with the top slide-in opening the best from a design aspect as the pockets do not gap open. Pocket hems are double sewn - wonderful! 7) Back pockets are unremarkable - they are a rectangular patch pocket and seem to disappear into the busy pattern of the jeans 8) Fabric stretches around the legs and hip - with no stretch the length of the jeans - very comfortable fit. It also has a nice heft to it - great for cooler weather, but would work even for the evening is spring and summer as a bit upscale I accidentally miss-order the size and received trouser that are too large; However, based on what I received I can share that the documented measurements in the size chart appear to be true to size> Order your regular size and you'll be great as to what the design is meant to be. My only criticism is, for me, the crotch definitely seems to be a bit long. I prefer higher waist trousers, but this is more than that. For me, I will have these altered down to my correct size plus have the crotch raised as its quite worth it - these are great trousers, and the pattern definitely puts them in the next level up - even from your best jeans. Surprisingly inexpensive for what you receive. Purchase these and check them out!

I wasn’t sure how I would feel about these wide-leg leopard pants at first, but they truly exceeded my expectations. They are even cuter in person! The fabric is very comfortable with great stretch, and they wash up nicely. I love how versatile they are. You can wear them casually with a T-shirt and sneakers or flip-flops, or easily dress them up with a blouse and heels or boots. For reference, I’m 5’7”, 165 lbs, and ordered a size large. The fit is perfect…not too tight and not too loose. I’m very happy with my purchase and have already recommended these pants to several friends.

These pants are adorable! The cheetah print is “spot” on 😉 and perfect for adding a bit of trendy animal print to your wardrobe. The material is lighter weight and softer than typical jeans material – almost like dress slacks, so the pants are somewhat more flowy and not as stiff as jeans would be. The slight bit of stretch to the material also makes them much more comfortable than jeans, nearly as comfortable as loungewear, except the waistband does occasionally constrict a bit when slouching. It’s not enough to be uncomfortable; it just reminds you that they’re high-waisted with a jeans-style waistband, not one made of elastic. I typically wear a size 10-12 (5’5” & 165 lbs) and ordered the size large because my measurements put me in between sizes with these pants. That may not have been the best choice because I feel like the pants are a bit big on me, so keep that in mind if you are between sizes like me. I’ve styled these with a khaki shirt, cream blazer, and tan loafers for casual office wear, and also worn the pants with a black satin blouse and a pair of strappy wedges for a date night out with dinner.

Overall, a nice pair of casual pants. I would say these pants are TTS. Size up if you want a baggier fit. I am 5'9, 170 lbs, and wear a size 12. They are very comfortable and are made of a stretchy jean material. The drawstring is mainly for looks as the waist is stretchy as well. It sits at my natural waist and hits the tops of my feet lengthwise. The color isn't totally black, is more of a charcoal gray and I like the contrast stitching. All the pockets are functional. I would say these pants are more of a 'sophisticated' cargo-look pant. You'll have to iron the back pocket flaps down though, they seem to naturally stick out, which can be annoying.

I am a plua size 5ft4in woman usually 1xl top with size 18/20 pants. I am heavier on the bottom. These pants fit my hips, waist, and butt nicely with the stretch band without giving a muffin top. They dont go too low to show underwear. They also fit my thick legs, which is the meatest part about me and honesty the reason why finding jeans are hard. With my height, these pants do drag a little with sneakers or flip flops, but in 4in heels they are pretty nice. The straight bell bottom look is super flattering for my big legs. I have already worn these about a dozen times and the fabric is still intaked and color not faded. I want more pairs in more colors. 2xl fits perfect 3xl would give that baggy boyfriend jean look for my frame.

Order a size down. These pants are a nice medium weight, and the blue wash is really appealing. Right out of their rolled up packaging, they look like your favorite pair of broken in jeans. They are wrinkle free out of the packaging and seem to be resistant to wrinkles of all sorts. They are also incredibly stretchy, and as they get warm from your body heat, they get looser.The straight leg description isn't kidding. The legs are the same width all the way down, which makes them look, in these larger sizes, like bell bottoms. Straight leg jeans I usually get are straight from the knee down, so not as much material flapping around the ankles, or in my case, sitting on the floor. I measured the inseam at 28.25" at 5'2" about 3.5" more than I need. I am hoping I can shrink them.The seams are straight, even, and well made; the material feels durable, and the color is lovely, but they look awful on me. Recommended for taller people or those who are good at hemming, and I strongly recommend you buy a size down from what you normally wear.

I own several pieces from luvamia at this point, and they’ve been consistently reliable for quality and fit. This is the first time I’ve had any sizing issues with the brand. For reference, I’m 5’3” and 154 lbs, and I ordered my usual size medium. Technically, they fit, but just barely. They’re a little tighter than I’m used to from this brand, especially through the hips and seat. I have a generous backside, and they definitely put the elastic waistband in the back to work. It dips slightly lower than I’d prefer because of the stretch, but thankfully the elastic stretches instead of gapping the way a regular waistband would. That flexibility saves them from being uncomfortable. (And for what it’s worth, my partner is very much a fan of the snug fit across the back.) I tried them on both before and after washing and drying on normal settings. They did not shrink and there was no noticeable fading. The “Darkness Blue” color is exactly what I hoped for, a true dark denim that is easy to dress up or down. The fabric is a nice medium weight with excellent stretch. The construction is solid with clean seams, even stitching, no loose threads. The front patch pockets are generously sized and actually usable, and the back pockets are functional as well. I really like the small side notch at the hem. It gives the cuffs a more finished look. The length is actually perfect for me at mid-calf. As a short person, sometimes capris can be closer to ankle length on me. At $30.99 at the time of my review, the price lands on the higher end of what I personally consider acceptable for capri denim. The quality supports it, but given the slightly snug fit compared to other luvamia pants I own, I do wish sizing were a bit more consistent. If you’re between sizes or carry more weight in the hips and seat like I do, I would seriously consider sizing up. If you prefer a more fitted look, your usual size will likely work just fine.

I own several pieces from luvamia at this point, and they’ve been consistently reliable for quality and fit. This is the first time I’ve had any sizing issues with the brand. For reference, I’m 5’3” and 154 lbs, and I ordered my usual size medium. Technically, they fit, but just barely. They’re a little tighter than I’m used to from this brand, especially through the hips and seat. I have a generous backside, and they definitely put the elastic waistband in the back to work. It dips slightly lower than I’d prefer because of the stretch, but thankfully the elastic stretches instead of gapping the way a regular waistband would. That flexibility saves them from being uncomfortable. (And for what it’s worth, my partner is very much a fan of the snug fit across the back.) My measurements are 5'3", 130 lbs, waist 28 -29", hips 38 - 40" and I ordered the medium in cream white. The capri jeans fit true to size, and the color is as shown in the advertisement. The materials composition is 75% Cotton, 23% Polyester, 2% Elastane which provides the wearer with breathable comfort and a garment that allows freedom of movement. I DO NOT like to iron so the Polyester content definitely caught my attention as Polyester in a cotton blend enhances durability, wrinkle resistance, and moisture-wicking properties, making the fabric easier to care for and longer-lasting. This combination also helps maintain the shape of the garment while providing the softness and breathability of cotton. I recently wore these jeans to a picnic on a warm day--84 degrees--and the fabric content was appreciated due to lack of shade. Sitting in a low, near the ground, lawn chair, I was comfortable as the pants were flexible and breathable. I was able to squat down without experiencing pinch points in the waist, hips or thighs. While getting ready for the event, I popped them in the dryer on low heat to soften the wrinkles. I stored my phone in one pocket and my car fob, Chapstick and tissue in the other. I did not need to take my purse as the pockets were big enough to store my essentials. I took a picture of the pockets--inside out so the dimension could be visible. If you like to travel light, as I do, then pockets matter. I ordered the cream white because the manufacturer, luvamia, has demonstrated its cream white matches over their various products. For example, I ordered a cream white jean vest and it matches these capri jeans perfectly. I continue to find that luvamia offers stylish and quality products. I am very satisfied with these jeans.

These Capri jeans are well made of 75% cotton, 23%polyester, and 2% elastane. The fabric has a good amount of stretch in it, not just in the elastic waistband. They are soft and comfortable. The thickness of the material leans closer to medium, than thin. The have an fully elastic waistband that is wide enough so that it does not want to roll down, but not extra wide. There are no buttons or zippers, they are pull-up jeans. I like the small slit on the outer bottom hems of the legs. There are 4 pockets total; 2 in front that are similar to larger sized patch pockets, and 2 regular sized pockets on the back. I really like the Timeless Blue color of these jeans! This pair of Capri jeans are so easy to toss on and out the door for running errands, carpooling the kids, etc. I ordered them in a size XL, which is my regular jeans size. They fit a bit snug on my hips, but with the amount of stretch in the fabric, they are quite comfy without pinching or binding anywhere. For reference: I'm 5' 4" tall, 33-ish waist, 44" hips, and 182 lbs. I'm very happy with this pair of Capri jeans and certainly will recommend them. The material is so easy and comfortable to wear. They're nice, quality made jeans. The current price (2-9-2026) of $30.99 is a good value, but also at this time, there is a coupon available to reduce the price and make them an even better value. They offer other shades of blue plus a cream white and a washed black for color choices.

It is difficult for my wife to find pants that fit her well. She complains that if the hip and rear fits, then the waist is too large, and vice versa. This pair of capri jeans actually fit her all over. She is 5'7", 133lbs and got the medium size. She washed the capris in cold water inside out and hung to dry, as directed. This cleaning method should keep the material from stretching out or shrinking. She likes the coloring of the denim and relaxed fit of the pant. The length is just right for a capri pant as well.

At 5'9" and 145lbs, I got the medium and these jeans are seriously high-waisted on me - higher than expected. The bigger issue though is the length, which is too short for my comfort . The waist fit is good and the wide leg design is exactly what I wanted, but when flare jeans don't hit the floor properly, it defeats the whole purpose of that silhouette . The sparkly rhinestones are really pretty and add that concert-ready look, and the stretch denim is comfortable around the waist and hips.

These jeans are everything I hoped for and more! The fit is absolutely perfect. The sizing chart recommended a Large for my measurements (30” waist, ~40” hips), and I found them true to size. The cut is high-waist with flared bootcut leg, as described, and comfortable enough to wear all day thanks to the bit of stretch in the fabric. From the tag, it’s a cotton/poly blend with just enough elastine (2%) to allow that extra bit of comfort so they move with you and keep on looking and feeling good. I’m also delighted because I am a shorter lady, yet when I put on any kind of shoes (I’m talking about sandals, running shoes, flat shoes; I don’t wear heels), the pants do not drag the ground! The lighter wash denim is casual and forgiving, easy to pair with different tops and seasonal looks or layers. And the sparkles along the legs? Pure magic! They catch the light in a little bit of colorful shimmer that makes even an ordinary day feel special. These jeans manage to be both comfortable and pretty, which is the best combination. I was a little concerned that as a middle-aged lady, I would feel silly with sparkly jeans. However, I am not having that problem. Not in the least. While some might feel these are better suited to younger women, I discovered as soon as I put them on that a little bit of sparkly magic can brighten any day, no matter your age, usual choice of style, or anyone else’s opinions. If you’re on the fence about whether sparkles belong on your jeans, I encourage you to take a chance. Nothing bad will happen if you sparkle, and you might find you enjoy it as much as I do. One important note: Be sure to wash them inside out on a delicate cycle to help protect the decorative appliqué and keep the shimmer alive.

The stretch is nice BUT they’re not the jeans in the pic. The color is off, the fading is off. AND they’re not tall girl friendly. I picked the pair of jeans that looked like they had the extra room at the bottom… again, not the jeans in the pic. The hem hits above my ankle. I’ll keep the jeans because of the price, stretch & high waist. They hit at my belly button so great mom tummy control. I’m normally a size 14 (5”9’ & 209) so I ordered a 14 of these. They’re a little tight but not enough to send back. My waist is bigger then my hips so not a normal female build.

I'm 5'3, weigh 127 lbs and ordered these in a size 2. They are a little loose in the crouch area as you can see from the photo how they are not smooth in that area. They are perfect in length because I like my jeans to go over my shoes whether stilletos, AF1s, chucks, etc. They are well made and very comfortable, not itchy.

CUPSHE Women’s Denim Mini Skirt Button Front A-Line Jean High Waist Scalloped Hem Casual Outfits THIS is an ADORABLE skirt! I am loosing weight, so I ordered a medium (148lbs, 5'4) and it DOES fit and looks good, but I did have to unbutton it to then button on me to get on, and then it's fine though you can see its puckering near the top, atound the 2nd/3rd button. I probably would do better in a large, but given it fits, and it's only a BIT small for me now, and I'm losing weight, I have no doubts this will be a perfect fit when I'm ready to wear it.I'm an hourglass figure and my waste is smaller, but the waist on this is tighter than the body/ hips, so if your waist is at all larger than your hips 100% size up! It's such a pretty skirt with the unique scalloped edge front and while I COULD wear it now, the med is tight, but it's Jan! So by the time it's warm enough where I live (march/april) it'll be PREFECT as I am activly losing weight!! Highly recommend!! See pics :)

Amazon Essentials Women's Women's Low Stretch Mid-Rise Boyfriend Crop Jean For the price, the quality of these jeans is impressive! Less than $30 for a classic jean that has no polyester is a great deal! These are a pretty standard jean material. The 1% elastane offers slight stretch, but not too much. They are pretty rigid. I have to take off a star for sizing. My best fit is usually a ladies size 6 in a couple different brands that I like, mostly American Eagle. A 6 is tight on my 40.5" hips, but I have a 28" waist, so I need the 6 in the waist, generally. However, these were so tight I could not even button them! Because they were so tight, the material also felt uncomfortable. I ordered the dark blue wash, so I'm wondering if maybe the wash makes a difference in sizing? Or if maybe these just don't run the same pair to pair. In any case, this pair was tiny! I let my daughter, who is usually a size 4 try these on, and the waist was tight on her, but she could button them. The hips had a little room for her. She is only 13, so she thought this classic cut was too old lady for her. Also, these jeans are pretty short. I am 5'2", and I thought the length was perfect, even unrolled. If you are taller, these will definitely hit about the ankle. These are probably best for petite ladies. I'm taking off one star for sizing. Everything else about the jeans was great! However, I can't wear these because the sizing was so off. Maybe it was just this pair, but if I was to order again, I'd definitely size up a size just to get them buttoned!

Amazon Essentials Women's Women's Low Stretch Mid-Rise Boyfriend Crop Jean I'm 5' tall and 115#, 60 years old. These jeans fit fairly well with only a small amount of gaping at the back waistband The fabric is a nice weight- not that really thin material some jeans are made with. I also like that the front pockets are large enough to put my hand inside and actually hold something. They aren't a cropped length on me becasue I'm petite, but they look good as a standard length. I send back most of the jeans I order, but I'm going to keep these. Overall- these are above average.

I am so happy that I am on the verge of crying!!! In my teens to early 30s, I wore nothing but denim. I wore them hard too, b/c of my figure (way smaller than now), I always had issues fitting my hips and waist. I had to give them up after giving birth to my son due to medical issues. That was 16 years ago. I have been watching tons of reels of ladies working out and their new wardrobes, and it inspired me to try a pair of jeans again. I am shocked I found the perfect pair on my first purchase! Holy Cow! I am in love. Of course, they aren't as comfortable as leggings, however, this pair has stretch in all the right places. I am still working on losing weight and went with flare b/c my thighs are WAY bigger than my calves. I am just stunned that I have no back gap. I always had an issue there b/c I am swayed back. Seriously, check out this brand on Amazon. Read the reviews and measurements. FYI, Elastic, high-waisted pants are best for those with a sway & not a gap. I hate the idea of high-waisted pants, but these are so comfy. I wear large in leggings, which is what was suggested for this purchase. My waist is larger than the sizing chart, 40, and my hips are 43. I fit perfectly in the large for 30.5 - 32 waist and 40.5 - 42 hips. Keep this in mind. Color is on point. Value for money is great considering I have issues fitting in most years. The Fabric is of great quality and itchy. The seam isn't tight in the crotch/butt region.

I have been living in these jeans. I love them. They remind me of the jeans I used to wear in high school in the mid/late 90s. I am extremely curvy and have a hard time finding pants to fit my smaller waist and larger hips/thighs/booty. Because of the elastic waist, there is zero gap in the back of the waist of these jeans! I am 5’6”, 165lbs, carry most of my weight in my hips/thighs/booty, and I ordered a Large. I don’t love the way they hug my upper thigh curves (or “saddle bags”), so I also ordered an XL to see if I can get a looser fit. I will also photograph and review those once they arrive. These were a great buy and I highly recommend them!
reviewer_name_raw: Comfy, Gen X High School Jeans

Aeropostale Women's Aero Classic Uniform Pant Since I work at a bank, my job requires business casual clothing (slacks or dress pants) but it’s been nearly impossible for me to find cute bootcut pants that aren’t insanely high waisted or weird yoga-pants or spandex material. Not to mention the inseam is usually really short for most women’s dress pants which looks weird on me. These pants are a nice mid-waist option that has a slight flare (and perfect inseam) that’s really flattering and versatile. Love that they also have 2 functioning front pockets. For reference, I am 5’6 and about 120-125 lbs and I ordered my typical size 4 which fit perfectly!! They’re really comfy and wash nicely too, just be aware that they wrinkle kinda easily but that’s expected. For the price, I really couldn’t be happier. :) Just wish there was more colors or patterns to choose from.

These turned out quite nice. The fabric is nice and soft, and the waist band is fairly flexible. We weren't quite sure on the sizing, for my wife's hip size we'd need L, but for her waist size we'd need size XXL. We gambled on L given that the waist band looked stretchy, and it ended up fitting, if a bit tight on the waist. The one negative is that the crotch area is a bit lower than expected. See pictures for size large on a 165cm/5ft5 H43/W35 model.

I'm pleasant surprised by these. I originally thought the placement of the rear pockets was very unflatteringly based on listing photos, but in person it's not really a problem. I am size 16 pear/hourglass shape with a large behind and thick legs. I have a difficult time finding jeans that fit appropriately without large excessive gaping in the back or enough fabric to cover the rear. These jeans in a XXL have enough to cover with no gaping the baggy style is slightly lost on me due to the thickness of my legs in the thighs but not lost completely, which is more common for me. There is still a flattering loose fit that is comfortable without looking saggy due to the good thickness of the material. I'm 5'7 and these jeans were a perfect length for me resting just on the top of my foot. I think this XXL would fit my shape better at a size 14, which would then need the drawstring to prevent gaping I bet, but will still work. Overall I am pleased with these. They wash well too and did not bleed a bunch or shrink. They are darker than I expected (black variety)

user_comment: I have these EVALESS high waisted stretchy jeans with paneling in a size 12, in the “dirty blue” shade. I think they’re great quality, comfortable, and flattering. The color in this wash seems much darker in person than it appears online but I do like it; I’m hardly seeing any of the faded white streaks in the middle of the thighs. The panel seams on the inner thighs are trendy. The rise is comfortable, the material is stretchy but thick and hugs you, and I find these flattering through the leg. Size 12 fits me true to size. For reference I am 5’5 and curvy. They came cuffed but I will consider uncuffing the bottom as the model shows depending on my shoe choice; as others have stated they are on the shorter side as they hit above my ankle bone after washing one time. I think these look slightly elevated and more expensive than they are; they would work with a variety of outfits and I will pair them with spring tops.

I am 5'4" so I usually have issues with pants being too long on me. The hem on these fit me perfectly, but if you are taller, they may be too short for you. I chose the Dirty Blue, and they're just a bit darker than the listing photos. The jeans are very well made, but the style just isn't right for me. The paneling on the inner legs stands out and looks a little odd to me. I think this style would probably be great for a taller, thinner person.

I really love these jeans and am now considering looking for more items from this particular manufacturer because the quality is excellent. This is a first for me for an item not made in the US. These pants are a good weighted material with just a bit of stretch. Stitching looks well done. Double buttons work, are not too tight to close and not too loosely sewn on. The pockets are very deep! My whole hand, all the way to my wrist, easily fits in them. Back pockets are also good sized. I am pretty short, so these are a bit long, but are fine with shoes or boots. Nice fit in the legs and I can comfortably bend in them. If I had anything negative to complain about, it might be no side pocket on the leg, but I like these so much that I won't complain. But, if they come out with a pair that has the pocket on the leg, I'll definitely buy them! I'm currently debating on whether to get another pair or two in black and blue before winter ends or wait till next winter to see if they go on sale. I definitely recommend these if your are of average height and weight or a little on the heavy side. They fit well. I'm currently between sizes so went a size down thinking they'd be a bit tight, but they are perfect. For reference, I'm only 5' and 170 lbs. and ordered a size 14.

The good things I can say about these jeans is that they run true to size and they are pretty comfortable for jeans that don't have a ton of stretch. I got the washed black color and I do like the shade of it. What I don't like quite as much as I hope was the styling and the fit of them. The way they are designed, there's like a weird seam in the front and the pockets are a little weird and I just don't love the design of them in and of itself, but I also don't feel like they're the most flattering jeans. They make my butt look much flatter than it actually is, and at 50 years old I need all the help I can get lol. That's sad I'm sure I'll get some use out of them, but I still wish they looked a little bit more flattering / stylish.

Aeropostale Women's Low Rise Baggy Jean These jeans are very flattering once you get the sizing and inseam correct. They are not true to size therefore I took one star off. I’m usually a 30” waist in Levi’s and had to return the size 10 for an 8 in these. I’m positive I could have fit in a size 6. Go down one, or two sizes for a snug fit. I would consider these a mid rise, not low rise. They are a true baggy jean not wide leg. The regular inseam is a little long. I can feel myself stepping on the hem sometimes with low shoes and I’m 5’7” in height. Just FYI, the second pair of jeans I received did not come with tags or a sticker suggesting they weren’t new. Look out for that too.

user_comment: I can't say enough good things about these jeans. They are so comfortable and fit so well. Very stylish and versatile. I highly recommend! I'm 5'5, 120lbs.
I can't say enough good things about these jeans. They are so comfortable and fit so well. Very stylish and versatile. I highly recommend! I'm 5'5, 120lbs.

I am very happy with these jeans. As a mom with a tummy, long gone are the days when I can wear the low waisted or hip level jeans. These jeans are nice and stretchy and are definitely high-waisted so no rolls popping over the top!- For reference, I am 5'5", muffin top tummy and zero waist (not zero as in zero inches, zero as in I am straight as a rectangle). I am usually a size 8/10 in pants, but I went with the 12 because I often find that I need a little extra room in the waist in denim. I think this worked great, and I would say that if you have a larger belly/waist go with the size up. Even with the really stretchy material (and there is a nice amount of 4-way stretch and give to this denim), the waist is a little smaller (the waist fit me more like a 10 where the rest of the pant was larger like I would expect a 12 to fit).- These are full length on me (even with the fold -- and that fold is tacked on the side so it isn't just an ironed in hem).- These are definitely "mom" jeans in terms of the height of the waist and there are no issues with my tummy rolling over. However, as I said the waistband is a little tighter than the rest of the pants for the size, so I did feel that is pushed all of my tummy into the front of the pants.I think these are definitely cute. I very much appreciate the stretchy denim and the high waist. I do think the waistband has a little less give and runs a little smaller than the sizing of the rest of the pants (especially if you have a muffin-top tummy/wider waist) -- so consider sizing up if that is your case, too.

As a 69 - going on 70 year old woman, who still lives in jeans and t-shirts, my wardrobe was in crisis mode. After a long and challenging illness and treatment, I lost so much weight that all my pld jeans became baggy masses that I couldn't keep up without the risk of public embarrassment. And some have turned into... let's just say 'ventilated' versions of themselves - rips under the butt that weren't there when I bought them! I can just hear people thinking 'Look at that grandma trying to look trendy!'.So, when these ETTELLO jeans arrived courtsey of Amazon Vine, I was overjoyed. The moment I opened the package, I knew I was in love. The jeans are well-made, with strong stitching, and the fabric is soft yet sturdy, with this magical 4-way stretch that's actually real denim. The design? Perfectly high-waisted, straight leg with trendy ankle fold hem that even this grandma can rock. The 5 pockets are a dream, and the light blue color is so fresh. They arrived quickly, well-packaged, absolutely no strange smell, and I couldn't wait to try them on.Now, here's where I almost cried. I used to be a solid size 6 at 115-120lbs, but I ordered a size 4 because, well, I dropped to 100-105lbs (yes, I'm working hard on gaining it back!), and they looked TINY. I thought, 'No way are these going to fit,' but lo and behold - they fit perfectly! Even better, they'll still fit when I finally gain my weight back, thanks to the stretchy fabric. And for reference, I'm 5'4" (I haven't shrunk too much, yet) with a 31-32" insem, and the length is right at my ankles - perfection. If I ever want them longer, I can just undo the ankle fold, and adjust to any length I want, but for now, I'm loving them as is.These jeans really hold everything in place. These are true to size. I sized up because I typically go with my size and many times Amazon brands tend to be on the smaller size. Definitely regret doing this as I think my regular size would have fit just fine. These look pretty baggy, but they'll be fine for wearing around the house. The length is great, I am 5'5" and they come down to my ankles, so I can use them with flats or heeled shoes. They aren't quite as dressy as I had hoped the denim would look in person, but I can still dress it up with a cute top or sweater. I know the baggy/relaxed and wide leg look is back in, and it is taking this Xennial a bit to adjust to this again. I have been in the "mom jean" skinny jeans mode for so many years, that I am trying to rediscover more wide-legged pants again, and these are a great way to explore this look again. I'm not sure if this is more related to getting a bigger size than I probably should have, but the backside is not very defining, so if you are looking for a bit of lift or accentuation back there, these probably wonn't provide that. Again, not an issue if I wear a longer top or sweater, but definitely something to note. Will update again later with how these hold up after a few wears and washes.

I have seen some gorgeous jeans in ads only to order them and they're not jeans at all: they're a jean-type pant, kind of a legging, dyed a phony blue, attempting a jean look and failing. Not these! Finally, a literal pair of jeans with real jean material that is stretchy, yet holds everything in in such a flattering way! I am 5'4" and weigh 177 lbs. I am mentally preparing myself to start eating healthier and add some fitness in my life, but I'm just not there yet. So I bought these pants in size 10, when I normally would wear a 12 to 14, hoping they would give me the motivation to start my slim down journey. I pulled the jeans out of the packaging and almost cried tears of joy when I felt the material and quality. They were perfect! I held them up to me and they looked pretty small for my size, but I decided to try them on to see how much I would need to lose. Welp, the stretch allowed me to actually fully squeeze in them and I can wear them NOW! In addition, if I lose 30-40 lbs like I hope to, these jeans will fit even better. As you can see in my photos, the jeans hold me in, instead of showing every real-life bulge I actually have. I wore them all day running errands and they were still comfortable, just a little tighter in the waist than I would have wanted but I'm lucky I can wear them at all. Do I recommend these? Absolutely. I actually recommend buying more than one pair.

Levi's Women's Premium 501 Original Shorts ​​​​​(Also Available in Plus) I’m 5’7, 135lbs, on a scale of Kim to Kendall butt, I have a Kourtney. And I got a size 28 (: *I did originally order a 27 and it was wearable but not at all breathable, 28 was perfect. Pros: The quality is great. They’re a true vintage high rise fit. They are loose enough to breathe and be comfortable which is perfect since there’s no stretchiness in them at all. They are great coverage, so even if you bend over your butt cheeks won’t show. Cons: So while they fit perfect around the waist and hips, the cut off at your legs is horrendous lol. In the last picture, the piece I’m holding was attached with strings that went over my thigh but it was so bad and even tighter than the actual shorts so I had to cut them out. The third and forth picture show what I mean, sitting down you will have biscuit can bulging thighs, period. Lol and they roll up when sitting down so it can get really uncomfortable quick…and as you can see the space on my waist and belly area even when I’m sitting so it’s not that the shorts are overall too small. When standing they aren’t tight around my thighs at all though. Also, in the third pic when I’m sitting, that poof that looks like my belly is actually space just more space in the shorts. Lol. The last two cons I have aren’t really cons that just are weird little things about the shorts that bug me but not enough to actually call them cons. So first one is the crinkle on the butt in the center area. I dont have a huge booty but I have a booty, if that makes sense, so it’s just a weird thing they do I guess. If you have a bigger butt you may not have that issue idk. The second thing is there’s 5 buttons. Which isn’t an issue expect the lowest one is LOW lol and it can be a booger to get them in the loops as some of the loops are not that big. So to sum it up, they’re great shorts for anything that doesn’t involve a lot of sitting.

Levi's Women's Premium 501 Original Shorts ​​​​​(Also Available in Plus) I feel like "cute/flattering but uncomfortable" is the tagline I could use for pretty much every pair of Levi's I've tried, minus the stretchier styles. To start, I got these in both a SIZE 32 and 33 in the color LUXOR HEAT. I'm anywhere between a size 8-12 with a 31" waist and 43" hips, give or take, but I always feel like I have to size up in Levi's. True to form, I would not have liked my true size 30 or 31 in these, so I'm glad I went up. I ended up keeping the size 32 (pictured), as the 33s were way too baggy. I could probably have done a 31 but I like having a little extra room for my thick thighs and big butt. Keep in mind these do have the button fly, like all the 501 styles do. I personally hate button flys, but I wanted to give them a shot anyway. These shorts are adorable on - I feel really cute when I wear them and my butt looks great. However, they are pretty uncomfortable. This jean material is what I'd consider a classic, sturdy fabric with almost zero stretch, though I will say the Levi's Premium fabric is softer than the Original. These do not conform to your curves much, though I'm sure wear will eventually soften the denim (or you can search for tricks to soften them, like I eventually did). If I'm walking around or standing, I don't really have complaints, but I would probably not wear these in a situation where I'll be sitting very much. On that note, if you have thicker thighs, these shorts feel like they're going to cut them off when you're sitting. I was a soccer player for a long time so have always had larger thighs, and this is one of the reasons I've never loved wearing short-shorts, because they cut into my thighs. The edge of the fabric just digs in while you're sitting, even with these cut off styles. I'd recommend these to someone who likes a stiffer denim and/or has skinny thighs, or someone who just doesn't care, but I find myself super self-conscious sitting in these, so I don't reach for them much. I kept them because there are situations I'd still wear them, plus I just needed shorts. I much prefer my Levi's Premium 501 Mid Thigh Shorts in the same size.

I wanted to posy honest review of a real plus size body in these stretchy leggings. I am 5'4, 313lbs. I carry my weight in my belly, buttocks and thighs. The fabric is very thin. I suggest tights underneath if you're living in a cold weather area. I'm in CA and stay cold so I'll probably wear some with tights and boots for the winter. They feel very soft, stretchy and don't feel tight on my belly. Plenty of room to breathe. I purchased a size 4X. Hope this is for those searching for comfy stretchy clothes.

These are possibly the most comfortable pants I own. They're very lightweight and stretchy. I'm normally a 2X (US22) but ordered a 3X because I'm used to clothes running a bit small. I definitely could have gotten away with a 2X, so they're true to size. The only downside for me is that they're short. At almost 6' tall, I'm used to pants running short, so I don't consider it anything wrong with the pants.

This is a very pretty lightweight denim dress that will be great to wear during those hot & humid days. I did assume that the denim material would be thicker than what it is, yet being made of lightweight material is perfect for the summer. It reflects a lightweight denim linen material. The dress provides perfect full back coverage— it is not short. Further, it provides great front coverage as well— it is not low cut. The waistband is stretchy and helps to cinch your waist. For reference, I am 5’5, size 14-16 with a lot of curves and size 40DD on top, and the size Large fits me perfectly.

This dress is cute. I love the material. It is so soft and silky and it feels very lightweight. I like how the dress is very versatile. You could wear it with a belt or a jacket or just plain. It is a comfortable lightweight summer dress or it could be a winter dress with boots, tights and a jacket. I am about 125 pounds and about 5‘3“ tall and I went with a size medium, which seems to fit well.

These jeans are super cute and do look good on the butt.. I only gave 4 stars cause of the price. I’m not quite sure I’d consider these to be a $60 pair of jeans. I am 180 lbs 5’4 and ordered a size 13, could have gotten a 11 if I wanted a tighter fit.

I'm 5'8", 125 lb, rectangle body shape and have had 3 babies so I'm constantly looking for any pants that fit my toothpick legs but don't cut into my squishy belly. I like these jeans from a distance but the stitching is so cheaply done I'm returning these. I also wanted a high rise that covers my bellybutton but that's almost impossible to find. The fabric is stretchy and not very soft to the touch but maybe washing and drying would help?

I just received these jeans, & I’m already wearing them as I write. I'm thrilled with them. -nice thick denim/ elastine blend material: the garment feels extra sturdy in the hand, & like it will be long lasting & hold its shape well; (unlike other jeans I own, that are so much thinner & have damage warping by the zipper area) -awesome faded brown color: looks a notch above wearing just basic jeans, in my viewpoint; a bit more ‘dressy’ -perfect comfortable form fitting: I was so happy to put these jeans on & find them just perfect form fitting, so soft, so comfortable in design & fit. They give a nice stretch when sitting, conforming to the body, softly, rather than the 'pinch' of more rigid jeans. (Note: I'm 5'4", & not way overweight but right now with a bit of an older woman mid-section gut-tire, approx 150ish lbs, especially after a lot of winter time indoors with less exercise + some cortisol stress middle weight gain this year. The L - large size fit me perfectly, in every dimension, including the back bottom edge just touching the ground with no shoes on. You could certainly be a few inches up or down from my height, & find this size to fit). -front design ridge (down the front of each pant leg): adds a nice touch of style/ quality, to take the jeans up a notch, toward being more of ‘dress’ jeans, rather than backyard gardening jeans -wider leg bottoms: give a nice design for slimming your appearance, since they widen as they go down, assisting in the appearance of the waist area appearing thinner, for us aging women, who can use that beneficial little bit of gain. -no label by manufacturer sewn on outside: I love this. It's a plus to me. I appreciate the logo on the cardboard tag: “Style is eternal, be your own label.” Wow! I’m impressed even with the mind behind these jeans. I’ve always disliked clothing labels. This is magnificent. I could hug these designer people. -garment care: per tag: machine wash gentle or dry clean acceptable. NO tumble dry; which is fine with me, because jeans with 2% “elastine” or spandex, blended into 98% cotton have warped in the past in the dryer, giving ripple wave damage wrinkles all around the zipper. Air dry is the best care to avoid this damage; so the label gives perfect advice. (I’ve found with such garments, you can get away with initial, brief use of low heat, for the 1st 10 minutes only, as long as they are still damp; & then switch to no heat, & throwing a towel in the dryer with them to absorb water & assist drying; hang dry for a final stage. But don't forget them in the dryer. And again, the label says 'no tumble dry' so for your warranty & safest care, listen to the manufacturer, not me).

Really cute in person. The Mariela is slightly thicker than a jeggging but less than regular denim. Stretch is all the way up so there’s even some give in the waistband. Nice and long so I can wear my boots or platforms as I hoped. Don’t stretch out too much with extended wear. Based on the measurements I should’ve ordered a medium but I went with small to compensate for the amount of stretch. If I went up it definitely wouldn’t started being baggy in the waist after a while. For reference, I’m 5’3”/32c/135-137lbs depending on the time of month/small pooch in the front since I never say no to late night street tacos/slight bit of birthing hips that never bounced back. For Gap/Old Navy and Hollister/AF denim and jeggings I get away with 6/28 for high rise and ultra high rise denim if that helps. Mid rise and low rise I’m a 4/27. Sweats and leggings of any brand I’m a womens small. I like all styles of bottoms but the FIT I prefer to wear is tighter in the hips and upper thighs then slightly relaxed the rest of the leg. Obviously, flares I give more grace to width from the knee down.

These jeans exceeded my expectations in all categories. I am 5'5" ~193lbs and have a ~39" waist. I typically wear my pants/jeans just above the belly button, so high waist location, and you can see how they perfectly drape down to the shoe line without falling to the floor. They are SO unbelievably comfortable and stretchy. I've never worn a pair of jeans that I could go around my house bending over a lot and doing various chores with without feeling a horrible tug against me from heavy denim fabric. You won't find that here at all, they just perfectly ebb and flow right alongside your body movements, thanks to the incredible degree of stretch they have. As well thanks to this, they don't sit so snugly at said waist that they're uncomfortable when sitting down or tug at you constantly there, either. I'm in the process of losing weight, so I hope they continue to fit for a long while yet, but I can without a doubt say these are even going on my personal shopping list to ensure I most definitely get more in the future. These are easily my favorite jeans I've ever owned. The color choice was also awesome! SO nice to have jeans that aren't just blue or black for once. I can't recommend them enough, honestly, worth every penny and then some.

I think the color looked lighter in the original listing photo than the actual pant. I wear a 4/6 pant and these were a bit big for me (size small). I was able to pull these on without unbuttoning. There's a good amount of stretch to them which makes them more comfortable. I love the style and how high the waist is on these. I'm 5'5" and the length was good for me. The quality is good too.

I ordered a Medium based on the size chart. I am 5'8", 140 lbs, I typically wear a small or medium, generally size 6 fits but depending of the brand I can sometimes fit a 4 or 8. My measurements placed me directly in between a small and a medium on this size chart (which happens to me ALL THE TIME), so I ordered medium with comfort in mind. I feel like the medium fit good and the small might have been too tight. They are stretchy (but not too stretchy) and might shrink some after being washed, so I think they'll be perfect after that. For pants like this I do prefer them a little bit more snug around the booty 🍑, but I still think they provided a decent fit. I'll back after the first wash.I like that the blue color isn't a super dark navy, it's more of a medium blue. They have REAL front and back pockets (🌟 win-win) which is always very important! They are very soft but still a thicker denim material. Another review said that they weren't high waisted, but I disagree!! These came up above my belly button. The 27" inseam is meant to be cropped, and for me they are at the perfect length. I can see how they would be full length on the shorter ladies. Depending on your style and the occasion, these could be worn out to dinner (but not a fancy dinner) or out on a semi-casual outing. They're perfect for looking nice and put together but still feeling comfortable!

Cute pants, but the color is actually burnt orange and not a true orange as you can see in the picture. I am 5’2” and the pants were to my feet, not cropped.

I’m an admitted Luvamia junkie, so when I saw these striped jeans from them I knew I had to have them. My experience with Luvamia is that their clothes often are a little tight to begin with but then their denim stretches while wearing it so you can normally order your usual size. However, on “specialty items” liked these striped jeans, I always size up because their things that aren’t plain denim tend to run a little smaller. In my photos I am wearing a large on my 5’5” 169# body. You can see that the waist is still a little tight in these photos but once I wore them for a little bit it loosened up and wasn’t showing my rolls as much. When I wash Luvamia, I just wash it in my regular laundry. It usually tightens back up a little bit they are never as tight as they were when they were brand new. These are so adorable I’m in love with them. Though they are striped, they aren’t clown-like and feel very beach and fun. Once again, Luvamia didn’t disappoint.

Looking for a chic capri pant? These black and white are made of a jean material. They don’t feel cheap at all. They are a nice wide leg that falls right at my ankle and look great with a sandal or mule. They’re not see through and they have some stretch to them. Despite being denim material, they are soft. The pockets are functional and fashionable. I ordered a size Small and had no problem sliding them on and off, there’s actually plenty of room - I’m 5’2” and 130lbs for reference.
